#### happiness, pleasure, soul
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZsCgNkcuyXo?t=171
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Happiness always gives pleasure, but pleasure does not always give happiness."
**Summary:** The lecture begins with defining happiness and pleasure, differentiating between existential bliss and situational bliss, and explaining the importance of healthy pleasures over unhealthy ones.

Krishna Consciousness, Happiness and Pleasure Meditation. The mystics derive unlimited transcendental pleasures from the Absolute Truth. And therefore the Supreme Absolute Truth, the personality of Godhead is also known as Rama. This is from Chaitanya Charitamrita, Introduction. We are Satchitananda. We always seek bliss or feel good. There are two kinds of bliss, happiness and pleasure. Let us understand happiness and pleasure. Happiness means feel good derived from the state of being or existence. Happiness is also called existential bliss or meditative bliss. Pleasure means feel good derived from an activity. Pleasure is also called a situational bliss or interactive bliss. Happiness always gives pleasure, but pleasure does not always give happiness. There are two kinds of pleasures. There are healthy, nutritious pleasures, and unhealthy, poisonous pleasures. Healthy pleasures give us happiness. Unhealthy pleasures give us suffering. An intelligent person indulges only in healthy pleasures that expand his happiness.

Welcome all of you. So we are in the series of happiness and pleasure. I had told our team here to rename the last four videos as happiness and pleasure series 1 to 5. #### material happiness, eternal happiness, sense gratification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2tyr7PYsMp8?t=61
**Verse References:** BG 2.47
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even the material happiness is not really happiness, it's a fake happiness, so-called happiness because happiness is existential and it has to be eternal if it has to be happiness."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts material happiness with eternal happiness, stating that true happiness is existential and eternal, unlike the fleeting pleasures of the material world.

And then pointing out to you the pleasures and pains of the senses, pleasures and pains of the mind, pleasures and pains of the intellect, pressures and pains of the social ego, all that I explained. And that, I can, all those things put together, that constitutes material happiness and suffering, and material pressure and pain. And then we switched over to something else, that there is something beyond this, there is happiness and pleasure beyond this. So even the material happiness is not really happiness, it's a fake happiness, so-called happiness because happiness is existential and it has to be eternal if it has to be happiness. Pleasure can be situational, it comes and it goes, but happiness is eternal.

---
#### material world, eternal happiness, false ego
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2tyr7PYsMp8?t=148
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "There is no real happiness in this material world, but it appears there is happiness and we go after it."
**Summary:** The speaker asserts that there is no real happiness in the material world, comparing it to a mirage, and emphasizes the soul's desire for eternal happiness (Satchitananda).

So there is no happiness, we concluded in this material world. And we read out Prabhupad's quote from the introduction to Gita where he gives example of the mirage in the desert. In the desert, there is really no water, but it appears there is water. So in the same way, there is no real happiness in this material world, but it appears there is happiness and we go after it. There is only hope for happiness, but there is no fulfillment of happiness. The hope for happiness is considered to be as happiness. Hope for water is considered as if, you know, I have water. But it really doesn't quench the thirst. Simply, you know, thinking there is water is not going to quench that thirst. Similarly, our soul is seeking eternal happiness. But just by thinking that there is happiness here, I am not going to be happy. I can continue that thinking, but I will always be dissatisfied. That is why all the Jivas in the material world are dissatisfied because they want Satchitananda. Sat means eternal. That means all, whether it is knowledge or Ananda, it has to be Sat. It has to be eternal. So happiness means Sat happiness. And Sat happiness is what we listed out, what 17 different points, where it is really our asset. We all possess as devotees that happiness. That is beyond the material happiness of the false ego. The social ego has a status in this material world. According to that, it has got material so-called happiness and suffering.

Keep all this nicely Krishna conscious happiness wealth in your bank account on a day-to-day basis. You do not draw anything and you do not keep your mind in a prasannata mood. And what is the result of that? As Krishna said, we do not have higher taste, you will go for lower taste. That is all Maya taste. If you do not want Krishna taste, if you do not feed yourself with, feed your soul with Krishna taste, the soul will go towards Maya taste. That is for sure because soul wants taste, soul wants feel good.

Success, failure, sukha, dukkha, completely forgotten your house, you are in another world. So this is called identified pleasure. So in the same way, this material body is outside me. Welcome, all of you. So we are continuing our happiness and pleasure series. And I think last time we read out a few of this. Okay. These are absolute truths about our existence, Krishna's existence, our relationship with Krishna, our eternity, Krishna's eternity. And you know, current in this body, again relationship with Krishna, Krishna is seated in our heart. And he's always our true friend. He's our maintainer, he's our protector. So all these things are matters of existential pleasure for a devotee. So existential pleasures are called happiness. So this is Krishna consciousness, happiness. These truths. If you have faith in them, they're Krishna conscious happiness. And then situational pleasure is called normally as pleasure. That means, you know, you start a pleasurable activity, and then activity ends, during that time you have pleasure. That is pleasurable. That is pleasure. So pleasure comes out of activities. Happiness comes out of existential truths. And the existential truths, as far as material world is concerned, as far as material consciousness of all the people in the world are concerned, even though they're expecting happiness, they're not expecting happiness because soul's nature is to expect eternal happiness. It is all false, fake happiness. Because what do the karmis, people who do not believe in Krishna or Krishna's instruction, they're called karmis. And these, those who do not, such people, the world is, this world is filled with such people. And they are always seeking happiness. What is the happiness they are seeking? Health, wealth, power, loving relationships, lot of sense objects. That's all. Possession. Possession of lot of sense objects. Ability to create different pleasures for their bodies, for their senses. Sensations for their mind, love pleasure, or intellect, intellectual pleasures, for their social ego, power pleasure. That's all. About pleasure. Name, position, all these things. Just see, these are all the pleasures. But to give pleasure they have the stock of something. If I have wealth, I can convert them to money power. Wealth is happiness when I use the money for buying a nice beautiful car. I feel I'm, you know, it's a pleasure of buying a car. You have loving relationships. Everything is equal. Everything is equal. You have a loving, you know, family and, you know, your wife cooks nice, lovingly cooks food for you. Go and take. That is pleasure. I have the pleasure of having a good cook in my wife. I have that pleasure, he will say. But I have the happiness of having a good wife. I have happiness of having wealth. I have happiness of having health. I have happiness of having power, money, money power also. Of course, wealth is money power. Influence, other power is influence, etc. So the whole world is after that. And that Prabhupada very clearly says, if you have become a devotee, first thing you should understand is, there is no true happiness here. What happiness is there, I am believing it is happiness, fake happiness. It doesn't satisfy my heart. My heart seeks eternal happiness. Nobody wants to die. Nobody wants good things to end. Everybody wants it to be always, you know, don't want to grow old. Health should be there always. But everything is fake. This conviction is required. Devotees have this conviction. That is why they come to Krishna consciousness. But having come to Krishna consciousness, why is it that we don't have the spiritual happiness? They materialist are seeking material happiness. We should seek called material spiritual happiness. And the seeking that spiritual happiness is spiritual happiness, and spiritual pleasure. My relationship with Krishna, my good fortune, my counting all the blessings of me as a spirit soul, that comes spiritual happiness. And when the soul is completely, again I am repeating each of these series, I am giving this, repeating it. That's a key thing. If I keep my soul happy, filled with happiness, then my, very easy for me to counter my old conditioning tendency for bodily, mental, intellectual, egoistic pleasures. The tendency that is there as a habit in my environment. In my mantra, in my chitta, the desire for sense gratification is there. Desire for love and giving love, taking love is there in the material world. Conditioned desires are there to be a jnani. By associating with a jnani, you become jnani. So I should become a jnani. Because you realize the value of that by association. You get the pleasure of jnana. Similarly, social. So these are all the urges that are there in our chitta. That means programmed. Just because you have now chasing spiritual happiness, doesn't mean this becomes zero now. Because these are conditioned tendencies. Because these are conditioned tendencies to seek. #### transcendental consciousness, sense control, higher taste
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eBjA1KP8Q8Q?t=600
**Verse References:** BG 2.62
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You can easily tolerate these things when you experience a higher taste."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the symptoms of a person in transcendental consciousness, emphasizing the importance of withdrawing the senses from sense objects and experiencing a higher taste to tolerate lower desires.

Transcendental consciousness means he is in bliss. He is filled with ananda, satchitananda. So what are the symptoms of that person? That is where Krishna starts speaking. The blessed Lord said, O Partha, when a man gives up all varieties of sense desire which arise from mental concoction, and when his mind finds satisfaction in self alone, then he is said to be in pure bliss. This is called transcendental consciousness. So in one line first Krishna says, One who is given up all sense desires according to his mental concoction. That means these bodily desires. Multiple. Body, mind, intelligence, social ego. All these things are one who gives up varieties of sense desires which arise from mental concoction. And one side is he gives up. And the other side is he finds satisfaction in the self alone. He finds satisfaction in his atma. In one line he says. But now he will further explain. What is this concoction of mental desire? What is this sense desire? Everything. All that he will explain. When somebody asks a question, first he will give one summary. Here Krishna gives a summary. That what is the symptom? He has given up all this. That's mental concoction. And he is fixed in his atma ananda. His mind is fixed in satisfaction of the self alone. Then what is the consequence of that? One who is not disturbed in spite of threefold miseries, who is not elated when there is happiness, who is free from attachment, fear and anger is called stage of steady mind. This is another symptom. When a person is in the ocean of happiness, threefold miseries, how does it matter? It will be there. But what does he say? He will not be disturbed. See, cause of disturbance, vanishing is different subject matter. And we being disturbed is another subject matter. So that we have to understand. You get the ability not to be disturbed by the threefold miseries. Threefold miseries may be there. Because the body is governed by laws of karma. Okay. Still, not only the reason why he will not be disturbed. He is not disturbed for another reason also. In the purport, in the very same purport, Prabhupada says, Such a fully Krishna conscious person is not at all disturbed by the onslaughts of threefold miseries. Why? For he accepts onslaughts. All miseries as the mercy of the Lord. Thinking himself only worthy of more trouble due to past misdeeds. And he sees that his miseries by the grace of the Lord are minimized to the lowest. These two thoughts. That Paramatma Lord is seated in my heart. And my miseries are my well deserved miseries. Material world, action means reaction. If I have done nonsense, I have to suffer. If I have given suffering to others, I will have to suffer. If I have disobeyed Krishna's instruction, I have to suffer. And then whatever I have is definitely, you know, the devotee has this consciousness that Lord has interfered. It is his blessing. He counts the blessings. He has gratitude to the Lord. So with these two things, he is neither elated with happiness. Why is he? Why he should not be elated? He should not be elated. Why? Because he knows that Krishna has given this. And Krishna himself says, don't get elated by all this. If I have given you more money, more opportunity, more strength, more influence, more power to preach. Don't get carried away by that. I have provided that to you. You get carried away by Krishna conscious happiness of being my eternal servant. You take pleasure in activities to please me. These things you take pleasure. Don't get excited about these things. Don't get excited by, you know, what all you have. So your, whatever you may have, he knows it's all by Krishna's mercy. So threefold miseries are not there. Neither elation nor happiness. See, who is not elated when there is happiness? And who is free from attachment, fear, anger, etc. And he is firmly fixed in perfect knowledge. What is perfect knowledge? Means knowledge of the self with the super self. The perfect knowledge. Such conscious Krishna consciousness. Krishna, such consciousness in Krishna situates one in perfect transcendental position. Now then next Krishna says. Another, another symptom of first is telling what are the symptoms of this person. One who is able to withdraw the senses to the sense objects as tortoise withdraws his limbs from the shell. Is to be understood as truly situated in knowledge. Each of those verses we should understand very, very important. They are all key understandings. Tell me why a yogi should withdraw. If he is all self-controlled, why he should withdraw the senses? Oh no, I have conquered all the pleasures, all the things in this material world. So why you should withdraw the senses? No. Please understand very important understanding in Krishna consciousness. The material world sense and sense gratification with pleasure. And it may be it is illusory pleasure. But as an experience. It is an experience. Experience is never illusion. The content of the experience may be illusion. But the experience is not illusion. Because a real do, a real seer is required to see a dream also. In the dream he is experiencing. Content of the dream may not be, may be false. But the experience is same. Similarly in the material world by Krishna's arrangement. He has left, he has given pleasure in senses and sense objects. You don't play with it. So you have to respect Krishna's thing. And that's what then next Krishna says that. Embodied soul may be restricted from sense enjoyment. But the taste for sense object remain. Because otherwise you will wonder. Why is this yogi so much scared or what? Immediately he will withdraw his senses. There it is not required. If he is fully self-realized, he is in transcendental consciousness. He should throw his senses anywhere and should be unaffected. No, he doesn't challenge Krishna's laws. He doesn't challenge Krishna's Maya. Because Krishna has created that. So therefore plus he knows that he has a body. He is conditioned. Ceasing such engagement by experiencing a higher taste. So he is, he accepts first of all. There are lower tastes. Not that lower tastes are illusion. Therefore I go to higher taste. As far as experience is concerned. It is all same. One may be counterfeit in terms of a fake. In terms of truth. In terms of sat it may be fake. But in terms of ananda it is duplicate. Exactly. So therefore we have to be. We have to accept this fact. And not play with this senses and the sense objects. And we withdraw the senses. When we don't need for service of the Lord. When it is required for the service of the Lord. Lusty idea means lusty intelligence. Everything becomes, everything began with contamination of our soul's intelligence. Soul intelligence gets an idea that I can be happy without Krishna. I can have pleasures without Krishna. So now, why struggle all this? Go to the root and reverse that. You will see wonderful things happening. Very good question you have asked. How to reverse this at the root? Go to the root and reverse it. Don't know all these things. At the soul level what is the mistake I am doing? That I should struggle to change. And in that, it is in that spirit that we are trying to infuse Krishna conscious happiness. Unless we have, unless we remember and get back our little taste, our original taste. We will not be able to give up. This is a problem. It becomes your, your nature. The feelings, the bhavana keeps coming out. That feeling of happiness, existential happiness keeps coming out. You don't have to do it. But when you are in, when you are in trouble, when you are, when the material suffering becomes little more, you contemplate more and strengthen it. Strengthen it, increase your feeling.

No. In the background, He is there. He is remembering, anusmarach. He is remembering. And with the Lord, He is fighting. So if He has to fight, He has to think of the goal. So some people think that, you know, that Krishna and Bhagavad Gita says, you should not be attached to the result. You should not be attached to the consequence of the result, consequence of your work. That means I will achieve it 100%. You should not tell it. You cannot tell. You cannot tell, because it will happen. It will happen. It will happen, because you cannot tell. If Lord wills, yes, I will achieve it. You will have to, without a desire, I have to achieve it, but it may not achieve. May, may not achieve. That option should always be there. Why? You are respecting Krishna's will. At the same time, once you have taken up a desire to please the Lord, go behind it, unless you want that Krishna gives some indication, you drop also sometimes. Some endeavor you drop. No point in doing this. So this is a way to handle success and failure in preaching Krishna consciousness in all our different missionary work. That should not bring us depression. If you do not have base level Krishna conscious happiness, it becomes depressed, because then what happens? You want only external result. Then it will keep you enthused in Krishna consciousness. If you are getting failure, failure, failure, you will become depressed, because there is nothing in failure. Your Krishna consciousness, you are attending, you are chanting, you are attending a program, chanting 16 rounds, going out or preaching, whatever duties you are discharging your duty over, you have to be happy. There is lack of understanding of, you know, because it's our tendency. Pleasures is equal to happiness. Something has to happen, then only I can be happy. But what does your Krishna consciousness happiness mean? Unconditional, nothing has to happen. Already you have achieved that. You have understood that. You are an internal servant of Krishna. You are engaged in devotional service of the Lord. Already it is, there is nothing to it. The big thing has been achieved. Now, I want to achieve it. I want to achieve service of the Lord. Sometimes success, sometimes failure. It's all left to the Lord's will. Phalena Parichyate is applicable, you see, to, to, in order to pinpoint. It has a limited, it has a limited application. You have to be very careful, Phalena Parichyate. Phalena Parichyate means have, if I'm not getting phala, I have to do. Have I put all my effort? Have I put all my intelligence? Have I depended upon the Lord? Am I doing without false ego? I'm thinking Krishna, Prabhupada, mercy, I'm doing. All this have I done? That way, you have to introspect. Phalena Parichyate, when you fail, when you succeed, you cannot say, if somebody has good guna karma, he will succeed. Then Phalena Parichyate. Suppose that his feeding is false ego. So it need not. So we have to be very careful about applying that Phalena Parichyate.

### Krishna will run your day if you do this! | @madhupanditdasaofficial

#### chanting, mindfulness, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YPsji1KqC1I?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna will run your day."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains the importance of chanting the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra with a fresh mind in the early morning to allow Krishna to guide one's day and life.

Hare Krishna Prabhu, when should one chant Hare Krishna Maha Mantra? The best time to chant Hare Krishna Maha Mantra is after a long sleep when you wake up because your mind is fresh. Reason being, a fresh mind is required. What does a fresh mind mean? Once you start your day, so many thoughts and this and that, it keeps like a guna, kala, karma will be churning inside you and something and all will be coming in your mind. In the midst of that, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare. So one side you are mechanically chanting, the other side all this thinking is going on. So that is not a quality chanting. You are not touching unless the shabda that you chant goes inside through your ears into the heart. Effectiveness is very less. As I said, it is a science. If you take a piece of iron and keep it near the fire, you will get a little heat. Put it inside the fire, red hot, little far away, little heat. So you want a little heat from Hare Krishna or you want a full fire? Full fire means attentively, early morning, before your day's prarada karma starts, finish your association. Finish your association with Krishna. Krishna will run your day. Your life will become smooth. He will run your day. Externally there may be so many difficulties. External difficulty is not the measure of difficulty. What you are feeling about that difficulty is difficulty.

#### Satchitananda, soul, material world
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UGmkk8Lsf7g?t=374
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Soul's nature is to seek Sat. Chit, pure knowledge, pure understanding, not ignorance. And Ananda means bliss, love, loving exchanges, exchanges of loving, exchanges of pleasure."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the concept of Satchitananda, explaining that the soul naturally seeks eternity, knowledge, and bliss, but is always incomplete and hungry in the material world due to the influence of Maya energy.

is a Surya of Satchitananda. Krishna is Surya of Satchitananda. Krishna Naama is also Surya of Satchitananda. All that our soul seeks in this world or in the spiritual world is to be immersed in this Sat, Chit and Ananda Shakti. That is our natural atmosphere. Whereas now the soul is in the atmosphere of Asat Shakti, Achit Shakti and Nirananda Shakti. This is Maya energy. There is internal energy, spiritual energy of the Lord. Satchitananda energy. Sat means eternity. We all want eternal life. We all want eternal relationship. We all want eternal love. There is no doubt about it. But in the bodily life we never get any of these things. So soul's nature is to seek Sat. Chit, pure knowledge, pure understanding, not ignorance. And Ananda means bliss, love, loving exchanges, exchanges of loving, exchanges of pleasure. These are all very natural for the soul. But somehow or the other even though very natural for us in this material world we are always defeated in our efforts for Satchitananda. So soul is always incomplete in this material world. Therefore it is eternally it is hungry.

When you put a piece of iron in the fire, the heat moves from the fire to the piece of iron. And gradually the piece of iron gets heated little by little. The longer it stays it gets heated and becomes red hot. The iron gets the quality of fire. At least 5 to 6 times Prabhupada has explained this phenomenon of how chanting works. So it is very important for us to remember this analogy of Prabhupada. Because from this analogy many things we understand about the phenomena of how actually Hare Krishna chanting influences us, it affects us. Just like our soul is like the piece of iron. We have to put in the fire of Satchitananda.

You keep some water for boiling on the stove It gives 95 degrees heat. Take it out of the stove. Keep it. When it gets cold, keep it. When it gets hot, keep it. When it gets cold, take it out. Does it ever boil in life? Okay. So This whole thing I am You have to remember this analogy. Because there is a big point I am making. Uninterrupted hearing is extremely important. Big change in the quality of japa. You have to stay in the fire for 16 rounds. It is a practice. It is a question of training the mind. It is a question of training the mind. And today, tomorrow I am going to do 64 rounds. I am going to explain how the energy for this uninterrupted chanting can be achieved by following from the Nama only. Only one instruction. That instruction of Prabhupada we all know. We have called it what is it? Golden command. Chant Hare Krishna and exactly hear the same sound. Such a one statement is enough to change the world. If somebody follows 16 rounds he will go back to Godhead. That powerful it is this instruction of Prabhupada. And one may say the mind goes away what to do? To stay within the fire. Let us go back to the analogy. You stay in the fire. Again you come out. Or you go. There is a fire. Go near the fire. You will get little heat. Go into the fire. And come out. Still it will never become red hot. So our complete Red hot means complete self realization if you want. We have to everyday stay in the fire. We have to practice staying in the fire. For two hours.

## HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### Married Life in Krishna Consciousness Movement | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 3.22.11 | 13.08.2024
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### marriage, household duties, spiritual advancement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nupyoz3cd-c?t=43
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The household duty of a man is not to satisfy his sense gratification but to remain with a wife and children and at the same time attain Advancement in Spiritual Life."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the role of a wife in household duties and how a householder should aim for spiritual advancement alongside family life.

Translation: Therefore, please accept her, O chief of the brahmanas, for I offer her with faith, and she is in every respect fit to be your wife and take charge of your household duties. Purport by Shrila Prabhupada: The words 'in household duties'—another word is also used here: 'सर्वात्म नानु रूपाम'—the purport is that a wife should not only be equal to her husband in age, character, and qualities but must be helpful to him in his household duties. The household duty of a man is not to satisfy his sense gratification but to remain with a wife and children and at the same time attain Advancement in Spiritual Life. One who does not do so is not a householder but a ग्रह में दी. Two words are used in Sanskrit literature: one is ग्रहस्था and the other is ग्रह में दी. The difference between ग्रह में दी and ग्रहस्था is that...

#### holy name, spiritual potency, ashrama principles
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nupyoz3cd-c?t=538
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But the holy name is so powerful that if one takes this medicine of the holy name on a daily basis then all kinds of adjustments will happen with the bodily, mental situation and somehow engage in service of the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the power of the holy name and how it can help individuals overcome material obstacles and engage in devotional service, even if they don't perfectly adhere to ashrama principles.

युगा धर्मा in युगा धर्मा simply by the यद्ञ of chanting this Hare Krishna Mahamantra. But the holy name is so powerful that if one takes this medicine of the holy name on a daily basis then all kinds of adjustments will happen with the bodily, mental situation and somehow engage in service of the Lord. You will see that we will be able to come out of this. We will be able to awaken our transcendental knowledge in spite of being neither here nor there, neither a proper brahmachari, neither a proper grahastha, neither a proper sanyasi. So Prabhupada always said forget all this, you are servant of Krishna, just serve Krishna. Those are all incidental. One should not pin so much faith on all those things. We have to follow those ashrama principles.

#### ashrama, chanting, protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/nupyoz3cd-c?t=627
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even though we know that there is nothing, there is no perfect ashrama that can be followed in the present environment, but those who chant the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, those who serve the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, because the spiritual potency comes from the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, one will be able to, in whatever ashram he is there, he will be able to easily follow those principles."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the difficulty of following ashrama principles perfectly in the current environment but highlights the power of chanting the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra for protection and spiritual progress.

At the same time for the outside world we have to set an example, so we have to be very very careful. Even though we know that there is nothing, there is no perfect ashrama that can be followed in the present environment. But those who chant the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, those who serve the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, because the spiritual potency comes from the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, one will be able to, in whatever ashram he is there, he will be able to easily follow those principles. Even if the environment is not there, in a sense he will be able to follow all those things. There is no difficulty for a devotee who is chanting sixteen rounds to follow the principles of any of these ashramas. Not at all difficult. Because the holy name is so powerful. Chanting is so powerful. It is not that you chant Hare Krishna sixteen rounds and your material life is in a mess. It will not be like that. The chanting of Hare Krishna is so powerful that it will in the midst of all this, just like look at the devotees in Hare Krishna hill or any of our temples. The outside world is such a big mess. And here devotees have no such thing. They are simply chanting, dancing, doing their service. Anxieties of struggle for existence is being spared for everybody in this Hare Krishna hill. How is it possible in Kali Yuga? Where will you get this luxury in Kali Yuga? Where you don't have to think about your eating, sleeping, waiting, defending. You don't have to think. You only think of serving Prabhupada, serving Prabhupada. Your whole life gets going. Nowhere. Because material energy does not allow that. Maya does not allow that. Because maya cannot enter when there is Sankirtana. Where there is chanting, maya cannot enter. So we keep ourselves under protection of the divine energy of the deities, of the holy name of Guru's mercy. By all these things we are actually continuously on a day to day basis we are protected and that is why we are in this particular situation. That is why we are in this particular situation.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Whether you are thinking you are the body or you are a snake or a scorpion whatever this body is size or size any kind of any kind of body and body is the body so it is a body that receives determination mode of determination if you are in a particular mode you will get a particular type of conviction."

So initially all the things that we exhibit, that is why it is said without getting that punya, one cannot come into Krishna consciousness. That punya Prabhupad gives, by serving Prabhupad's movement, we get that punya. Punya is connected to the body. So there is a bodily qualification coming up due to service to one's Guru. Once that comes up, then you enjoy higher values from this same body. To have a body with sattva guna is a gift. When you serve Srila Prabhupad slowly, you will come into that guna. When you come into that guna, कुना जि disadvantages have come into that guna when you hear Bhagavatam and everything then impact will be much more. So our journey is through the yantra only. It starts through the yantra.

## HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### Self-certification is Not a Spiritual Qualification | Sri Madhu Pandit Dasa | SB 3.22.5 | 24.07.2024
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### association, saintly person, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Somehow or other, if one meets a saintly person completely engaged in transcendental duties and achieves his favor, then one's life mission becomes complete."
**Summary:** This segment focuses on the importance of associating with saintly persons and how even a moment of association can lead to liberation and the fulfillment of one's life mission, as exemplified by Srila Prabhupada's experience with his guru.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 3, Chapter 22, Text No. 5. Tavasandarshanadeva Chinname Sarvasamchayaha Yatsvayam Bhagavanpretya Dharmamaharirakshisho. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace Shri La Prabhupada. Now I have resolved all my doubts simply by meeting you, for your lordship has very kindly and clearly explained the duty of a king who desires to protect his subjects. Purport by Shri La Prabhupada. Manu described herewith the result of seeing a great saintly person. Lord Chaitanya says that one should always try to associate with saintly persons, because if one establishes a proper association with a saintly person, even for a moment, one attains all perfection. Somehow or other, if one meets a saintly person and achieves his favor, then the entire mission of one's human life is fulfilled. In our personal experience, we have actual proof of this statement of Manu. Once, we had the opportunity to meet Vishnupat Shri Srimad Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Goswami Maharaj and on first sight, he requested this humble self to preach his message in the western countries. There was no preparation for this but somehow or other, he desired it and by his grace, we are now engaged in executing his order which has given us a transcendental occupation and has saved and liberated us from the occupation of material activities. Thus, it is actually a fact that if one meets a saintly person completely engaged in transcendental duties and achieves his favor, then one's life mission becomes complete. What is not possible to achieve in thousands of lives can be achieved in one moment if there is an opportunity to meet a saintly person. It is therefore enjoined in Vedic literature that one should always try to associate with saintly persons and try to dissociate oneself from the common man because by one word of a saintly person, one can be liberated from material entanglement. A saintly person has the power because of his spiritual advancement to give immediate liberation to the conditioned soul. Here Manu admits that all his doubts are now over because Karadhamma has very kindly described the different duties of individual souls, thus ends the Bhaktivedanta purport.

---
#### duty, respect, brahmana, kshatriya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=233
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The brahmana is always ready to give advice as per his duty to the kshatriya, to keep him aware of his responsibilities, as a servant of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, to protect all the other three classes."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the roles and relationship between a brahmana and a kshatriya, emphasizing the brahmana's duty to advise the kshatriya and the kshatriya's duty to respect the brahmana as a source of the Lord's mercy.

There is no moment of rest. Manu is glorifying the words of Karadhamma muni. He is glorifying Devi. He is glorifying Karadhammaman. The king, he is glorifying the power of association through the words of a brahmana, like Karadhamani. And this is the natural relationship between a kshatriya king and the brahmana. The brahmana is always ready to give advice as per his duty to the kshatriya, to keep him aware of his responsibilities, as a servant of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, to protect all the other three classes. And it is the kshatriya's duty to give very special respect to brahmana, as a person from whom the Lord's mercy is received. Those brahmanas who are in the kshatriya, who are situated in transcendental platform, they represent the vision, the will of the Lord, with respect to mankind. And those things are, according to those advices, the kings rule the country or rule the planet.

---
#### association, mercy, spiritual advancement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=341
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Spiritual growth happens by constant shower of mercy of the spiritual master, mercy of the deities, mercy of prasadam, mercy of great devotees like Tulsimaharani, mercy of holy rivers, mercy of holy dham."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts the material paradigm of achieving through hard work with the spiritual process of advancement through receiving mercy from various transcendental sources, such as the spiritual master, deities, prasadam, and holy places.

Here, Srila Prabhupada in the Bhaktivedanta purports, he explains the principle of association with great saintly persons. Even a moment of association, even a moment of association, sadhu-sangha, one can attain all perfection by achieving his favor. This is a very important principle in the process of spiritual advancement for every devotee. Normally, everything in this world is achieved. That's the paradigm of everyone, that I work hard and I have to achieve. I work hard and I have to be successful. So when we come to spiritual life also, naturally the tendency is to think that I work very hard for my sadhana, etc. and then result is guaranteed. In other words, it's like a making spiritual advance, turning spiritual advancement to simply following some process. In the material world, it's like that. You go through a process, the result has to be there in the end. So we have to be very careful in our mind to change this paradigm. This paradigm has been ingrained into us through our education system. We are all victim of this. By default, our thinking is like this. It is only when you understand the principle of spiritual flow of mercy, how actually spiritual growth happens, not like how you learn in school. Spiritual growth happens by constant shower of mercy of the spiritual master, mercy of the deities, mercy of prasadam, mercy of great devotees like Tulsimaharani, mercy of holy rivers, mercy of holy dham. What is this mercy, mercy, mercy? How does it? How does it impact me? How does it impact a sadhaka?

---
#### descending process, realization, mercy, begging
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=528
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Spiritual advancement means receiving key understandings through the descending process."
**Summary:** This segment explains that spiritual advancement is a descending process of receiving mercy and realization, emphasizing the importance of a begging and anticipating mode for a devotee to receive the Lord's mercy in various forms.

Spiritual advancement means receiving key understandings through the descending process. At least we should, even if our mind is stuck in a process-oriented thinking, turn it into it is a descending process. That today if I have some advancement, it's not because I read Prabhupada's books alone, not because I thought a lot and I understood. It is because I read Prabhupada's books and Prabhupada gave his mercy. It is because I thought about all this and Krishna in the heart gave me the realization. Realization and understanding. So everything that we have today is a combination of whatever we desire, whatever is our attitude towards the desire and what we have received from a transcendental source. All that we receive from a transcendental source, it is not temporary. It is permanent. It stays with us. In the material world, so many energies come and go. You say spiritual, if you do your PhD this life, next life that knowledge is gone. But if there is a mercy of from the transcendental source of enlightenment, that will remain with the soul even though it may get covered, but doesn't matter. It remains. It's an eternal asset for the soul. So our business in spiritual life will be successful if we constantly collect mercy. Of course you cannot go on like plucking a flower, you cannot collect mercy. Mercy has to be begged. So the begging mode, anticipating mode is extremely important for a devotee. The devotee is constantly in the mode of begging and anticipating the mercy of the Lord in various forms.

---
#### sadhana, mercy, transcendental process
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=675
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Every day the mercy coming out of the altar to each devotee is different. Externally it is same, internally it is different."
**Summary:** This segment discusses how even seemingly repetitive sadhana practices are unique experiences of receiving mercy, emphasizing the importance of being sensitive to the transcendental aspects of these processes rather than viewing them as mere routines.

Just like we can say, okay, every day we are coming for sadhana, it is a process. 4.15 you have to be there, then samsara dava has to be sung, then Tulsi has to be, Tulsi Maharani's song has to be sung, she has to be glorified, then you go, Prabhupada Puja, etc., etc., all these processes you have to follow. So in the neophyte, a devotee thinks, this is, this is, okay, I have to go for this, I have to go for this, this is a process. Even though externally every day it appears to be the same thing, 4.15 Mangalarati, 4.15 same song, every day same song, don't you get bored? 30, 40 years same song, samsara dava, samsara dava, samsara dava, you don't get bored. The fact is that we don't get bored. Why? Every day the mercy coming out of the altar to each devotee is different. Externally it is same, internally it is different. What plays in the devotee's heart, his understanding, his realization, his situation for that day, his faith, his prayers, all are different. So you don't get bored. That is a variety. So every day is different. Every day Mangalarati is different. Every day Guru Puja is different. Every day Tulasi Aarti is different. It is different. If you go into an ascending process, process paradigm, then you will feel, oh what is this, what is this, the same thing, same thing, because you are only seeing that. You are not seeing the difference, you are not sensitive to the what is not visibly seeable things that happen from the Lord's mercy. The glance of the Lord upon the devotees, the glance of the spiritual master upon the devotees, who is worshipping him, Divya Gyan, Hire Prakashita. So it is a transcendental process. It is a transcendental thing that is going on.

#### Jnana-Prayasa, service, qualification, mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=891
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Essence of all realization is devotional service to the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the dangers of imbalanced effort on the jnanic path, cautioning against excessive speculation and emphasizing that true qualification and understanding come through service and the mercy of the spiritual master.

So we should hold on to the lotus feet of Srila Prabhupada, beg for the mercy of the Lord. There is another kind of contamination when there is an imbalanced effort on a devotee's path, Jnanic path. You have to think, think, think. You have to cross sometimes this thinking stage. Sometimes you have to think. We all thought about it and came to Krishna consciousness. Go on thinking, thinking, thinking, thinking everything. When we have to put effort to think, then that is speculation. Thinking is not wrong, but thinking will automatically come. Thoughts will automatically come. When you struggle to think, then it is Jnana-Prayasa. Unnecessary endeavor. You have a philosophical question, you think too much, you cannot answer now. Jnana-Prayasa. Wait, get the Adhikara. Get the qualification. Adhikara for the soul means enough more services to be done. You have to raise your spiritual platform. Then you will get the answer. Then you will have qualification to understand the answer. It is not that for every question that you ask, you have to get an answer now. What is the use of you getting the answer if you cannot understand the answer? So the question has to be raised, no doubt. It is our nature to raise question. But then we have to understand that ultimately the Adhikara, the qualification comes when the spiritual master is pleased, when Guru is pleased, Srila Prabhupada is pleased, Srila Prabhupada is pleased with the devotee who is sincerely trying to advance and who is trying to serve him because ultimately essence of all Jnana is what? Service to the Lord. Essence of all realization is what? Devotional service to the Lord.

---
#### service, Srila Prabhupada, mercy, books
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=1038
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Srila Prabhupada himself has considered this to be most important contribution, which is coming from the spiritual world under the order of the Guru Parampara and Krishna, that he has contributed this to this world. It is his books."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the immense blessings of serving a pure devotee like Srila Prabhupada and highlights the importance of his books as his most significant contribution to the world, carrying the mercy of the spiritual world.

So when we get an opportunity to directly serve a pure devotee like Srila Prabhupada, we are all greatly blessed where we are able to accumulate what nobody can accumulate in millions and millions of births. Today we are able to understand Krishna consciousness, we are able to chant Hare Krishna, we are able to understand Bhagavatam, read Bhagavatam, talk about Bhagavatam verses. All these things are only possible because of the mercy of Srila Prabhupada. Now Srila Prabhupada is not an ordinary sadhu. Ordinary sadhu, person to person, you sit with him, he talks something, he can bring about transformation in you. Srila Prabhupada is a sadhu, here see very clearly explained the duty of a king who desires to protect his disciples. He is very grateful for Kardama Muni explaining to him everything, for the words that came out of Kardama Muni. But today Srila Prabhupada's words, it is all Lord's Leela. These words of Srila Prabhupada are printed in this form. His words are captured in sound. In recorded sound. If one has faith in Srila Prabhupada, then one can continuously receive his mercy. He can receive his mercy. Transcendently receive his mercy. Anybody can receive his mercy. That is why Prabhupada always stressed, distribute my books, distribute my books, distribute my books. This is the most important thing that Prabhupada considers as his contribution to the world, are not the temples, but it is the books. Srila Prabhupada himself has considered this to be most important contribution, which is coming from the spiritual world under the order of the Guru Parampara and Krishna, that he has contributed this to this world. It is his books.

Now the problem is not that way. Prabhupada so mercifully has given, Krishna has made this arrangement. Mercy is available for those who want to take it. How much you can collect, how fast you can collect, we advance in spiritual life. We progress in spiritual life. So this is not an ordinary, ordinary period of time in the history. It's a very special time in the history. As far as spiritual growth is concerned, all because of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's movement, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's Hare Krishna movement, and His mercy distributed by Srila Prabhupada all over the world, all these things are very easily available to us. #### Srila Prabhupada, association, qualities, meditation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=1363
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

You don't have to see Prabhupada. If you read his life history, he'll touch you. You can feel him. Therefore devotees should all read Srila Prabhupada's Leelas. They should hear, and they should read Srila Prabhupada's pastimes of how from 1965, he built up this movement, till he left this planet, all the different activities that he did. When you read it, then you'll, you'll be able to, you have to perceive a person, means you have to perceive that person's desire, you have to perceive that person's aspirations, you should perceive that person's purity, you have to perceive that person's Iccha Shakti. Perceiving, those things are perceiving the person. How do, a devotee asked me, how to be conscious of Prabhupada? It's not simply his form. You get stuck there. The person can be perceived by his interest. The person can be perceived by his determination. By his personal qualities, you can perceive a person. You can pinpoint, very uniquely, who was Prabhupada, by his determination to go to America and preach, by his desire to please his spiritual master, by his aspiration to spread Lord Chaitanya's movement to everywhere, irrespective of any country. His confidence and trust that the Holy Name is powerful enough to change everyone. These are all the things that one can perceive in Srila Prabhupada's heart. Once you perceive that, you go on perceiving that, you'll, if you perceive these things in Srila Prabhupada, then those qualities will come into our heart also. Our determination to progress, our faith in the Holy Name, our aspiration to preach Krishna Consciousness, unrestrictedly in this world, to bring down the mission more and more into this material world, all these aspirations also will come to us by meditating on Prabhupada and his qualities. Any questions?

#### sadhana, process, mercy, transcendental process
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=1529
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Think of the process as a transcendental process of effort and mercy."
**Summary:** This segment addresses a question about attending sadhana as a process, clarifying that it's acceptable if understood as a two-way transcendental process involving both our effort and the mercy of Prabhupada and Krishna, contrasting it with the material concept of process where only visible effort guarantees results.

Generally, sadhana we attend like a process. Will it be okay, Prabhu, or anything consciously we have to make some effort? It's a process. As you understand what process means to you. If it is a two-way process, if you think it is a two-way process, that means you are doing something and Prabhupada is also doing something to you. You are doing something and Prabhupada and Krishna is doing something to you. If this perception is there, then thinking of this as a process is fine. But normally in this material world, process generally means it's you put your effort only and the result has to be there. Or even your effort, somebody, all other people's effort, it's all visible, grossly visible efforts. In the material world also, process sometimes means this way and that way, people, all those things, combination, combination of... Just like a factory produces something, they say it is a... It was a process of teamwork, they will say. But here, it's not visible like that. It's a transcendental process. That means your effort you can see, grossly. But, the other effort that, that other mercy that comes from Prabhupada and Krishna and Lord in the heart, these things cannot be seen. If you are Shrabdha, you can capture it. You know this is coming from... You feel that it is mercy. So think of the process as a transcendental process of effort and mercy. Shrama, cheshta from our side, kripa from the Lord. Kripa from transcendence, effort from our side. So, it is God helps those who help themselves.

#### Mayavadis, guru, eternal dependence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GFHkEL1Sipk?t=1655
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We understand it's an eternal process. I am eternally dependent upon my guru."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts the Vaishnava understanding of the guru with the Mayavadi perspective, emphasizing the eternal dependence on the guru for guidance and service, even in the spiritual world.

Mayavadis... The contrast is Mayavadis. Mayavadis say there is nothing like kripa. First of all, there is nothing like God, so where is kripa? It's all... It's all like you have to gyanically come out of it. This entanglement. That is the reason why Mayavadis say that if you are... Say you should accept a guru, they say, to neophytes. And then they say that if you don't advance enough to one day give up the need for a guru, then you are not advancing. So this is, you take a guru, I mean in a derogatory way if you put it, in a personal way if you take it, you accept a guru so that one day you can stamp him out. You advance, then you climb above his shoulders and then here I am. Advanced. So we think, we understand it's an eternal process. I am eternally dependent upon my guru. I go to the spiritual world also, I depend upon my guru. Here he will teach me how to be liberated from material energy, how to purify. There he will teach me how to adopt the right kind of mood to serve the Supreme Lord. Nobody can go and directly serve the Supreme Lord. You need a, there is transcendental intelligence in the spiritual world. So there are masters in the spiritual world who knows how actually to please Krishna. You cannot just go and please Krishna the way you want. So my guru is eternal guru.

That surrender that being talked about, the same faith that you have in Krishna has to be in the guru. Means what? Unless one is authorized by the predecessor spiritual master, one cannot become guru. That is why Prabhupada did not authorize anybody. Because it will amount to cheating. Because the principle says that this is what a guru should be. He should be in the category of Narada Muni. He should be one who is embraced by Lord Krishna. Anybody can be a teacher. Anybody can give, can voice Prabhupada's teaching just like we are speaking now Bhagavatam. These are all gurus who can assist the main guru. Now I am talking on behalf of Srila Prabhupada. Today, tomorrow I will sit there, you will talk on behalf of Prabhupada. It's just a shravanam kirtanam process. It's just a shravanam kirtanam process.

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "These two processes of capability cannot be self-certified."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the importance of absolute faith in the spiritual master and the need for the guru to be capable of taking surrender and transparently passing it on to Krishna, emphasizing that these capabilities cannot be self-certified.

So that is the perception we have. janme janme prabhu sei. Even grossly we sing janme janme prabhu sei. I mean imagine if some guru falls down. You do guru puja everyday janme janme prabhu sei. That means all along till now whatever prayer we made is a joke. That means in see why it is a joke is because in a path to transcendental achievement no portion of it can be no portion of that process can be based on falsity. One, this was very nicely explained once by Vejavaswami. He said this process is to achieve the absolute truth. The path cannot be a path where there is falsity. That means it's not the path. The path also has to be absolute truth. So faith in the truth. So faith in the spiritual master has to be absolute. If it has to be absolute, then the person whom you are putting that faith has to be absolute. There is a science behind all these things. Not simply, you know, anybody, you know, a few friends, few God brothers say Prabhu, so many years now you are a devotee. Why don't you do some service to Srila Prabhupada of accepting some disciples? Okay, okay, I don't want to be guru. I don't want to be personally guru. But all my God brothers are pushing me. So I will take up this service. This is the explanation that many are giving. I mean, you may intend well also. We don't mind. We give benefit for good intention also. Yes, yes, I want to serve Prabhupada like this. But then there is a short-circuiting, a faithful process. According to our Siddhanta, a person accepts a spiritual master, a person is a spiritual master, he has to, you know, surrender his soul to him. Is a person capable of taking that surrender? Is a person capable of transparently passing on to Krishna? And these two processes of capability cannot be self-certified.

### Soulful Japa : Session 1 : Lord Sri Krishna has incarnated again as Nama Avatara-Module 1
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### Nama Avatara, Incarnation, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/BZiZtt80gyQ?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna has incarnated in his name, and specifically Chaitanya Mahaprabhu spread the form of the name."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the concept of Nama Avatara, the incarnation of Lord Krishna in the form of the Hare Krishna Mahamantra, as revealed and propagated by Chaitanya Mahaprabhu.

Hare Krishna! We all start today a series of video talks on soulful japa, and it is going to be basically following the japa modules that I have posted in Telegram and WhatsApp groups. So today, we will be talking about module 1 and module 2, and also I will take questions and try to answer these questions on these two modules. So the module 1 is about a great news. In modern times, the great news is about the incarnation of the Lord. He is present on the earth today! Just like we say 5000 years back, Lord appeared in Vrindavan, and 500 years back, Lord appeared as Bhaktavatara in Nauvadi. In the same way, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu said, kalikale krishna, he revealed this great secret that Krishna has incarnated in his name, and specifically Chaitanya Mahaprabhu spread the form of the name. Every incarnation does specific leelas, so naturally we expect just like Krishna came 5000 years back, so many leelas he did. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu came, he did so many leelas with his devotees. In the same way, Nama Prabhu is also engaged in unlimited leelas on this earth right now. Thousands of devotees, they are participating in this leela.

#### Mercy, Hare Krishna Mahamantra, Spiritual Sound
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/BZiZtt80gyQ?t=158
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "When you chant the Hare Krishna Mahamantra with your tongue, with your material tongue, the Lord descends into that sound, the Hare Krishna Mahamantra, the spiritual sound."
**Summary:** This segment explains that those who receive and follow the Hare Krishna Mahamantra, especially through Srila Prabhupada, are blessed and participate in the leelas of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Krishna, and Nama Prabhu; the Lord descends into the spiritual sound of the mantra when chanted.

Participating means just like Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, he gave out his mercy to so many living entities by distributing the holy name. In the same way, anybody who has received this Hare Krishna Mahamantra, fortunate to receive this Hare Krishna Mahamantra, and especially to follow a pure devotee of this Hare Krishna Mahamantra like His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada, he's really very blessed and they are very much part of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's leela, Krishna's leela, Nama Prabhu's leela. So we should feel very, very blessed in the modern times if we have got an opportunity to know and associate with the Hare Krishna Mahamantra. And what is the specific Lila of the Hare Krishna Mahamantra? What is the specific Lila of Nama Prabhu? The specific Lila of Nama Prabhu is that when you chant the Hare Krishna Mahamantra with your tongue, with your material tongue, the Lord descends into that sound, the Hare Krishna Mahamantra, the spiritual sound. There is a spiritual sound, that sound that we chant with our tongue, because the tongue is material. It's a material sound, sound of matter. And in that sound produced by matter, the matter vibration, into that sound, a sound from the spiritual world, Shuddha Nama, Nama Prabhu. He takes an avatara and enters into this sound. And this sound is no longer an ordinary sound, the sound that is generated with your material tongue is no longer an ordinary sound. And that's called Shakti Avesh Avatara.

---
#### Purification, Self-Realization, God-Realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/BZiZtt80gyQ?t=291
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Lord's incarnation as Nama Prabhu means, he first he descends into the Nama that you chant with your tongue, then through the ears, he enters our hearts and in the heart, there something called Chetodakpanam happens."
**Summary:** The Lord descends into the sound of the Hare Krishna Mahamantra, enters our ears and heart, purifies us, and bestows Satchitananda, leading to self-realization and God-realization; receiving the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra and serving it daily is the greatest blessing.

That sound of the Lord, his Lila continues. First he descends into the sound. Then you hear that sound. The Lord enters our ears and then into our heart. And these Lila's continue. What is the Lila? After he enters his heart, the Lord is like the sun of Satchitananda. Just like sun is a brilliant light and heat, in the same way Lord is a brilliant sign of Satchitananda. His Satchitananda energies flow into the soul when the Nama reaches our heart. And this way we become purified, our heart becomes purified. Satt means eternity, Chitta means pure knowledge, who I am, who is God and Ananda is experience of love and pleasure in serving the Supreme Lord. So Lord, Lord's incarnation as Nama Prabhu means, he first he descends into the Nama that you chant with your tongue, then through the ears, he enters our hearts and in the heart, there something called Chetodakpanam happens. Our hearts are cleansed of ignorance because the name of the Lord is pure knowledge. That pure knowledge goes inside our heart and it churns our Chitta, our consciousness, our memories, everything, it cleanses, cleanses and then we experience what is self-realization and God-realization. So this is the specific Lila of Nama Prabhu. Now today it is happening all over the world and everything that has happened in your life is not as important as the Nama Prabhu. It is the greatest blessing to receive this Hare Krishna Maha Mantra and to serve the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra every day.

Therefore, he's called Namo Mahavadanyaya, the most merciful incarnation of the Lord. Just like if you want to go, if you want to see Archa Vigraha, you go to a temple and have darshan. You want to. But here simply vibrate your tongue. God is there. God is there. God is there. God is there. God is there. God is there. God is there. God is there. God is there. God is there. God is there. God is there. God is there. God is there. God is there. God is there. God is there. God is there. God is there. God is there present on your tongue. #### Srila Prabhupada, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Six Goswamis
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/BZiZtt80gyQ?t=578
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Srila Prabhupada actually was blessed by Chaitanya Mahaprabhu to take this nama all over the world."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada was chosen to spread the name worldwide, blessed by Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and empowered through his connection with Rupa Goswami and the six Goswamis in Vrindavan, making the Hare Krishna movement an expansion of their teachings.

And Prabhupada was the chosen devotee of the Lord who took this name all over the world. Once Prabhupada was asked, Srila Prabhupada, if Chaitanya Maha Prabhu wanted his name to be spread all over the world, why didn't Chaitanya Mahaprabhu himself go to the Western world? Why didn't he himself go all over the world 500 years back? Srila Prabhupada laughed and said, Yes, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu wanted to give that credit to me. So Srila Prabhupada actually was blessed by Chaitanya Mahaprabhu to take this nama all over the world. And when you are in Vrindavan, you should... There is something very important in this connection of nama vathara, I want to say. Srila Prabhupada, before he went to America, he stayed in Vrindavan for six years in Radha Damodara temple. And that Radha Damodara temple is very much part of the Leelas of the Lord. Lord Krishna. It's one corner of the Seva Kunj where Krishna performed his Rasa dance. And that Radha Damodara temple is a location where Rupa Goswami Samadhi is there, Srila Jeeva Goswami Samadhi is there. You are all aware that our leaders of our parampara are the six Goswamis, the original leaders. Because Chaitanya Mahaprabhu directly instructed them what is Krishna consciousness, the entire science of Krishna consciousness to the six Goswamis. And these six Goswamis, they prepared all this literature which you are enjoying today, which Prabhupada has translated and Prabhupada is further writing. And all this knowledge is coming from the six Goswamis. And the leader of all the six Goswamis was Rupa Goswami. And Rupa Goswami's Samadhi is situated in Radha Damodara temple. And Prabhupada, Prabhupada spent six years staying in Radha Damodara temple. Across the window, he could see Rupa Goswami Samadhi and he was intensely praying to Rupa Goswami Samadhi and he was intensely praying to Jeeva Goswami, Rupa Goswami. And he had darshan of Rupa Goswami and Jeeva Goswami live. Though 500 years back, they had left this mortal body, but they appeared to Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada, they blessed him and empowered him to write about Krishna consciousness and preach all over the world. So this Hare Krishna movement is directly an expansion of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, the six Goswamis and that is how this movement is spreading all over the world.

#### Soulful Japa, Devotion, Association
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/BZiZtt80gyQ?t=770
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Lord has incarnated, he is on my tongue, he is ready to go into my heart and cleanse my heart. But then, how effectively this happens depends a lot on whether you allow the Holy Name to go inside your heart."
**Summary:** We are fortunate to chant the Hare Krishna Mahamantra, honoring the Lord's presence by attentively hearing and chanting with devotion; the effectiveness of the Lord cleansing our hearts depends on allowing the Holy Name to enter, which will be explored further in the soulful japa series.

So we are all very fortunate to have this kind of an opportunity to pray to Rupa Goswami. So we are all very fortunate to have this kind of an opportunity to chant the Hare Krishna Mahamantra. Now, the most important thing, the Lord has appeared, he has incarnated, once he has incarnated, we need to... the Lord is, we know that the Lord is present before us. When he is present before us, we have to receive him properly. We have to honor him properly. And how do you honor a sound? We have to honor the sound of the sound, by attentively hearing the sound, chanting with devotion and receiving him with relish, happily, heartfully, we should receive the avatar. So Srila Prabhupada, basically now our challenge before us, Lord has incarnated, he is on my tongue, he is ready to go into my heart and cleanse my heart. But then, how effectively this happens depends a lot on whether you allow the Holy Name to go inside your heart. The Lord is on the tongue, but will you allow yours? There is a way, there is a particular consciousness by which the Lord goes into our heart. So this soulful japa series, I called it soulful because we have to soulfully receive the Lord and we have to soulfully serve the Lord. And what is this soulful receiving, soulful welcoming and soulful association of the Supreme Lord in the nama form, that the series of this we will discuss in more and more videos. Thank you for being with me today. Hare Krishna.

#### Seva, Savior, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wsY4ZcnxrYs?t=264
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Service is attentive chanting, hearing and repeating the name of the Lord, which amounts to pleasing the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the concepts of savior, sevak, and seva, explaining that seva is offering service to the savior through attentive chanting with love and devotion.

We introduce three terms: one is saviour, sevak, and seva. Now japa, the Incarnation has to be served by the living entities. So Nama Prabhu is served by the sevaka. One who serves is called sevaka. One who received the service is called saviour, and he is served. And here the saviour is a brilliant sun of Satchidananda, Krishna Nama, Nama Prabhu, and the Shakti Abhishek Avatara combined. And seva. Seva is what? What is the process of service? What is the actual service? Seva is offering service to saviour. What is the service? Service is attentive chanting, hearing and repeating the name of the Lord, which amounts to pleasing the Lord. Service is meant for pleasing the Lord. So seva is that. Now in this chanting, hearing and tasking is a seva. But the seva has to be always rendered with love, with devotion. With attention, devotion, love, surrender, all those things. Because it is, japa is not a, simply a process of mind control, mind training, etc. No. Mind training is one part of it, small part of it. Basically it is about, you know, offering all of our soul energies to the Lord. So, that was. So remember this concept.

mind, I gave the example. I don't know whether I gave the example. I think yesterday I gave this example to somebody who wanted clarification. For those who are familiar with computer, let's say this computer screen. Okay. That is like the foreground attention, where activity is going on. And the hard disk is like the, like the subconscious mind, chittam, all memory, everything is there. Then there is a RAM memory from the hard disk, what applications you are right now running, etc. Few. The quantity of that subconscious memory comes into your RAM memory. So in the way, in the same way, the background mind is like the RAM memory. And it's not very difficult to understand. Just like you, you have relationship with thousands of people. You come across, suddenly you come across your father. Immediately. Your RAM will, your subconscious will load into your RAM, all the things about your father. That is how you're able to relate with the father. As soon as you see his image, you'll talk to him, you'll relate with him meaningfully. You don't have to make any effort to remember, Oh, he's my father. These are his qualities. All these things are not required. So all automatically it is called background context. So it's a context mind. Are you same? I mean, in very next minute you go and meet somebody else, something else comes in immediately. It's loaded. Where is it all coming from? It's all coming from the subconscious mind. Another example I gave is just like in a theatrical play, there is a stage where actually the drama is happening. And then there is, there are backdrops, different kinds of backdrops. Suppose the scene is a forest scene. There'll be a forest backdrop, house scene, palace scene, a palace backdrop. So these backdrops are like context. They're not active conscious mind, but they're static, they're staying there. The active thing is going on in the stage. Similarly the conscious background, conscious mind, your free will is not active there. It is just there in the background to give you a meaning to the activity in the foreground. So then what is subconscious mind in this example? Because to make those sets for the drama, there'll be 10, 20 sets. All that has been done off stage. You know, so many people worked on it. They all prepared and brought it and set it up. One by one, as per scene, they open the screens. So in the same way, that is like the subconscious mind. So in this way, it's very clear about this, this understanding.

So that is, so for that, another very important point in that connection, we said, what is the relevance of following four regulative principles? If you don't follow four regulative principles in order to explain that, I gave the example of a firewood. If you have a piece of firewood dipped in water and you throw it in the fire, you see that the firewood does not catch fire. That means visibly you cannot experience the fire. The firewood cannot experience a fire because all the heat is going to not to burn the wood, but it is going to dry and evaporate the water. So our sinful activities, our sinful reactions are all like this, wetting the wood. They are like water. So if we want rapid effect, if we want our soul, our soul is like the firewood, if we want our soul to catch fire, catch the fire of Satchidananda, we have to keep it dry. We have to free, keep ourselves free from sinful activities. Yes. Activity, sinful reaction. And the four regulative principles are the pillars of sinless life. No intoxication, no meat eating, no illicit sex, no gambling. As I said, these four activities are like a huge, like a major wetting process, like going and dipping in a pond fully. From your sessions, I understood that the reciprocation, Nama Avatara, does not depend whether one is initiated or not. If the latter is having Shuddha on the holy name, no problem. Japa is a relationship with the holy name. So there are different degrees of relationship as you progress with the personality. So at some stage, the Nama would want you to... To be connected to a Siddha, a one who has perfected his chanting, who is a pure devotee of the Nama. So then only you can progress further. So even if you're not initiated, you can progress up to a certain bit, but you cannot progress totally. There has to be... So it's a personal thing. You have to go step by step, step by step, you have to go. So therefore you have to... Nama is a Prakrit Nama. No need of empowerment. But the name you chant needs empowerment. Then. Lord's name is invested with all his energies. Can we say his name is more powerful than him? How can you say that? Lord is invested all his power means not that he has become bankrupt. Poor Akali, he emptied everything to the Shakti of a Shanaama. He is an eternal source of Shakti. One minus one is one in spiritual dynamics. Does different names of Krishna have different strengths? No, all of them are equally empowered with all the Shaktis. But their reciprocation is different. In other words, reciprocation means their Rasa. If you chant Narayana Narayana, the Rasa is not like same as Rasa with Krishna.
