#### Chanting Hare Krishna, Liberation, Universal Religious Principle
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/im4fsM_E_fw?t=45
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Without chanting this Hare Krishna, there is no other alternative for self-realization in this age."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of chanting Hare Krishna as the only universal religious principle in this age for self-realization and liberation, as stated by Chaitanya Mahaprabhu.

Hare Krishna. Yesterday we discussed how, right now, the incarnation of Lord Krishna is on this Earth and is engaging so many living entities in His pastime of giving out mercy to these living entities. And yesterday I came across another quote, you know, apart from what Chaitanya Mahaprabhu stated, that Kali Kale Nama Rupe Krishna Avatara, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu also stated, you know, a few things when He was preaching to the impersonalists in Benares. One may... Prabhupada talks about this in February 6, 1967, San Francisco in a lecture. I just read that, it's very interesting. Now one may question, oh Vedanta Sutra, understanding Vedanta Sutra, one is liberated from the impurities of the world, but one is not liberated from the material entanglement. So you are asking me simply to chant Hare Krishna? Will that purpose be served? Oh, his guru said, yes it will be served. He said, Krishna mantra haite have samsara mochana. If you go on, continue this, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, there will be a day when you come to the perfectional stage. Then you will be liberated. The same result as one gets out of studying Vedanta philosophy or Sankhya philosophy, same result also you will get by simply chanting Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna. Krishna Nama haite pabe Krishnaera charana. Not only that, Vedantists, sometimes they cannot reach up to the Krishna planet. But your advantage will be that you will not only be liberated, but you will be achieving the highest planet, planetary kingdom or the kingdom of God. You will be able to achieve there. And naam vinu, naam vinu kali kaale nai aara dharma. Now Chaitanya Mahaprabhu explained that this Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna is the only universal religious principle in this age. No other religion process will be effective. Because people are so many and diverted in different ways. This is the common formula and that is the effective formula. So Chaitanya Mahaprabhu says, naam vinu kali kaale. Without chanting this Hare Krishna, there is no other alternative for self-realization in this age. Naam vinu kali kaale nai aara dharma sarva mantra saara naama yai shastra marma. Now the essence of all scriptures is this Hare Krishna. Essence, sarva shastra marma means essence of all scriptures. And sarva mantra saara and there are different kinds of hymns and mantras. And this is the, I mean to say, topmost part of all mantras. In this way my Guru Maharaj asked me to chant Hare Krishna. These were statements made by Chaitanya

#### Cleansing the Heart, Ignorance, Knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/im4fsM_E_fw?t=273
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Hare Krishna Maha Mantra is an ocean of pure knowledge."
**Summary:** This segment discusses how chanting Hare Krishna cleanses the heart by removing ignorance and revealing our relationship with the Supreme Self, emphasizing that the mantra is a source of pure knowledge.

before the impersonal scholars. So we will come to the module 2. Basically we know that Srila Prabhupada distributed the Harinam all over the world in the form of, you know, individual chanting of Hare Krishna as well as mass chanting of Hare Krishna. Because as it is, as Chaitanya Mahaprabhu has assured, it will cleanse our heart. And what is the meaning of cleansing our heart? Cleansing our heart means what is the heart? There should be some dirt in the heart to cleanse. And what is that dirt? That dirt is basically, it is ignorance of the self and ignorance of our relationship with the Supreme Self, God. So cleansing the heart means cleansing the covering that is covering our body with the medium of the Supreme Self. And the medium of the Supreme Self is the same, covering this relationship, covering who we are and making us feel we are this material body. So this is ignorance. So how can ignorance be removed? Ignorance can be removed only by knowledge. But where will the knowledge come from? Yes the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra is an ocean of pure knowledge. God is all knowledge and the holy name is non-different from Krishna Himself. Krishna is all knowledge and whatever appropriate knowledge, step by step knowledge that is required for us to rise out of this ignorance of who we are, the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra reciprocates with the devotee who serves Him every day as per the instructions of the spiritual master.

**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/im4fsM_E_fw?t=391

And the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra reciprocates with the devotee who serves Him every day as per the instructions of the spiritual master. And then ultimately by Lord Chaitanya's costless mercy, we can experience what is Krishna consciousness and what is our relationship with Krishna and enjoy love of Godhead. Now the different kinds of material existence, material knowledge, so much of knowledge, library is full of knowledge, internet is full of knowledge, but they are all ignorance. That is not knowledge. Knowledge is about the self, knowledge is about God, knowledge is about God's activities, devotees' activities, knowledge is about the spiritual world, details of the spiritual world. In the material world, everybody is seeking actually existence in the spiritual world. They need that, they are seeking for a quality of existence in the spiritual world. They want love that is there in the spiritual world but it is perverted here. They can't get that love. What the soul is expecting you cannot get. Love, intimacy, play, so many sense pleasures. They are all there in this body. They give these things. The body and the senses provide us something but it never satisfies.

#### Holy Name, Satchitananda, Faith
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/im4fsM_E_fw?t=477
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The holy name, the Hare Krishna Nama Prabhu is an ocean of Satchitananda."
**Summary:** This segment explains that the holy name is an ocean of Satchitananda (eternity, knowledge, and bliss) and that the Lord descends with all His energies when chanted, requiring faith to perceive His presence.

His bodily existence in this material world is a big trap. So the holy name as I described yesterday, as Chaitanya Mahaprabhu says and Srila Prabhupada repeatedly says, the holy name, the Hare Krishna Nama Prabhu is an ocean of Satchitananda. He is a Surya of Satchitananda. So when we say that the Lord descends on my tongue, the Lord never descends alone. He descends with all His Shakti. Just like the sun is coming down, He will come down with the sunshine. You cannot separate sunshine from sun. The same way the ocean of Satchitananda cannot be separated from the Lord Himself. So Kalikale Namo Rupe Krishna Avatara means along with all His Shakti He comes. And His Shakti can all be under three categories: Sat Shakti, Chit Shakti and Ananda Shakti. So this potency, it is always good to compare the sun and sunlight. How sun is emanating light and heat. In the same way, the Nama that is on our tongue is reservoir of Satchitananda. But we cannot see. Why you cannot see? You cannot see because you don't... Sat means eternal. Our own soul we cannot see. So how can we see the Nama? Chit is pure knowledge. Pure knowledge needs pure intelligence to recognize pure knowledge. So you cannot sense pure knowledge. Ananda you need pure senses and pure mind to experience love and pleasure, spiritual pleasure of the presence of the Lord.

The first quote, the first week he practiced concurrent chanting... hearing with the direction from Shri La Prabhupada's quote, golden quotes of Jaipur. One may ask, okay, then when my doing goes to thinking, now this knowing can pull that here. That is what we are trying to do wrongly and we get tired. Mentally you get tired. **Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that success in japa is due to mercy and stresses the importance of humility and giving due respect to all, including karmis, while maintaining proper priorities.

So just because we do mindful japa successfully, never become proud. Everything is by the mercy of Prabhupada and Nama Prabhu. The moment we become proud, we will lose that power. We lose that power. Why? Because it has come from them. The chesta ruchi, Nama chesta ruchi has come from Nama Prabhu. Why? Why? So we have to be very careful in these things. Humility means respecting all devotees, respecting everybody, karmas, everybody. Everybody has a, everybody deserves a due credit, whatever it, due respect. Due respect means what? Respect of different degrees. Soul deserves, soul is Krishna's part and parcel. Soul deserves unconditional respect. Everybody is divine part and parcel of God. But karmas come before you, they're full of false egos. That jiva also deserves some credit, whatever material credit he, material respect he is. If I am in the material world, I have to give him the proper material respect. Because what he has is what God has given. If he is respectable, to degree he is respectable. Sometimes devotees think, why we are after God? Why? Why? Why? Because after some politician came to the temple and why you are giving VIP treatment and all these things. For us Krishna is supreme personality godhead, pure devotee is a VIP. All these karmas, how they can be? No, no, no. We have to give due respect to everybody. Not, you don't have to give due respect that you give to a pure devotee, to a politician. But for some reason in the material, that power is there, that material power deserves, say, let's say he is a chief minister. In other words, we are dependent upon him for everything. So, we are dependent on the mercy for self-purification. We are begging for self-purification. That means he doesn't have to do anything. Nama Prabhu is not doing some service for you. Nama Prabhu's presence itself will purify you. He does not have to do anything. He does not have to take a broom and sweep and all those things. I will walk there. No problem. In the end, I will get that desire. Chesta. Here also, we have chesta. But this chesta is for pleasing the Nama program. #### attentiveness, mind control, relationship with Krishna, japa
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/B0d2uC9-dh4?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "ultimately japa is a relationship japa is a relationship japa is not a process"
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that japa is ultimately a relationship with Krishna, not just a process, although process is important for beginners.

Hare Krishna. So yesterday we did the second module. Again, in a line, the first module was about how the Lord has descended on this earth and is in our midst in the form of Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. And next module we talked about how actually the Lord is presenting Himself. We chant with our material tongue and the Lord incarnates into that material sound, and then we hear that sound. To hear the sound, we need the mind, and if you hear this sound, we are feeling like we have been sacrificed, and the Lord sound with the mind, then the sound would enter our heart and it will cleanse all the ignorance and enlighten us about who we are, who is God, everything. So the challenge, if we do not give attention to our chanting, because mind is the carrier of attention from the soul, if we don't train our mind to carry the attention energy from the soul to the Nama who is before us, Nama Prabhu is before us, then we are not, the soul is not touching the Nama. So that the challenge that we left yesterday, we ended with that, that the real challenge is how to be attentive to hearing the Hare Krishna Mahamantra, and hearing is like a sound embrace. Just like you embrace somebody with your hands, you embrace the sound with your attention, and then purification ensues. The holy name, again to repeat, the holy name is brilliant like a son, the son of Sat Chidananda, radiator unlimited Sapta Chit and Ananda energy, and it is this Sat Chidananda mercy of the Lord that actually makes us or Sat Chidananda Icist our soul when he has apparently have to repeat the holy name, I that is most anger out of that one for us right so towards the other side and from the Lord we are like a piece of iron, but when you are in touch with the fire of sacchidananda, we become fire like, we develop our original sacchidananda quality. Now we just talked today about how to train the mind, means how important it is to train the mind to provide attention between the soul and the nama that is on our ears. Does that necessarily mean that if the mind is trained and the mind is disciplined, it solves all the problem, or ultimately is it that just because if I train my mind to give attention to the nama on my ears, will I get bhakti, is that everything? So I want to caution the devotees that even though we will be talking a lot about training the mind, we should know that the training and disciplining the mind has its limited role in making us associate with the holy name. Ultimately japa is a relationship, japa is a relationship, japa is not a process

So this is our this these are these are the things that we give to the Lord and the devotees there is so many different soul energies that we give to the Lord and the devotees there is so many all the things that finally that builds a relationship with with the holy name. So all the things that finally that builds a relationship with with the holy name. So therefore in summary I want to say that don't get obsessed with processes and don't get obsessed with mind skills, but you have to know it has a place, it has a role to play because we are now covered with our material mind, material minds cooperation is very important for the soul to build a relationship with

#### relationship, service, mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/B0d2uC9-dh4?t=637
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "japa is a relationship what is the relationship you have to understand the relationship is there are three three components to any relationship one is the relationship one who is receiving the service he is called saviour"
**Summary:** The speaker explains that japa is a relationship with three components: the sevaka (server), the saviour (Lord), and the seva (service), and quotes Prabhupada on the causeless mercy of the Lord.

as I said there are japa is a relationship what is the relationship you have to understand the relationship is there are three three components to any relationship one is the relationship one who is receiving the service he is called saviour in a japa the saviour is the sound incarnation of the lord in japa the the sevaka the one who is serving is called sevaka and that is the chanter and the seva is a process of service what chanting and hearing and tasking this is a service that we are offering to the holy name this is a seva. So japa is a relationship of the seva with the saviour through the process of seva so that we should always remember that three components are there when you are doing japa. Usually when we think japa is a process we completely forget saviour that there is somebody who is receiving your service then it becomes mechanical and impersonal and that's how if we have if we have chanted for years together without saviour. In the picture then very difficult unless you train your mind to change that way of chanting there is one one quote of Prabhupada which I will read out there is no mechanical process to see the form of the Lord or completely if it completely depends on the causeless mercy of the Lord. We cannot demand the Lord to be present before our vision just as we cannot demand the sun to rise whenever we like the sun rises out of his own accord so also the Lord is pleased to be present out of his causeless mercy one should simply avoid the opportune moment

and go on discharging his prescribed duty in devotional service of the Lord. In Narada Muni thought that the Lord could be seen again by the same mechanical process which was successful in the first attempt but in spite of his utmost effort he couldn't make the second attempt successful. The Lord is completely independent of all obligations he can simply bound up by the tie of unalloyed devotion nor is he visible or perceivable by our material senses. When he pleases being satisfied with the sincere attempt of devotion service depending completely on the mercy of the Lord. Then he may be seen out of his own accord. So just see ultimately we are relating with the Lord. Material attempts will not necessarily give us the Lord. Material attempts is required in other words whatever effort we have to put with our mind with our body. All these things. All these things we have to do. But it doesn't guarantee that at the end you will have the result because result of relationship with Krishna, result of a relationship of service is a gift given by him. So ultimately if we have to give up mechanical chanting and develop natural chanting then we need the mercy of the Lord. That we have to remember. So as I said it's ultimately japa is very personal, very loving and a reciprocal activity

**Summary:** The speaker cautions against reducing japa to a mere process, emphasizing devotion and removing mental obstacles, and introduces the concept of foreground and background in the mind.

between the sevaka and the saviour. Even though we are there is a lot of stress in the beginning about the process the reason why I am cautioning this is because we have to be while I am going to talk lot about process please do not reduce japa to be a perfection of a process. Please do not reduce japa to be a perfection of a process. Please do not reduce japa to be a perfection of a process. Please do not reduce japa to be a perfection of a process. Ultimately it is a perfection of devotion. Devotion comes from the heart. At the same time we have to be remove all the obstacles that our mind brings due to our conditioning. So there are back in the while doing japa there are some things in the foreground and background. Foreground is in front of the background and background is behind the foreground. This concept of background and foreground is something we all regularly practice. Our mind works in that particular way by a combination of foreground and background mind. For instance now you are listening to work. You are listening to the work. You are listening to the work. You are listening to what I am speaking. Now what I am speaking is what your foreground mind or your conscious mind is holding onto. Your conscious attention is in the foreground mind. You are listening to what I am saying but at the same time though you are not conscious in the background mind you are very much aware that who is the speaker. In the background you are very much aware that who is the speaker. You are very much aware what is the purpose of this process of you know hearing the speaker. So that is all these things are in the background. Different feelings that are there all your expectation hopes everything in hearing the speaker it is all in the background but in the foreground is the actual process of chanting

**Summary:** The speaker describes the background elements of faith (Shraddha), humility, and love (Preeti), emphasizing the desire to please the Lord.

So jana. There has to be background jana. Then there is Shraddha. That is also in the background. You have to have faith because the Lord is on my tongue. You are not you are not seeing with your eyes. You have no evidence. Lord has incarnated on your tongue. You have no evidence. It is only Shraddha. It is only Shraddha from the Shastra. The Lord is very merciful. He enters through the ears. The empowered sound. Shakti Avesha Nama. Enters through the ear holes. Through my mind into my heart. This is also a Shraddha. Because ultimately what your senses tell you is it is an ordinary sound. So Shraddha is another. Another faculty. Another energy of the soul. Only soul can give Shraddha. So Shraddha. Then there is an attitude. You know an identity. I am servant of Krishna. Can I have different attitudes? So Chaitanya Mahaprabhu says one should chant with humility. So attitude of humility. Attitude of servitude. Of course. The egoness that I am a servant of the Lord. The servitude attitude automatically comes. But then you need that humility. I am insignificant servant of the Lord. So like that so many feelings are there. Has to be there in the background when we chant. Then need the love energy or Preeti. Love means what? The desire to please the loved one. That means the Lord is before me now. So in the background I should have the desire to please him as much as possible. That is what makes us be attentive. He is pleased with. If I give my attention to him he is pleased. If I chant. If I am giving my attention somewhere else that much he is less pleased. Of course you are vibrating his name he is pleased. And if you are chanting with devotion with faith then he is more pleased. So the desire to please him is called love. Means one has to have a strong desire to please the loved one. That is called Preeti. In the Bhagavad Gita also Krishna says Bhajatam Preeti Purvakam. That means service has to be rendered with Preeti. Service has to be rendered with desire to please the holy name who is descended and who is right before you. To please that personality mantra after mantra after mantra.

But last four or five months you are not used to calling him Anand, you don't associate. So next few days you say Anand, Anand, Anand, Anand, immediately your son comes into your picture, isn't it? So it has gone into your subconscious. So let's say after six months you call Anand. Even you're not used to it. You're not even hearing the sound as soon as you say Anand, your son is fully coming in before you. He is loaded on your background. So it takes time. So in the same way, the moment we say Krishna, he is the Supreme Personality of Godhead. No one equal to or greater than him. These thoughts will come into the feeling and these feelings will come into the background. Supremacy of the Lord will come into it like that. So you have to increase the quality of your association with Guru, Sadhu and Shastra, in a descending process we should receive it. If you don't understand something, what you read, please understand this, whatever he said, he is 100% correct and only I have to understand and go on hearing again and again and again and by Lord's mercy, that actual truth will be understood by us.

#### faith, holy name, reciprocation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/B0d2uC9-dh4?t=2112
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If it happens, it is nothing but Hare Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker shares an incident about the construction of the Bangalore temple as an example of how faith in the holy name can lead to miraculous results.

Could you please share one or two incidents from your life that significantly strengthened your faith in the Holy Name? Yes. I can tell one thing. This temple at Hare Krishna Hill, Bangalore. The manifestation of this temple increased my faith in the Holy Name because it was practically an impossible thing in my understanding at a time in the year 1991 when only few pillars were standing in the ground level. At that time, I had not even made a full estimate, whatever donors gave little money I started. But then one day I sat down and calculated, okay, I got this many devotees, this much every month, this much money they are collecting, this much is required for maintenance. And then I did a balance calculation. Then I calculated how much the total cost of the project. This much. Then I was surprised to find it would take me 60 years to build this temple. At that time, what came in my mind, looking at that early morning I was chanting and I was on the rock and I remember now also, okay, maybe this will not happen. 60 years it will take. If it happens, it is nothing but Hare Krishna. Hare Krishna. Krishna Krishna. Hare Hare. And it miraculously happened. In seven years it happened. So this one incident I can tell. Hundreds and hundreds. Devotees once they become attached to the holy name and they depend on the holy name, countless, every day is a reciprocation for the devotee.

Krishna is Supreme Personality of Godhead. This, your attention goes away from the Shraddha. Lord is before you. So understand this concept of background and foreground. ### Soulful Japa : Session 4 : Mindless japa, Mindful japa, and Soulful japa - Module 4
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### Nama Prabhu, Sound Incarnation, Attention
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ir5igQ2KZLQ?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When we hear the sound, the sound enters our heart and the sound enters our heart, the sound is not, the sound incarnation is with all his energies, Satchitananda energies and there is a flow of mercy of the Lord in the form of Sattva, Chitta and Ananda, mercy into our heart which cleanses our heart."
**Summary:** The Lord is present in the sound of the holy name, which enters our heart and cleanses it, but connecting our Chitta with the Lord is a challenge, achievable through attention.

Hare Krishna, Srila Prabhupada ki Jai, Nama Prabhu ki Jai. So we will continue today. We have covered three lessons from three modules so far, and today we will take the fourth one. Before that, just to brush upon the first three lessons, in one line each, first lesson was that the Lord is in our presence. In the fourth lesson, we have covered three lessons from three modules so far, and today we will, the form of sound incarnation in this modern times, right now, in the present time, Lord Krishna is engaged in his pastime, in the form of Nama Prabhu, the main pastime being liberating thousands and thousands of Jivas from this material world. By giving his association, he is brilliant like a sun. He is a Satchitananda vigraha emanating brilliant rays of Satchitananda, and when he incarnates, he incarnates with his brilliance, and the sound that we chant with our tongue, Hare Krishna, into that sound the Lord incarnates as a sound from the spiritual world. So on our tongue, it is not only the sound that I create with my tongue that is existing, but also the spiritual sound that has come down into the material sound, materially generated sound. Thus the sound has become the Saktyavesha avatara. It is an omnipotent sound, empowered sound, empowered sound, not ordinarily empowered, but it is an omnipotent sound. That sound can do anything. Now that sound enters our ears, and through our mind, we hear this sound. When our attention is on the sound, then we hear the sound. When we hear the sound, the sound enters our heart, and the sound enters our heart. The sound is not... the sound incarnation is with all his energies, Satchitananda energies, and there is a flow of mercy of the Lord in the form of Sattva, Chitta and Ananda, mercy into our heart which cleanses our heart. This is the heart of Asat Achit Nirananda. This is a reality, this is an actual phenomena that is happening, and we discussed yesterday that the biggest challenge, the first biggest challenge is to connect our Chitta with the Lord who has created us.

#### Mind Control, Attention, Discipline
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ir5igQ2KZLQ?t=234
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So therefore disciplining the mind is so important to ensure that the attention connection is there."
**Summary:** Disciplining the mind is crucial for maintaining attention on the holy name, as the mind tends to wander, but we have the power to train it.

The Chitta has descended down and that is only possible through the conduit of attention. So the mind is the carrier of attention. Attention is a property of the soul, it's the energy of the soul. I am, I as a soul, I am where my attention is. So if I have to touch the holy name, my attention has to be there on the holy name, on the sound. And mind is now in our conditioned state, he is a master of wherever it wants it to take the attention. Our attention is completely given over to the mind. So therefore disciplining the mind is so important to ensure that the attention connection is there. It is not very difficult because the mind is our servant. If the mind is taking away our attention here and there, it is because we have trained our mind in that way. We can train our mind the way we want. It will again start listening to us. So this secret, the mind does not have any power over you other than the power that you have given to the mind.

**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ir5igQ2KZLQ?t=340

**Summary:** Japa progresses from disciplined chanting, requiring sacrifice, to spontaneous chanting with attachment, achievable within six months with proper practice.

So there are two stages of development of our Japa. First is... Disciplining Japa or disciplined Japa. Voluntarily we discipline our mind to establish and stay connected with the Nama Avatara. Because by default the mind has its own tendency to run away in different directions because of the force of Kala. Because if I was not doing Japa, my mind was going to be a nuclear power. But now I am not doing Japa. Doing japa, mind would be running here there here there here there so many things will be running so that same force continues even after I start japa so I have to discipline my mind to hold the attention on Nama Prabhu so that is disciplined japa. Disciplining requires tapas shakti or austerity or sacrifice. So the beginning of japa is always as a sacrifice. No doubt by discipline japa, by keeping my chitta connected to the nama continuously for 16 rounds day after day day after day day after day or at least as many rounds as possible, there is a flow of pure spiritual energy into the heart of the chanter and there is awakening of spiritual qualities. There is awakening of spiritual knowledge. There's awakening of even faith, deepening the faith in the supreme personality of godhead. All these things start awakening and with the help of that awakening our discipline chanting will slowly move towards spontaneous chanting or natural chanting. That is when you chant with priti, we develop our attachment to the Lord's name. So first chanting as a discipline which requires tapas, austerity, sacrifice. So we need first sacrifice then it will lead to priti. So it we need not think that my whole life I have to struggle to hold my attention on the holy name is very very uninspiring to take up japa like that. No, not at all. If you're very serious about it, if you practice it properly, then in six months you can complete your journey of disciplined japa because mind doesn't take much time to learn. However, if you practice it properly, then in six months you can complete your journey of disciplined japa because mind doesn't take much time to learn.

mindless japa, mindful japa and soulful japa. Oh before that I want to say something else very important. I want to read one quote of Prabhupada of how the mercy of the Lord is freely reigning for everybody to take. This is the Lord's mercy. This is from teachings of Queen Kunti chapter 12. The Lord's mercy upon the fallen souls is equally distributed. The sun rays are open to everyone, but the capacities of the receptacles differ. By utilizing the sun's rays, sometimes even stones become valuable, whereas a blind man cannot see the sun, although there are enough sun rays before him. The Lord's mercy is open to him, but he is reluctant to receive it. Somehow or the other if someone puts himself open in the sun rays, he is sure to get requisite benefit, both by heat and ultraviolet rays. So the same thing is applicable to the holy name. Somehow or the other If we expose our chitta, our heart to the rays of Satchitananda from the Nama Prabhu, we can receive Satchitananda mercy. It is freely available. The Lord's mercy is freely reigning.

#### Purity, Capacity, Chitta
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ir5igQ2KZLQ?t=1078
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "It is no fault of the rain."
**Summary:** The capacity to receive the Lord's mercy depends on the purity of our chitta, similar to how different materials absorb rain differently.

Now, somebody may ask, if it is freely reigning, when I chant, why can't I instantly get completely purified? As I said, when you chant, even if you have attention, once you set up attention connection with the Nama, the Lord is there. When the Lord is in front of you, you establish your connection. Rain is falling all over. The rain falls on the rock, rain falls on the mud. But the capacity of the rock to absorb the rain is different. Capacity of the mud to absorb the rain is different. It is no fault of the rain. It is a fault of the... It is a capacity of the rock and capacity... capacity of the mud. Different capacity to receive the water. In the same way, our chitta, chittam, means our contaminated consciousness. The capacity of the contaminated consciousness differs in its ability to receive this kripa. Now, when rain falls, you hold a vessel, the vessel fills with water according to different capacity of the vessel different water you can collect, different quantities of water you can collect similarly the capacity of the chitta corresponds to the level of purity of our chitta

#### Mindless Chanting, Attention, Present Moment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ir5igQ2KZLQ?t=1805
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Mindless chanting means you are training your mind, auto mind, train your mind to automatically move your tongue, your lips, chanting and even beads are moving, everything is moving, even 16 rounds are done."
**Summary:** Mindless chanting involves mechanically performing japa while the mind is elsewhere, in the past or future, preventing us from truly hearing and absorbing the holy name.

Now come to mindless japa. First, understand that the Lord is incarnated before you right now, here and now. Not yesterday and today. You are relating with the Lord here and now before you. So, where should your consciousness be? If you want to serve him, you have to be here and now. So, mindless chanting means you are training your mind, auto mind, train your mind to automatically move your tongue, your lips, chanting and even beads are moving, everything is moving, even 16 rounds are done. Because of training, only mechanically you do that, all the while your attention and you was somewhere else. And what is that somewhere else? Either there and then in the past or there and then in the future. So, therefore, when your consciousness is out of the present space here and the present time, your mind is absorbed in something else and something else, then you are not hearing. You cannot hear. You are not taking the holy name into your heart. So, that is called mindless chanting. Mind is not on the japa. I am where my attention is and where my mind is, my attention is also.

#### Mindful Chanting, Attention, Present Moment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ir5igQ2KZLQ?t=1907
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When your mind is here and now, then your chanting is called mindful chanting."
**Summary:** Mindful chanting occurs when our mind is present in the here and now, with attention focused on chanting, hearing, and counting.

Because attention is a, mind is a transporter of attention. So, I have to keep my attention here and now. My attention should not go to there and then. It should be here and now. Now, what is, that is a mindless chanting where your mind is not here and now. When your mind is here and now, then your chanting is called mindful chanting. The Lord is before me. So, I should receive him here. Right in front of me I can hear. So, that is a mindful chanting. Basically, mindful chanting means your attention is on the japa. Your attention is on chanting, on hearing, on counting. You know what you are doing. Consciously, you are doing these things. That is mindful japa.

#### Soulful Japa, Thought Feeling, Bhavana
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ir5igQ2KZLQ?t=2057
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Thought feeling behind every feeling, there is a thought."
**Summary:** Soulful japa involves cultivating sadbhavana, or eternal feelings, by training the mind with appropriate thoughts and feelings.

This is our very clear goal. And for this two things are important. We have to put effort to train the mind during japa. We have to put our effort of japa times to train our subconscious into all this thought feelings. As I said yesterday also, please understand this concept of thought feeling. Thought feeling behind every feeling, there is a thought. Feeling never stands alone. If you are angry, then there is an angry thought. If you are humble, there is a thought of humility. If you are proud, there is a thought of feeling of proud. There is a, there is a thought behind it. So it's called thought feeling. In Sanskrit, there is one word for this thought feeling. It's called bhavana. Bhava. What is a bhava? So that is the main thing that we need to understand that in soulful japa, there is something called sadbhavana. Sad means eternal bhavana. Eternal bhavana. Eternal feelings. Not, not material feelings. Eternal feelings. Eternal surrender, existential surrender. Not ordinary respect. Eternally, Krishna is eternally respectable. Krishna is eternally lovable. Krishna is eternally, we have to be eternally gratitude to, you are in an eternal space. So soulful, in mindful japa, you are here and now. But in soulful japa, you are here and now, but you know this here and now

And this Shakti comes from the Nama. So now we, earlier modules, you know, before we went into [the] training module. We had several, at least we had about 17 to 18 sessions. Where slowly we brought about the details of how this Nama Avatara means what. Just to refresh your memory. The Supreme Lord and his name are not different. The name incarnates Kalikale Nama Rupa Krishna Avatara. The Lord incarnates, Shaitanya Mahaprabhu says. He declares that the Nama has incarnated. And then we understood from the different statements that Shaitanya Mahaprabhu made. From different revelations from Prabhupada. Bhakti Vinod Thakur, Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Thakur. That the name on our tongue, which we chant now. That is a name that we generate. And into that sound, the absolute sound, name of the Lord, enters and empowers this sound. Just like how a copper wire is electrified in the same way. This Nama, which is on my tongue. Normally what my tongue produces is called Prakrita Shabda. So the Prakrita Nama Shabda that I utter becomes Shakti Avesha Nama Avatara. You know, potentially omnipotent sound. And that is, it is with that sound we are continuously associating with to experience our self-realization and God-realization. So we understood the concept of something called Shakti Avesha Nama. And then Aprakrita Nama. Aprakrita Nama means opposite of Prakrita. It's a transcendental sound. It is not a material sound. It's a transcendental sound.

**Summary:** The speaker clarifies the relationship between Aprakrita and Prakrita Nama, explaining sound incarnation as God descending in a sound body.

So, please don't get confused about this. Many get confused about this Aprakrita Nama and Prakrita Nama. Not to get confused, in few sessions, you will become very familiar. Initially, if something you cannot follow, you don't think, don't block your mind. You will very soon become familiar. Just like I will tell you an example. My soul in this body is Aprakrita. My body here is Prakrita. Right now, I am a combination of my Aprakrita Self and my Prakrita Self. And my Prakrita Self is temporary. My Aprakrita Self is eternal. My Aprakrita, my Prakrita body is taken by me for limited purpose of this life. I will take another, another, another, another. I have taken so many Prakrita bodies. But I am Aprakrita. Similarly, Shuddha Nama is Aprakrita. And he takes on so many Prakrita's body. When you chant Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, he takes on the body of that sound. In other words, sound incarnation means what? God has incarnated in a sound body. Exactly. Lord has descended in a sound body. Same Lord, who has incarnated into an external deity vigraha. Similarly, instead of the deity body, here he takes sound body. So we should feel that close. And the sound body, what does the Lord say? It's as good as me Baba. You please serve.

Today, you are only capable of serving a Prakrita body. You serve just like you offer a garland to the deities externally. I am receiving it. Let us have a relationship now itself before you go back to Godhead. When you come back to Godhead, you can, you can transcendently hear me. You can relish my transcendental form, everything. But now you have decided that you want to come out of this material world. So having decided to come out of this material world, having decided that you want me, I should accelerate your journey. Therefore, I am here as your incarnation. I am here. You may not be able to experience the sweetness of my name, but I am receiving the seva that you are doing through the Prakrita, through the Shakti Avesha Nama. I am receiving it. You don't worry. Just because you cannot see me, just like God says, you cannot see me. As soon as you serve me, I am taking that service. Don't worry. You are not qualified to see me, but I am taking that service. In the same way, Nama Avatara, the Shuddha Nama is receiving the service. Sound incarnation. Incarnation means what? Into gross material nature. Like we say, we undergo reincarnation. Now I am incarnated in this body. I am Aprakrita. My body is Prakrita. So the Aprakrita soul is incarnated into the Prakrita body. Different Prakrita body in different lives. Lord is... When I come into this body, I forget who I really am. And I think I am this body and I go into illusion. When Lord incarnates, He will never forget. He is who He is. Therefore He is called Achchuta. He is infallible. He never forgets that He is Aprakrita. He is transcendental. He is the source of everything.

So if you were there at that time, yes, original forms only. Prabhupada says, "Original form, but yet, to bear for my set of problems." Isn't Paratindaka smooth, Prince? någon with ni Srila Prabhupadaiklehta wohir.. My noble dear, soulful devotee. Welcome, kindly come. Yet tomorrow I will discuss about that, how even demons could see him, but the demons could not see his beauty. Kamsa was also seeing Krishna, devotees were also seeing Krishna, Yudhishthira was also seeing Krishna, Duryodhana was also seeing Krishna, we will discuss that tomorrow. Infinity, one type of infinity, another type of infinity, another type of infinity. This is spiritual world. Mathematics don't work. ## HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### Soulful Japa : Session 49 : Holy Name: Drink it through hearing
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### Nama Avatara, Deity, Aprakrita
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CLQ04bJVGQY?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This Nama is Krishna himself; there is no difference between the Lord and this Avatara."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains the three components of Avatara: Avatari, transcendental Avatara (Aprakrita), and empowered Avatara (Shakti Avesha), emphasizing the non-difference between Krishna and His Nama.

Welcome all of you for the 49th session of soulful japa. Now, we have discussed yesterday the chart where the three components of Avatara, both the DT as well as the Nama Prabhu, consisting of the Avatari, then transcendental Avatara, then empowered Avatara. Transcendental Avatara is Aprakrita form and Aprakrita Shabda in the case of Nama Avatara. In the case of DT, it is Aprakrita Rupa. It simply means that Rupa is Krishna himself, and this Nama is Krishna himself. There is no difference between the Lord and this Avatara. Only for a very specific purpose, He expands as the Avatra and takes on a form which is resembling the external Vigraha. Here, takes on a sound form that is resembling the external sound produced by my tongue. Sound produced by my tongue becomes the Shakti Avesha Nama. In the case of Deity, the external Vigraha becomes the empowered Shakti Avesha Rupa.

#### Aprakrita, Shakti Avesha, Attention
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CLQ04bJVGQY?t=134
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When I give attention to Shakti Avesha Nama, automatically my attention is touching the transcendental Lord also."
**Summary:** The speaker differentiates between Aprakrita and Shakti Avesha, explaining that the latter is material energy spiritualized, and emphasizes the importance of attention in connecting with the transcendental nature of the Lord through the external forms of the Deity and the Holy Name.

Now, the difference between the Aprakrita—some people have asked what is the difference between the second and the third one—second is a spiritual substance. Third is not a spiritual substance; it is material energy spiritualized. That is the difference. Okay. So there is a difference between these two things. Substance difference is there, though the features are identical. The features of the external Vigraha, Deity Vigraha, and the features of the Aprakrita Vigraha are identical. And we understood that why the Lord takes on a form that is resembling this, because ultimately, He wants to give a chance for our attention to touch His Aprakrita nature. And right now, we are stuck with Prakrita sound. Therefore, we hear and chant the Prakrita sound with attention. And the Lord has features identical to it. When I give attention to Shakti Avesha Nama, automatically my attention is touching the... when I give attention, my same attention is touching the, embracing the Shakti Avesha, the transcendental Lord also. Okay. Just like when you see the Deity, you need attention. When you give attention to the external Vigraha, attention, if there is an identical form of the Lord there, which is transcendental, which my eyes cannot see, but my attention can touch because attention is spiritual. So we are touching the transcendental. We are touching the Shakti Avesha Nama as well as we are touching with, as far as attention is concerned. Sensually, we are touching only the external Vigraha.

Similarly, when you feed our Atma with the Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, how do you feed here? Here you have to drink through hearing. In the case of Prasadam, you have to eat through the mouth. In the case of Hari Nama, you have to drink through the ears. You have to drink through hearing attention, knowing attention. You hear the eating. So just like food goes inside, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, Shakti Abhashanama goes inside. The sense, the Satchitananda energies, the spiritual energy which is transmitted through the Shakti Abhashanama, keeps going in through the attention into our soul. That is how we become red hot, we become Satchitanandaised, soul becomes godly. Just by, just like eating the body grows and gets strength and everything, as simple as that yesterday we discussed, forget about the mind and all. We simply have to drink the Holy Name, hearing, by hearing is equal to drinking. So where is required, when I give, when you give me some drink, I don't need mind to drink, I will drink, I will enjoy the taste, I will drink, similarly eating also, I don't need mind. Food is there, I will eat. As simple as that, feeding the soul in Kali Yuga is as simple as that.

**Summary:** The speaker shares a quote from Srila Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Thakur about seeing the deity. He explains that the first thought should be that the deity is seeing us, not the other way around. The deity sees the pure soul, free from ignorance, along with its material covering.

He says, I see the statue. This kind of thinking is in the spirit of enjoyment, whereas the deity sees me. My uncovered pure self is deity darshana, dhrit dhrishya vichara, dhrit dhrishya vichara. That means when you stand before the deities, you should have the vichara. What is that vichara? The vichara that the deity is seeing me. Over, close the port. So deity is seeing me. Got it? When we go and stand before the deity, first thought is not you see the deity. First is a vichara in your mind, background that when I go before the Lord, deity will see me. How will He see me? He doesn't see me like how other people see me or even how I see myself in the mirror. Because He is free from all ignorance. So He sees the pure uncovered soul along with his covering.

**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CLQ04bJVGQY?t=3416
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

He emphasizes that just as it would be abnormal to think only the body has come without the mind, it is a partial understanding of the truth to think that only the external sound vibration is present without God Himself.

Suppose I come to your house and then you call me for a feast and then you offer me the plate and everything. Suppose if you are thinking that only my body has come, something about his mind, what he thinks about me and what he is expecting me. That's all. It's as simple as that. Okay. So, I come to your house and then, you know, you call me for a feast and then you offer me the plate and everything and suppose if you are thinking that only my body has come, no mind has come, hmm, it's abnormal. Similarly, if I think only body is there, this external sound vibration, but God Himself has not come. Okay. Or only Shakti Avesha Nama is there. It's a partial understanding of truth. Just like, you know, you think a body has come and sat and you are giving different items on the plate and I am eating and going. No. It appears very stupid to you, isn't it? Similarly, if you think we are mechanically, we are offering something to the Lord, then it's a very stupid thing. Apply, move it to God. Only all this is happening because we don't see God moving. We don't see the deity moving, etc. That is the whole problem.

#### Japa, Devotion, Atma Cheshta
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CLQ04bJVGQY?t=3653

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

And that exactly. My role is what Krishna wants. Krishna wants that. And what is that role? You need to understand that what ultimately is happening during Japa, devotionally what is happening, your tongue is actually automatically moving. Some people ask this question, chesta, new devotee, new person has to give chesta by remembering the tongue and moving the tongue distinctly and all. But already the mind has learnt all those things, mind has learnt all those things and what are you doing, your activity of devotion service is going on in your heart. What is that? You are at the starting point of all this chesta. First Atma chesta is there. Then effortlessly the subtle body and gross body is chanting. Gross body and subtle body is automatically following. But Atma chesta, that's what. Do you, after having chanting become automatic, that means after having become trained, do you still like the name of the Lord to be called out? That is real Japa.

#### Aprakrita Nama, Shakti Avesha Nama, Attention
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CLQ04bJVGQY?t=3838
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even in a grossly uttered name or the name in your mind...it becomes Shakti Avesha Nama."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that even a name uttered grossly or in the mind becomes Shakti Avesha Nama. He explains that the difficulty lies in maintaining attention and connection at the mental level alone in Kali Yuga, which is why Chaitanya Mahaprabhu recommended chanting loudly.

Will Aprakrita Nama convert the sound into Shakti Avesha Nama if one is chanting in his mind or in an audible or only an audible sound become Shakti Avesha? No, no, no, no, no. If even, even in a grossly uttered name or the name in your mind, you utter the name in your mind, it becomes Shakti Avesha Nama. Subtle body or gross body? Don't worry. It becomes. But the only problem is to do, to establish and maintain the attention connection and the attention embrace at the mental level alone is very difficult in Kali Yuga. Therefore Chaitanya Mahaprabhu recommended that you chant loudly. It is easy to lock the attention. Okay, that's all. If you are not, if you are capable of that much concentration, no problem. In your mind itself, you can chant. But same thing what you are doing grossly, it should happen. Atma, chasta should still be the same, whether it, Atma, chasta ends at the subtle body or it ends including the gross body. Gross body is chanting, subtle body is chanting, Atma chesta, Atma is chanting.

#### Relationship Faith, Mercy, Kripa Apeksha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CLQ04bJVGQY?t=4167
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Chant with Kripa Apeksha."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the concept of relationship faith, which is the faith that Krishna will always protect us. He distinguishes it from existence faith and emphasizes that having faith that Krishna will give us mercy is crucial. He encourages chanting with Kripa Apeksha, a feeling of wanting everything from Him and complete dependence upon Him.

It's not about whether Krishna exists or not. That is existence faith. There is a relationship faith. And it had been written from the Guru. It's an exasperating application of Krishna. No. existence faith. Whether Krishna is on my tongue or not, I doubt. No, no, I have faith. That means it is about His existence. Okay, having come into existence on my tongue, having descended on my tongue, can I have faith that He will give me mercy? Sure, you should have faith. That is called relationship faith. #### Deity, Japa, Worship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CLQ04bJVGQY?t=4431
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The preference is easy access to the nama worship."
**Summary:** The speaker answers a question about sitting before the altar and doing Japa. He says it is perfectly okay, but one should hear and chant, connecting the name with the deity. He cautions against focusing on the deity's beauty, as that is another kind of worship (archana). He explains that in our impure state, nama worship is preferred, and deity worship comes after a certain level of purification.

In a sense, I will give you the short answer. You are saying that, you are asking the question that, can I sit before the altar have darshan of the deity of the Lord and then do Japa. Perfectly okay, do it. But that time, you should hear and chant. And if you are able to connect that name is name of the deity, who is whom you are seeing, it is very good. But seeing the deity, you should not focus on the deity, rupa in the sense that appreciating the beauty, all those things. Then that is another kind of worship. That is archana. As I explained in the earlier module, that the preference is, in our impure state, the preference is easy accesses to the nama worship. And after nama worship, when we get purified, then we are in a better position to get access to the transcendental deity of the Lord. So that is where deity worship comes after a certain level of purification.

So therefore we have understood that through these three analogies. One is the electrical wire, second is a currency, third is like the post box analogy. Each analogy has its own limitations. But it really gives us an idea of the action that is happening when the Lord comes down and what is the relationship between the material energy and the Lord. The external sound that you hear. The sound that you produce is not ordinary because that sound becomes the body of the Avatara. The soul of that is the Lord himself and that becomes the body of the Lord. Just like now in this world I am having a body and I am having a soul, right? My soul has taken a body. If you serve my body automatically I am living in this world. I the soul is living in this world through the body. So I am the enjoyer and sufferer of everything even though body is there. So in the same way Nama Avatara, Shakti Avaisha Nama Avatara, the omnipotent sound, the soul of the omnipotent sound is the transcendental sound which is descending from the spiritual world.

#### Prakrta Sound, Aprakrta Sound, Vedic Mantras
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IfbdyUpP9C4?t=1437
**Verse References:** SB 2.4.22
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "There is a gulf of difference between the two qualities of sound, namely prakrta and aprakrta."
**Summary:** The speaker reads a quote from Prabhupada differentiating between prakrta (material) and aprakrta (spiritual) sound, emphasizing that Vedic mantras can only be understood through inspiration from the supernatural sound descending through disciplic succession.

Now let us read one quote of Prabhupada which tells the difference between ordinary sound of this world and transcendental sound. This is from Purpat Srimad Bhagavatam 2.4.22. There is a gulf of difference between the two qualities of sound, namely prakrta and aprakrta. The physicist can deal only with the prakrta sound. Prakrta. Prakrta means the sound which is belonging to prakriti or the sound vibrated in the spiritual sky. Prakrta sound is the sound vibrated in the material sky. And therefore we must know that the Vedic sounds recorded in symbolic expressions cannot be understood by anyone within the universe unless and until one is inspired by the Vedic sounds. These sounds are the sound which is inspired by the vibration of supernatural, namely APRAKRTA sound which descends in the chain of disciplic succession from the Lord to Brahma, Brahma to Narada, Narada to Vyasa and so on. No mundane scholar can translate or reveal the true import of the Vedic mantras.

#### Mantra vs. Nama, Diksha, Spiritual Master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IfbdyUpP9C4?t=2384
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Nama is God himself. Mantra has got lot of restrictions of how to chant, where to chant. Therefore guru is a must."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to differentiate between mantra and nama, stating that nama is God himself and has fewer restrictions, while mantra requires a guru, and explains that while anyone can chant nama, a spiritual master is needed for deeper reciprocation.

First please understand that there is a difference between mantra and nama. Even though we, for the normal worldly sense, we say Hare Krishna mantra. That's why we say Maha Mantra. Maha Mantra, nama is different, mantra is different. Nama is God himself. Mantra has got lot of restrictions of how to chant, where to chant. Therefore guru is a must. But as Chaitanya Mahaprabhu says, Krishna is incarnated in this holy name in Kali Yuga and therefore this nama is different, mantra is different. So nama is different. That's why anybody can chant and in fact Prabhupada says in the Chaitanya Charitamrita that one does not even need diksha. But these are all very technical things. We need to have formally received from a spiritual master. Up to certain level, the nama will reciprocate if you don't take diksha. That's why we are all getting effect of Hare Krishna before even taking initiation. But a reciprocation beyond a certain point. You need to have a relationship with the person who is a worshipper of the holy name. That is a spiritual master. He is a nama sadhaka.

#### Nama Diksha, Relationship, Krishna Prema
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IfbdyUpP9C4?t=2465
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker explains that Nama Diksha is transcendental, based on faith, and involves receiving the nama from a spiritual master, which is necessary to progress to Krishna Prema.

From him you have to receive, transcendentally, mystically, not a physical process. Just like we sit for initiation, Prabhupada doesn't come and give that mantra in your ears. Even when he was on this planet, he didn't tell the mantra in your ears. So it is not a mantra diksha. It's a nama diksha. It's a mantra diksha. It's a mantra diksha. It's a mantra diksha. Nama diksha is transcendentally, by faith, guru has, shishya has faith, guru has, has, gives the blessings and he says, okay, here, take the nama that I have. That means his nama. That means he has a personal relationship with the nama. Yes, take this. I initiate you. Now you do sadhana. Now you worship the nama. So that is why then you can go further ahead. Because relationship. Relationship means up to one point, it's not up to one point, relation. So relation goes on up to Krishna Prema. Without initiation, you cannot go up to Krishna Prema. Without initiation, by chanting, you will get some detachment. You will get some transcendental knowledge in terms of differentiating between matter and spirit, all these things. But actual bhakti you start getting only when you surrender to a spiritual master, take nama from him and worship the nama.

### Soulful Japa : Session 7 : The principle of Nāma Avatāra - Module 7
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### purification, spiritualization, Nama Prabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HOxdLzjh_ik?t=30
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "if we place our soul in the fire of Satchitananda of surrounding the Nama Prabhu, our Chitta also becomes Satchitanandized and it becomes purified."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how placing our soul in the fire of Satchitananda surrounding Nama Prabhu purifies our Chitta.

Yesterday we read from Jai Shila Prabhupada the very powerful analogy of a piece of iron placed inside a fire. It becomes warmer, warmer, and red hot and acts like fire. In the same way, if we place our soul in the fire of Satchitananda surrounding the Nama Prabhu, our Chitta also becomes Satchitanandized.

---
#### spiritualization, Krishna consciousness, chanting
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HOxdLzjh_ik?t=81
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Similarly, by this Krishna consciousness, continuous chanting, you will make your body spiritualized."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of an iron rod in fire to explain how continuous chanting in Krishna consciousness spiritualizes the body.

Now one more quote, let us read today, an extension of the same concept. But here Prabhupada says in this quote that our body becomes spiritualized. Not only Chitta, even our body becomes spiritualized. I'll read the quote. This is a lecture on Srimad Bhagavatam, New York, 1969, April 10. The same example as I have given several times, just like an iron rod. You put it into the fire, it becomes warmer, warmer. The more it is touched with fire, it becomes warm, warm, warm. And at last it becomes red hot. So that, so that at that time, if that iron is touched to any other thing, it burns. It does not act as iron, it acts as fire. Similarly, by this Krishna consciousness, continuous chanting, you will make your body spiritualized.

#### Satchitananda, Nama Prabhu, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HOxdLzjh_ik?t=158
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That means that effulgent rays of Satchitananda, which descends, surrounding the Nama Prabhu."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the effulgent rays of Satchitananda surrounding Nama Prabhu and how they cleanse the heart and body.

Similarly, the iron, without being spiritualized, without being red hot, if you touch, it will not act. So it's a very interesting quote. That means that effulgent rays of Satchitananda, which descends, surrounding the Nama Prabhu. When Nama Prabhu descends, these personal energies are also surrounding. They are not impersonal energies like sunlight. They are also personal energies: Satya energy, Chitta energy, Ananda energy. They are personalities. Everything in the spiritual world is personality. And this Satchitananda energy makes the external sound vibration into an empowered Shakti Avaishana, or an omnipotent sound. That sound enters through the mind, into our heart. And now this quote says, not only your heart gets cleansed, all the urges, the sensual ideas of sensual pleasures, all these things, vanish when the body is spiritualized.

#### Krishna's name, purification, spiritual senses
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HOxdLzjh_ik?t=260
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We cannot hear Krishna's name with material ears... But if we engage our tongue in his service, he will reveal himself saying, here I am."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that Krishna's name cannot be grasped by material senses but is revealed through devotional service, leading to the awakening of spiritual senses.

Here Prabhupada in this quote, he says that we cannot hear Krishna with material ears. We cannot hear Krishna's name with material ears, just like we cannot see Krishna with our material eyes. We cannot see Krishna with our material eyes, nor hear about him with material ears, nor touch him with our hands. But if we engage our tongue in his service, he will reveal himself saying, here I am. So this famous quote is always, you know, very, very commonly used by Prabhupada, Atashi, Krishna, Namadhi, Bhaved, Grahyam, Indriye. By Indriyas, we cannot grasp or we cannot capture Krishna by our material Indriyas. And this is precisely the goal of Japa. Ultimately the goal of Japa is by association, we purify our material mind, material senses. And following that, our spiritual mind awakens, our spiritual senses awaken.

---
#### Aprakritanama, Shaktiavaishanama, incarnation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HOxdLzjh_ik?t=352
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Aprakrita means it is transcendental name... So there is the aprakritanama and there is shaktiavaishanama."
**Summary:** The speaker differentiates between Aprakritanama (transcendental name) and Shaktiavaishanama, explaining how the Lord acts in this world through the latter.

Aprakritanama and prakritana. Prakritanama. Aprakrita means it is transcendental name. And there is actually nothing like prakritanama because as soon as there is a nama of this material vibration of belonging to matter, it immediately gets empowered by the descendants of the Lord into it. So actually, shaktiavaishanama, every nama is shaktiavaishanamaya. Because Krishna has decided that kalikale namo rupe krishnavatara he has decided. So there is the aprakritanama and there is shaktiavaishanama. Yesterday we also discussed how this aprakritanama is the soul of the shaktiavaishanama. So it is like, what I desire, what I want I express through my body. Similarly, because in this material world if I have to speak to you, I have to speak through my body because you can see only my body. So in the same way, avatara means Lord has incarnated. So this shaktiavaishanama, the Lord acts in this world through his shaktiavaishanama. #### japa quality, mindless japa, mindful japa, concurrent chanting, chesta shakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-GhND-zjUwg?t=117
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Chanting and hearing that very chant makes it very easy to lock the attention is the first secret of locking the attention, first secret of begging for the chesta shakti from the Lord himself, from the nama himself."
**Summary:** The speaker reviews the four stages of japa quality: mindless, mindful, heartful, and soulful, explaining mindful japa as concurrent chanting and hearing, attracting chesta shakti from Nama Prabhu.

So if you recall, we categorized the progress of our japa, quality of our japa. Stage one is mindless japa. Stage two is mindful japa. Stage three is heartful japa. Stage four is soulful japa. Mindless japa, I just touch upon each of them also without going into too many details. Please recall, mindless japa means what? Mind is not on your japa. You're doing auto japa. That means your physical body is doing japa and your, you know, trained mind is tracking how many beats, all those things, and in this way it is in auto mode. So this is called mindless japa. What is the problem with mindless japa? You are not giving your soul. Your soul is somewhere else, but Krishna wants your soul, wants your attention. Where your attention is, there the soul is. Therefore attention is very much linked to good japa. Now what is mindful japa? Mindful japa is when our attention is locked to chanting and hearing that very chant. And please get familiarized with all these words. We use a word concurrent chanting. That means you should, when you chant and you hear that very chant concurrent, and automatically that will attract chesta shakti from Nama Prabhu himself. So in concurrent chanting, chanting is a doing effort, hearing is a knowing effort. Chanting is called doing effort, is kriya chesta, and knowing effort is called jnana chesta. So kriya chesta, when you want to do something, then it generates doing attention because without attention on something you cannot do. And similarly, hearing is one of the five input knowing activities, so hearing it comes under knowing the sound, so for that you need to have attention to know Hare Krishna Rama. So you need attention. That knowing attention is released when there is knowing effort. That means we have to offer our kriya chesta and jnana chesta on every manifesting nama, on every manifesting nama, not mala, every manifesting nama. Okay, these are some of the secrets of attentive japa, not only chanting, not only hearing, chanting and hearing that very chant makes it very easy to lock the attention is the first secret of locking the attention, first secret of begging for the chesta shakti from the Lord himself, from the nama himself. When you attract the chesta shakti from the Lord, what happens? You like doing and knowing. You learn the sound. You like chanting and hearing, chanting and hearing. You want to repeat again and again. You like, like, like keep doing, keep doing something that gives you energy. You like to do, keep doing, is it not? Then we talked about tasking.

#### tasking, repetition, stacking, scattering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-GhND-zjUwg?t=396
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Japan is not chanting come hearing alone. Japan means chanting come hearing repeated. What is a vidhi which is a way it has to be repeated? If there's a qualifying qualifying factor there for repeating what is that uninterrupted."
**Summary:** The lecture emphasizes that japa is not just chanting and hearing, but also uninterrupted tasking, which is repetition. He contrasts scattered tasking with stacking, explaining that stacking builds up the effect of purification of the heart.

Tasking is a very important part of Japan. Japan does not mean chanting and hearing only. That is a misconception we carry in our head because we think Japan is chanting and hearing. We take special care for chanting and hearing and we neglect something more important, very important, equally important. I would not say more, equally important is uninterrupted tasking. And tasking means what? Tasking means repetition, repetition of chanting come here in channel, chanting come hearing. So Japan is not chanting come hearing alone. Japan means chanting come hearing repeated. What is a vidhi which is a way it has to be repeated? If there's a qualifying qualifying factor there for repeating what is that uninterrupted. And what happens in stacking in in in in tasking there are two ways of asking. One is okay I had to finish my 16th round somehow the whole day you finish or you chant about you know half a mala or something else comes you keep it then again start other half mala like this kind of things so that is the first scattered bricks you see every now and then you chant and they are like placing many bricks spreading the bricks on the table and you see what is the effect of the second drawing proper tasking is stacking. Stacking means one above the other. In this what happens if you see the weight on the table the force on the table below each brick on the left side is one brick weight whereas suppose your table is the table is your heart and here in this table you see it is piling up what is the weight sum total of all this similarly in tasking as you go one round two round three round four round it builds up the effect of purification of the heart is built up it is not scattered chanting so it is tasking by stacking not tasking by scattering.

**Key Quote:** "Bead after bead you have to recall your desire. That's what we said desire hope."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that heartful, mindful, and soulful japa share a common driving force: intention, desire, and purpose, emphasizing the importance of recalling desire with each bead.

Now let us come to whether it is soulful japa heartful japa or mindful japa all three have a common driving force driving force means what do you want to achieve you want to achieve out of this action of chanting and hearing these what do you want to achieve in terms of action it is called intention in terms of immediately what you want to please Krishna that is called desire and there is a bigger purpose why you are even associating with Nama why you are even doing japa there's a bigger purpose that's called purpose that's like we said purpose of life I may be taking prasadam now but what is a purpose of life you don't say taking prasadam of the purpose of life is to become Krishna conscious taking prasadam is my desire through various desires I achieve my purpose then not similarly you feel that it's a new thought is it bead after bead there is a desire Whereas purpose is for the entire japa. You don't have to recall your purpose for a japa bead after bead But bead after bead you have to recall your desire. That's what we said desire hope Now.

Om Agyanati Murandhasya Gyananjana Shalakya Chakshurun Militam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Namah Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Prishtaya Bhutale Srimati Bhaktivedanta Swami Nithilamane. Just like one sun in the sky shines sunlight into so many water bodies and forms the reflection of that sun. The same way one ever effulgent, eternally sounding Nama of this Supreme Lord in the spiritual world takes avatara first by shining all his shaktis into the sun.

That is our present consciousness, each of our consciousness. What is temporary, we consider as everlasting, sat, asat. Everywhere we are seeing, things are changing, eternally changing, our body is changing, yet we think that, we never think that the body dies. So asat, we consider as sat, I am the body. And so much of achit, we have forgotten we are eternal, we have eternal relationship with Krishna. Achit means, I am the body and bodily relationships are mine. The bodily world is real world. The world of bodily egos are real world. This is achit.

Lies, we are living in lies. Nirananda, jadananda. Nirananda means no ananda, but we think it is ananda. All the material sense pleasures, so called material love and intimacy in this world, we think it is true. Only love, intimacy and pleasure in association with Krishna is satya, is truth. Others is nirananda. We have got, our heart is full of false pleasure beliefs, that this will give me pleasure, that will give me happiness, this will give me… This is called nirananda.

Heart is contaminated. Now we are contaminated. We take pleasure in seeing material beauties, which are maya or just momentary appearances. Does not last. So, our heart is full of asat, achit and nirananda, thinking that to be satya, chitta and ananda. Because the soul is never satisfied with asat, achit and nirananda. The soul's original essence is satya, chitta and ananda.

When our heart is cleansed, as I said, Lord is absolute. There is no difference between His reflection and Him. There is no deflection, there is no distance between Him and His name. He will immediately manifest on our tongue the shuddha nama, provided our heart becomes pure. When our heart becomes pure, then our body and senses also become pure. They become spiritualized. This is the goal of chanting Hare Krishna Maha Mantra. Therefore, very effectively if you want, you have to take, you want the effect of nama avatara, you want to experience our original satya, chitta and ananda, then we have to see that the holy name, one after the other, goes into our heart. So we chant, and the Lord reciprocates by entering our heart, as iron put in the fire becomes red hot, the Lord, to the extent to which we are ready to give up satya, chitta and ananda, immediately He is ready to replace it with satya, chitta and ananda.

#### Shraddha, Prithi, Feelings
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9Mb2l1YFPX0?t=1674
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This sound is Krishna himself, that feeling, this sound is infinite sound, this sound is infinite consciousness, all this feeling is called shraddha."

When Krishna has come before me, everything should be given to him.
