Okay. So anyway, the time it takes to completely change the old habit is about 90 days. NASA had done some experiment with astronauts because when they go into the space, you know, the gravity, all those, they have to condition them. They studied how the mind adapts to the new environment and new situations. So what they did was they gave goggles to the astronauts. Okay. The convex goggles which will make each image upside down. And they had to wear it even during sleep, continuously they had to wear it. So what was happening? They were seeing the world upside down. And naturally the mind has to reinterpret the image in order to see the world as it should be seen. So it was continuously doing this. Mind was engaged in this. And 29th day, he removed the goggles. Again, you know, the habit had not changed. You are directly seeing. Normal. Normal world you are seeing. But then after 30 days and the goggles were removed, you are reversing the whole world upside down, the other way around. Because it has become a habit now. another example for you to understand the difference between doing effort and knowing effort or doing attention and knowing attention. Both are same. Doing effort releases knowing attention. Can you use a spanner to remove the screw? You cannot. Can you use a screwdriver to remove nut and bolt? You cannot. Doing effort is for chanting, knowing effort is for hearing. And if I try to use my, you know, doing effort to hear, what is it? Frustration, isn't it? So remember these two examples. And suppose now I give you. Another thing, another solution, another scenario. Suppose I have fixed two pieces together with a screw and a nut in the other end. It's tightened. Now, if I had to remove, I need both screwdriver on this side and spanner on the other side. Combined, I can loosen it. So there is a place where both are required. So in the same way, Japa requires both doing effort and knowing effort simultaneously applied on each sound. And the reason why we get so frustrated and our mind goes off and the attention goes away, keeps going away because we are using the screwdriver to remove a bolt. We are using doing effort to hear. You will never hear. You'll never hear by trying to hear. By trying to do doing effort, by trying to do hearing, you cannot do hearing, you have to know hearing because it is a jnana indriya, it's very, very, very fundamental to this discovery. Now, focusing on one instruction, somebody tells you, no, no, you should only hear. The problem with your chanting is you're not hearing, they'll say. The next day you're determined. You're giving only doing effort. Again, you fail. So we should not over-emphasize. We should understand that it is a linked up activity, chanting and knowing that chant. Doing effort to chant, knowing effort to do so that when done together, there is no mental fatigue. There is no self-control required. Otherwise, continuously self-control to bring the attention back, attention back becomes such a big drain of mental energy.

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the concepts of Ekathva (concentration on one doing) and Anutthva (sharpness of knowing) as qualities of doing and knowing attention, respectively.

So you have to be patient. So now before I leave you all, I'll give you a new concept which is called the mind has got two qualities. While I was taking this japa seminar, one Ayurvedic student told me that in their Ayurvedic college, they are taught from the Vedas. And the quality of knowing attention is called Anutthva. Now Ekathva means doing only one thing, not two things. Doing only one thing. That means I decide now I will chant. You can only do one thing at a time, no doubt. But while chanting, doing something else, that means now my only doing is chanting, chanting, chanting, chanting, chanting, nothing else, only doing. So there is a concentration on a particular doing, well-defined doing. And I will not do anything else at that time. So this is called Ekathva. Ekathva is a quality of our doing attention. Then there is Anutthva. Anutthva is a quality of our knowing attention. What is the quality of knowing attention? Quality of knowing attention. Anutthva is a quality of knowing attention. Anutthva is our sharpness or resolution. Just like you see, you see high resolution, low resolution of knowing is there. When there is low resolution picture, you will see granularity very clearly. High resolution, you see a continuous picture. So this kind of granularity of details of knowing, that is called Anutthva. That is why yesterday somebody asked, is this knowing just like being aware? No, no, no, no, aware of details. That's why I said, I use the word sharpness of knowing is called Anutthva. Distinctly chanting Ekathva, Prabhupada says, distinctly chant and hear, where he means actually distinctly chant and distinctly hear. But I, that for the sake of, for the hearing, I use that word instead of distinctly, sharp, sharply hear. Otherwise, people will think that distinctly is only for chanting, for hearing there is no adjective. No, there is an adjective sharp hearing, distinctly chant and sharply hear. Sharply hear is a focus of knowing attention or a quality of the knowing attention and the doing and Ekathva is a quality of doing. Okay. Doing attention.

#### chanting and hearing, reprogramming the mind, linked chanting

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains the importance of linking chanting and hearing, emphasizing that hearing is knowing and chanting is doing, and shares his personal experience of reprogramming his mind to overcome the perception of hearing as a separate activity.

When you are fresh, let me give you the main concept for the day. All that we are going to talk about in these next few days are different kinds of knowledge that will strengthen our background linking direction. That means in the background we have to have the direction to not chant and hear separately, but chant and hear the same word, and hearing is knowing chant. If most of you, because at least it was for me like that, hearing was also like a doing. So always I was putting doing effort to chant and to hear, and it was not going anywhere. So that is the reason why I keep telling that hearing is a knowing and chanting is a doing. I had to tell myself a hundred times to come out of that endeavor to hear as a doing. So finally I came out of it. So that is, after that, for quite some time I used to simply say chant and know the chant, chant and know the chant, chant and know the chant instead of hearing because my old programming is there, hearing when there were two things I had to do, chanting and hearing. So chant and know the chant, chant and know the chant. So in this way I reprogram my mind with, you know, replace my old program as two doings as only one doing and one knowing of that very doing as Srila Prabhupada says, chant and exactly the same word you hear, exactly the same word you hear.

First take the ball, one hand, then you throw to the other hand. The other hand should catch, then to the other hand, then to the other hand, then to the other hand, then to the other hand, like this, practice this. Now you see, there is doing and knowing. The very simple exercise. Doing and knowing. In this case, doing and knowing, after some time it gets so practiced that even without knowing you will do. But that's not what we want in japa. We want the knowing to be always there, inattentive, this can become inattentive doing. What is that inattentive doing? Got practiced. I can think of that, I can think of this, I can do so many things, already practiced thinking. So that means inattentive doing. Inattentive, auto doing, even doing I am not attentive, auto doing. What is attentive doing and knowing? Attentive doing is consciously throw, catch and know. If I can do without consciously, why should I, what is the need for me to be conscious? This is where it comes. Krishna wants you, not your hand and strength of your hand and throwing the ball, all that, no. Krishna wants you to chant Hare Krishna, not your simply your tongue and your automatic chanting. Krishna wants you to chant. Where are you? You are where your attention is. What is your soul energy? I told you that energy comes from the Nama Prabhu. This is the fact that people, devotees who have started practicing this are able to do 50 rounds, 25 rounds easily. So easily and, you know, increasing their rounds from what they were doing to more to reaching 16 rounds. In overnight, they are able to do it. Why? Because of the, I gave you the explanation because there is a taste induced in it. There is a taste that comes to a heart and soul by doing, knowing, doing and then tasking it. Knowing, doing and tasking. This, I may get fed up of this because this is a ball. There is no end to it. There is no energy coming from this ball for knowing and doing. I will get fed up of it. In the sense, it will be uninteresting for me. But the chanting and hearing, chanting and knowing, knowing that it is God's name, Lord's name, it gives energy and simultaneously, that simultaneous linking direction. When I am doing this, how did I learn so fast? Because the person instructed me, do this, like this. That went into my background consciousness and my background consciousness is directing my hands. There is an auto doing and there is a Kriyachesta where you are doing consciously. Somebody chants 16 rounds in one sitting. Starts with bead one. Goes on. Mala after mala. Sakshi mala also is moving. Sixteen rounds he has completed because for years together he has practiced and it can automatically do it. At the end he keeps his mala down. You ask him did you hear any mantra? Did you chant any mantra? He will say not. I did not. I did not give any chesta. I did not give any doing chesta. I did not give any knowing chesta. What are we trying to do here? We are training a new mind slice. For the purpose of mindful chanting, mindful japa. Right now, due to our old japa habits, we are having a mindless japa, thinking japa, we have been having. So we want to create a new mind slice. This is our effort. And how can a mind slice be created? It can be created by repeating what you want that mind slice to do. What we are doing in japa, even though one tongue only, one sense only is involved, tongue and mind is involved, ear is involved, all the senses are not involved, doesn't matter. ## HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### Soulful Japa: Session 35 :Secret is Linking: Training Mind to Link Chanting and Hearing-Module 38-53
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### chanting, hearing, linking
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T4V4gJZff7U?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The training is specifically for linking chanting and hearing."
**Summary:** The lecture emphasizes the importance of linking chanting and hearing in japa practice.

Welcome all of you for the 34th session of soulful japa. So today also, we shall discuss something to strengthen your practice module. We have had several key points with respect to practice.

---
#### attention, desire, linking
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T4V4gJZff7U?t=60
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You to desire to chant consciously to hear consciously in spite of chanting properly instead of having inspire having desire to chant consciously and in spite of having desired to hear consciously for some reason the attention does not stay there so."
**Summary:** The speaker identifies the problem of attention wavering during chanting, even with a desire to chant and hear consciously.

And in spite of that, I see that some devotees are not able to catch the secret. If you don't catch the secret, only then you can fail; otherwise, in this, there is no failure. The mistakes, what can happen, mistakes I will tell you. The training is specifically for linking chanting and hearing. Please understand that training is not meant for total chanting to be trained. The linking portion we want to train. Giving chesta off from the soul is a voluntary thing. You have to give, you cannot train that. You desire to chant consciously, to hear consciously. In spite of chanting properly, instead of having, having a desire to chant consciously, and in spite of having desired to hear consciously, for some reason, the attention does not stay there. So this is the problem. Very specific problem. By training, we are not going to replace, you know, automatically, you know, something from the soul will be pulled out by the mind. Kriyayachesta will be pulled out from the mind. Kriyayachesta will be... No, it doesn't have to pull out.

#### mindful japa, linking, effort
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T4V4gJZff7U?t=150
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Chant and hear that very chant."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that mindful japa involves conscious effort to link chanting and hearing the same sound.

Kriyayachesta and all, doesn't have to pull out from the mind, from the heart. Without pulling out, once you teach it, it will chant and, you know, automatically it will chant and do auto-tracking. Beads will move. So mindful japa means, understand once again, mindful means consciously you are giving, you are doing effort. And in a linked way, you are giving your knowing effort. Offering your doing effort is not what we are training. Offering your knowing effort is not what we are training. It means the training part. What we want the mind to learn is to link these two things. And that's exactly what the golden quote says. Chant and hear exactly the same chant you hear. So that's why, exactly the very same word that you hear. You are not practicing chant. You are not practicing, if you take that golden quote and say, okay, Prabhupada is saying I should chant and I should hear. You got it wrong. The key, secret in that golden quote is, chant and hear that very chant. Chant and hear that very sound. While you chant, you hear. That means that same thing you should hear.

#### linking, knowing, doing
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T4V4gJZff7U?t=270
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Is a linking."

## HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### Soulful Japa: Session 38 : Key Points Analyzed : 37 -53 Modules - Module 54
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### Nama Avatara, Satchitananda, Transcendental Sound
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/PaJLXrn_7dk?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Supreme Lord has descended in the form of his name, especially Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Hare, the Aprakrita Nama or the transcendental name of the Lord, Shuddha Nama in the spiritual world most mercifully enters the sound that we chant with our tongue and converts that so-called material sound into an omnipotent sound, very important to understand this."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the purpose of the session is to summarize key points about the training, focusing on the idea of Kriyachesta and Jnana Chesta, and that the Lord has incarnated as the omnipotent sound, Satchitananda, on our tongue.

Jai Shila Prabhupada, Hare Krishna Namo Prabhu Ki Jai, welcome all of you, sorry for the delay, there was some technical hitch and, you know, I hope it won't break down in between. So, on request of some devotees, they wanted me to summarize all the important points that are there from module 37 till now, till 55, in connection with the training session. So, let us begin. I think that was a very good suggestion and it will give an opportunity for all of you to revise that and recall one by one because if you read all those modules, key points, ultimately many of those things are explanation to bring out, you know, the idea of Kriyachesta, Jnana Chesta, concurrent application of Kriyachesta and Jnana Chesta, so these kind of things, this is the whole, you know, purpose of this now summarizing. Before that, okay, let me see, let me start. The whole idea, the purpose is association with the Nama Avatara. The Supreme Lord has descended in the form of his name, especially Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Hare Hare, the Aprakrita Nama or the transcendental name of the Lord, Shuddha Nama in the spiritual world, most mercifully enters the sound that we chant with our tongue and converts that so-called material sound into an omnipotent sound, very important to understand this. So the Lord has incarnated as the omnipotent sound and our entire goal is what? Association with the Lord and the Lord has incarnated means Lord is Satchitananda and he's a Surya of Satchitananda. He's on our tongue, but we cannot see because Satchitananda is transcendental. It's beyond this dimension.

So, so that starting point was that, and we said that unless the soul is attentive to the Nama on who is manifest before me, unless you are attentive, there is no question of connection with the Satchitananda Nama, the Surya of Satchitananda, when the Lord says, I've empowered the Satchitananda. When the Lord says, I've empowered the Satchitananda. When the Lord says, I've empowered the Satchitananda. Nam Nama Kari Boudha Nija Sarva Shakti. That means this vibration is empowered. Means what is that power? That power is nothing but Satchitananda energies. That sound is Satchitananda-ized sound. It's not ordinary sound. Just like when electricity goes through the wire, it becomes electrified copper wire. Similarly, the sound that I chant is not ordinary sound. It is electrified or it is Satchitananda-ized sound. And when I give attention to the Satchitananda sound, it is Satchitananda-ized sound. It is Satchitananda-ized sound. It is Satchitananda-ized sound. to that Satchitananda sound, then there is a flow of Satchitananda Kripa from the Namavatara into our soul. And that is how when we are attentive to the chanting, when we give serving attention to chant and hear the holy name simultaneously, concurrently, there is a flow of spiritual energy and that spiritual energy makes us wanting to chant more and more and more and more. Okay, so with that our full entire goal is what? To establish that attention connection and consequence of attention connection is attention touch of the Lord. Okay, attention touch is maintaining that touch. Attention touch means hearing. Okay, you chant, chanting like welcoming the Lord into your sound. He comes into that sound and then you hear that sound, you take him inside. You generate your sound, the Lord gets in, comes in, descends, empowers it. Now the empowered sound goes inside. Through the mind it reaches the heart and there is transformation because just like the iron put in the fire becomes red hot, the same way when the soul is put in the fire of the Hari Nama, Satchitananda Nama, the fire of the Lord, we become Satchidanandaized, we become awakened, everything, Satchitananda.

#### Attention, Chesta, Kriya Chesta, Jnana Chesta

**Summary:** The speaker explains that to give attention, one must give effort (Chesta), specifically Kriya Chesta (doing effort) and Jnana Chesta (knowing effort), and that the control point is Chesta in the heart, not just trying to hold attention.

Now, so now the next thing you have to understand is we have to give attention. But how to give attention? To give attention, you have to give effort. What doing effort gives doing attention to Karma Indriyas and knowing effort releases knowing attention to Jnana Indriyas. In other words, the idea of saying that is ultimately we want to be attentive to the Japa. We want to be attentive to the Nama. To be attentive to the Nama, the control point is not attention. Don't try to hold the attention, bring back and do all that. Is not the attention. The point is the Chesta in the heart. That is what releases the attention. So at the control point, control point is Chesta and not the attention. So therefore, Kriya Chesta means you want to give, do attentively, do something. You give Kriya Chesta. You want to attentively hear something. Then you give Jnana Chesta. Now another idea of this also important. Chanting. Number six. Chanting is an outgoing doing activity and hearing is an intaking knowing activity. And very important paradigm we broke, which commonly many people have is hearing is not a doing activity. So hearing is not a doing activity. Hearing is a knowing activity. Yesterday also I mentioned that you cannot do multitasking. Multitasking means multi doing you cannot do. This is not doing and knowing are not two tasks. So therefore it is possible to know and do, but it is not possible to do two things. That is multitasking. Multi doing is not possible. #### Kriya Chesta, Jnana Chesta, Multitasking
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/PaJLXrn_7dk?t=678
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Multi doing is not possible, but simultaneous concurrent doing and knowing is very much possible because they are not two doings. There is one doing and one knowing."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the spanner and screwdriver analogy to explain the difference between Kriya Chesta and Jnana Chesta, and clarifies that while multi-doing is not possible, concurrent doing and knowing (chanting and hearing) is possible because they are not two separate doings.

If there is a screwdriver, suppose in this example spanner is Kriya Chesta, nut and bolt is doing, screwdriver is Jnana Chesta, screw is hearing. Suppose you want to use a spanner to unscrew, operate on a screw, will it work? It will not work. Similarly the other way around also. So that example gives us a very clear understanding of hearing is not a doing. Therefore both can be applied simultaneously. Multi doing is not possible, but simultaneous concurrent doing and knowing is very much possible because they are not two doings. There is one doing and one knowing.

#### Krishna, senses, spiritual vibration
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zZfOfZ9elt4?t=1329
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna cannot be seen with our material eyes, nor can Krishna be heard with our material ears."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna cannot be perceived with material senses but descends into the vibration of the chanted name, empowering it.

So, Krishna cannot be seen with our material eyes, nor can Krishna be heard with our material ears. This is a fundamental fact. That is what he said. Atashri Krishna Namadi Nabave Grahi Mindriye. With our material senses, we cannot chant Krishna's Shuddha Nama. It requires spiritual tongue, spiritual mind to chant because it's a spiritual vibration. However, that vibration of the Lord, transcendental vibration of the Lord, takes avatara, descends, means takes avatara into the vibration that I chant with my tongue. And what happens, like sun and sunshine, the Lord is the ocean of Satchitananda. So, all is potency, just like electricity through a copper wire, energize, electrifies the wire. In the same way, the sound which I make, Krishna has already decided. Kalikale Nama Rupe Krishna Avatara. So, you utter Krishna, Krishna's original spiritual name, in the sense, his sound, spiritual sound. That sound enters the sound and makes it, empowers it. That is the statement of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. Nam Nam Akari Bhauja Nijasarva Shakti. He invests all his powers into this vibration on your tongue. Therefore, this vibration on your tongue is called Shakti Avesha Nama. And the one who empowers this is called Aprakrita Nama or the transcendental name or the Shuddha Nama.

There is electricity. Through this glass, through these wires, the power can be expressed. There is a process. According to reciprocation of the devotee. What kind of reciprocation? How much energy? All those. So, therefore, I have used the word for shakti avasha nama as potentially omnipotent. This is another term that I have used to get familiar with it. Potentially omnipotent. That means according to the degree of sincerity, degree of bhakti, degree of purity. The Lord sends through, whether it is 50 watts or 100 watts or 1000 watts like that. Normal material wire is limited. This is what, you know, 420 wire can carry this much. **Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zZfOfZ9elt4?t=1918
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Fire is situated in one place while the heat and light produced by the fire act in many ways."
**Summary:** Using examples from Vishnupurana, the speaker explains the Achintya Bheda Veda philosophy, stating that the transcendental name is not different from Krishna.

The Lord acts, how the Lord acts is nicely explained in Vishnupurana. Fire is situated in one place while the heat and light produced by the fire act in many ways. Another example given is that the electric powerhouse is situated in one place but its energy is many different types of machinery move. Now the next Prabhupada is saying, the production is never identical with the original source of the energy. See, energy is not original. Energy is coming from energetic. So it is not exactly identical. But the original source of energy being the prime factor is simultaneously one with and different from the product. In other words, even though the energy is different from the energetic, energy has come from him only, no? Therefore, in that sense, he is the Lord himself. So in that sense, this is called Achintya Bheda Veda philosophy and Prabhupada said, this is the perfect way of understanding the therefore Lord Chaitanya's philosophy Achintya Bheda Aveda Tattva is a perfect way of understanding. So therefore the Shakti Avesha Nama and the Transcendental Nama or the Shuddha Nama or the Aprakrita Nama Aprakrita Nama is not different from Krishna himself. That is Krishna's name is not different from him. And he is the one who is empowering the sound vibration. That I make with my tongue. So that is Shakti Avesha Nama. So therefore, Krishna is non-different. Krishna's name Shuddha Nama is non-different from Krishna. Shuddha Nama is empowering this Nama because he is empowering in this Nama, this Nama on my tongue. Again, this Nama is non-different from Krishna, but in a different way. Whereas the Shuddha Nama and Krishna, they know like Achintya, Veda, Veda, Tattva. They are one. Krishna himself.

#### Srimad Bhagavatam, Anama Rupa, material nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zZfOfZ9elt4?t=2704
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "God does not have a prakrta name."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Sridhar Swami, explaining that God does not have a material name, but the material name can be empowered.

Now regarding Nama, see in Srimad Bhagavatam, Sridhar Swami, Sridhar Swami is the original commentator of Srimad Bhagavatam. Even before Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Vaishnava commentaries, in regard to significant, in regard to the significant word, Anama Rupa, Sridhar Swami says, prakrta nama rupa rahito api. The word Anama, which means having no name, indicates that Supreme Personality of Godhead has no material name. There is no name of the Lord which is produced by material. What is the name that is produced in the material nature is Shaktiyaveshanama. For the time being, it acts as good as the Lord. Electricity goes away? No, it is not. It is ordinary copper wire. Just like some thieves come and steal the deity, they cannot steal Krishna. For whatever reasons, why allowed and not allowed is a different subject matter. But the fact that he stole the deity, went away, and then he melted it for money. Is Lord subjected to all these things? No. Immediately, his external vigraha, for the time being, he is residing in that. But he withdraws himself. But when he is in it and when he is in it, it is completely non-different from himself. He makes no difference. You offer a garland to the external deity, it is for me. Because that for me, Krishna, you should understand for Krishna, there is no difference matter and spirit. As I told you, it's like another dress. So you offer to him. The greatest fact is that, the most merciful fact is that. The Lord has decided to come in that particular form.

#### dream, material life, Vedas, Vaishnava philosophy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SIm1lzp5KZk?t=802
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Before that let us analyze what happens when we lie down and when we dream."
**Summary:** The speaker analyzes what happens when we dream and clarifies that Vaishnava philosophy does not say that the material world is a dream that each of us are creating.

Before that, let us analyze what happens when we lie down and when we dream. We lie down, we close our eyes, we go out of the external consciousness. That means my senses, my body, my real eyes, real nose, real ears are all inactive. But my mind is active. Who is dreaming? Through my mind only I can dream. I am the dreamer, but I have got, you know, body, senses, mind, intelligence, all this I have, and they're all sleeping except spiritual mind. Spiritual mind is also sleeping. Others are inactive, sleeping and inactive. There, spiritual mind is sleeping but active in the sleep, in a dream. What is the dream? That is sleeping but active in the sleep, in a dream. What is the dream? That is that I am seeing and I am creating. That is that I am seeing and I am creating a world for myself. In the case of lying, a world for myself, in the case of lying on a bed and dreaming, you lie down, you create your own world. They create their own world. So one should not think that, then our Vedas say that this material life is like a dream, that each of us are creating our own this material world. And then when you get self-realized, when you see the truth, the whole world vanishes, it doesn't exist. This is not what we are saying. This is not what the Vaishnava philosophy says.

This idea of illusion. I'll explain in a very, it's a very common example given in our shastras. In the darkness, when you see a piece of rope. Suddenly, you may mistake the rope to be a snake. Then immediately fear comes. Then you light a torch. You see the light on the light. Oh, it's only a rope. The torch light of knowledge. Om Ajnana Timranthasya Jnananjana Shalakyasya. Om Ajnana Timranthasya Jnananjana Shalakyasya. Ajakshu Van Milirthamien umbrella, atasmai Shri Guravenamaha. The Guru is the one who shows this torch and says it's not a snake. It's a rope. So, in the material world... So what is this? Now compare. Rope is like the soul. Snake is like the body. What is a fact? It's a reality. It's an objective reality. This material world is objective reality. The seer in the body is also a fact because I am seer, I am spiritual energy.

**Summary:** The speaker explains that the purpose of Nama Prabhu is to liberate us from the illusion that we are the body, and that a self-realized person uses the body like a car to reach a destination.

So the purpose of the Nama Prabhu liberating means what? Liberating us means freeing us from this drama and understanding that I am part and parcel of Krishna. I am the seer in this body. I am the thinker, feeler, willer, everything in this body. The active agent is me, the soul. And then a self-realized person, he deals with this body just like I see rope as a snare as a snake. After the torch light, I say, oh, it's a rope. Rope is not useless. I'll use a rope as a rope. Similarly, when I get self-realized, I'll use this body as a body as an entra, just like I'm driving a car. What's the problem? Just like if I just, if I realize that I am not the car, I cannot, I need not stop driving the car. In fact, if you think you are the car and then you forget that the car is meant to take you to a destination, when you wake up, what happens? Ah, come on, not the car. Car is supposed to be used by me to take me to a destination. I have forgotten even destination. Here I am decorating the car and sitting as if I car. That's what everybody in the material world is doing continuously. They are thinking I am the body and all their desires is centered around the body. You know, that's how the life is set right from childhood. It's all around my body, my bodily security, my bodily, I need security, I need power, I need gratification for my senses, I need love and affection. Everything I want to feed my mind with loving sensations. I want to feed my my intelligence with with knowledge, you know, and I want to feed my social ego, my with position, position in the society. Because everything is spirit for him. He can move everything. But for us to see, there is this spirit of the Lord. So, then from this, how he wound up his pastime. A hunter aimed at a pink object thinking it is something else, a deer or something. He shot the arrow and that arrow pierced the toe of Krishna. Krishna was lying down. It was actually that pink object. It was Krishna's toes. And that is how Krishna entered his Leelas in this world. This is his Leela. Our Acharyas have said that for the sake of bewildering the atheist and for the sake of not revealing his transcendental nature to the atheist, a material body was left behind looking like Krishna there. As if like an ordinary man died. These are all. These are all. These are all. This is exactly the acts of Atma Maya. His personal potency, Atma Maya, makes all these arrangements so that his Leela is not disturbed. He does not. I do not reveal my true nature to everybody. Everybody need not know. Only those who love me will know about my true nature, that transcendental. Let ordinary people think I am like any other guy. Like you, you took a body and then... arrow shot and you died. So attention is the one that comes from the soul, it makes me know the thinking of the material mind, feeling of the material mind, willing of the material mind. You may anaesthetise, externally may not be, just like when you lie on the bed in the sleep, you are not aware, but internally whether you are experiencing or not, what you are experiencing, when you sleep, you go, you are still experiencing thinking, feeling and willing in the sleep. But we do not know what happens in the case of anaesthesia, even that is also still, probably brain is still nothing, there is no thinking, feeling and willing.

**Audience:** Newcomers, General public

**Summary:** Emphasizing that chanting the holy name is the only religious principle in Kali Yuga and that the holy name is as good as Krishna himself.

In this age of Kali, there is no religious principle other than chanting of the holy name, which is the essence of all Vedic hymns. This is the purport of all scriptures. The principles of Parampara system were strictly honoured in previous ages, Satya Yuga, Treta Yuga, Dhvapara Yuga. But in the present age, Kali Yuga, people neglect the importance of the system of Shrouta Parampara, or receiving knowledge by disciplic succession. In this age, people are prepared to argue that they can understand the principles of the Parampara system, that they can understand that which is beyond their limited knowledge and perception through so-called scientific observations and experiments. Not knowing the actual truth comes down to man from authorities. This argumentative attitude is against the Vedic principles and it is very difficult for one who adopts it to understand the holy name of Krishna is as good as Krishna himself. This is a key thing, holy name of Krishna is as good as

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, to feel the presence of the Lord, a Prakrita, a Prakrita Lord, Transcendental Lord, having descended on my tongue, to feel the presence, it is very much required to have Shraddha or faith."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the importance of faith (Shraddha) in experiencing the presence of the transcendental Lord on one's tongue during japa, emphasizing that spiritual sound is beyond material perception.

Welcome all of you for the 46th session of soulful japa. So yesterday we had discussed the content of module 13, and it was basically to deal with the factor of faith in japa, and the most important thing that we discussed yesterday was the role of faith in making us feel the presence of the transcendental name on our tongue, though we do not experience that spiritual sound with our material tongue nor with our material ears, as revealed by the scriptures. Krishna's transcendental name, form, etc., everything about the person Krishna, they're all beyond this material energy. Material energy is only an energy of the Lord, not the Lord, no doubt, because it is coming from the Lord. In one sense, the energy is not different from the Lord. The Lord can become the energy, energy of the Lord at a particular time in his person, just like, you know, I can say I am my hand, but my hand cannot say, tell me I am your body. It cannot say, but I can say and is me. The same way, the energy of the Lord is his part and parcel of. He has got unlimited energy, his para up Parashakti, Transcendental, they are all part of Parashakti, the Transcendental nature, that means beyond Prakriti. The soul belongs to a nature beyond Prakriti.

**Summary:** This segment discusses the conviction that the holy name is not just a vibration but contains the Lord's energy, and uses the analogy of a ghost to explain the concept of unmanifest presence, relating it to the Lord's presence on the tongue.

Today, those who have followed this sequence of video sessions by now would have had, would have developed a kind of a conviction that what is on my tongue is not just a vibration that I chant. No. What else is there? Namnam akari bahudha nijasarva shakti. Oh, all the Shakti of the Lord has gone there and therefore it has become Shakti Vaisanama. If the Shakti has to go, then Shakti is always with Shaktimaan. So, Shaktimaan is Shuddhanama, Aprakritanama. And we discussed that at this stage, we can feel the presence of the Aprakritanama only by Shraddha, by faith. It is called unmanifest presence. Lord is present near me in his unmanifest presence. Just like you see, I gave you example yesterday in the normal world, in the material world, we don't have examples of unmanifest presence. That means you cannot see him but he is around you. Then today morning it struck me. No one example we can give. See how, of course, it is not appropriate still with respect to seeing the senses, seeing and not seeing this example suits. In the material world. In the material example, in the material world, you can have unmanifest presence of a person in the form of his ghost. You know in this room that ghost is there. That means that person is present but you cannot see him. In haunted houses you go. You know things will move. Windows will shake. All kinds of things manifest into the gross plane also. But you cannot see. You cannot see the ghost with your gross eyes. Unless you get so scared. When you get so scared, your subtle eyes will see the subtle body of the ghost. Can see. As if it is real gross experience. But subtle body cannot be seen with gross eyes. When people say they see ghost, it is because of their subtle body is charged up. They are so subtle almost like going into dream state. And they see the subtle with their subtle. The subtle can be seen with their subtle body. The same way spiritual body of the Lord can be seen only by spiritual senses.

#### spiritual senses, purification, soul's nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5b4mLI7sgR4?t=473
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The same way spiritual body of the Lord can be seen only by spiritual senses."
**Summary:** This segment explains that the spiritual body of the Lord can only be perceived through awakened spiritual senses, and that the soul's true nature is to love and be loved, but this is misdirected in the material world.

So when I awaken my spiritual senses, the unmanifest presence of the Lord on my tongue can be experienced as manifest presence. Manifest means manifest to my senses. That means with the tongue. With the spiritual tongue I can chant Hare Krishna. This present tongue, sense of all the senses are covering the spiritual senses. We get purified then we can chant Shuddhanama even with this tongue, because this tongue will only fully spiritualized tongue. And behind it will be the awakened spiritual tongue. Just like Prabhupada says, why do you think a tailor when he stitches a shirt, why does he make a hand for the shirt? Because there is a hand on which this dress has to go and sit. Similarly if I have a tongue, my spirit has a tongue and a sense of tongue. If I have a body, the spirit has sense of having a body. Only thing it is mistaken. This is my body. That bodiness or whatever that sense of having a body, sense of having a tongue, sense of having a pair of eyes, sense of having Jnana Indriya, Pancha Indriya. That sense of having that belongs to the soul. Now that sense is supposed to be operating through my spiritual senses, but my spiritual senses are sleeping. Therefore now in a dreamy attention I am exercising my sense of taste, sense of smell, everything through this material body. That is why it is said that the soul is trapped in this material body, to think that this body is me. Once I think that this body is me, then I have to enjoy and suffer all the nature of this body. What is this nature of this body? Sukha, Dhukha. Pain, pleasure. All dualities of this material body. It has to die. It has to grow old. Etc, etc. And we do not like. Why we do not like old age? Because my real body does not get old. Why we do not like to die? Because my real body in the spiritual world is to have an eternal body, not a dying body, not a diseased body. Why I love people? Because my spirit wants to love. It is my nature to love and be loved, but it is all misdirected in the material world.

**Summary:** This segment introduces two types of faith: existence faith (believing Krishna exists on the tongue) and applied faith (feeling His unmanifest presence during chanting), emphasizing the need to apply faith during japa.

Before I come to that one new, very exciting quote from Hari Bhakti Vilas, what Krishna himself says about his holy name, before that, let me tie up little more things with respect to faith. There are two kinds of faith. One is existence faith. That means, okay, Krishna exists on my tongue. I cannot see. So, I need faith. It is not that all that I can see only exists around me. So, I have faith that he really exists. And what that faith correspond to a feeling in our heart about the truth of that existence. Yes, I cannot see, but he is there. That feeling is applied faith. You may have so many faith in your mind. But when an action comes, when I am chanting, actually, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna comes. Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. The sound is here. Then I have to apply the faith means I had to feel his unmanifest presence. By faith, I had to feel. You get the difference? Very important. Because I want you to apply that feeling during japa. So many faiths we have based on our knowledge in our minds. But when it comes to action, that is the time it has to be applied means you have to release that feeling. God is here before me. Old philosophy of Nama Avatara, you know, in that lot of faiths are involved. So, I call this as applied faith. You have to apply your faith during japa. So, there are two kinds of faiths. One is called existence faith. This I have added in my module today morning. I extended that module 13. You can go and read it also later. This subject of faith is very interesting. I am going to develop. I already developed. Later on, we will see. At least four or five modules, I have to speak on this Shraddha. Because this Shraddha, this faith is the first item where mindful japa gets transformed into soulful japa. First ingredient. Without Shraddha, nothing. Soulful japa does not start at all. So, therefore, it is very interesting, important to understand not only what is faith, but what is the mechanism, what is the skill by which we actually apply the faith at that

**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa reviews tips for mindful japa, including focusing on the entire mantra as a unit and understanding the mantra surupa, emphasizing the presence of Hare Krishna and Hare Rama strings within the mantra.

Welcome all of you for the 46th session of soulful japa. So we have dealt with module 13 yesterday, and also I gave some tips for those who are really having some more difficulty in mindful japa where their chanting is taking out a lot of their energy. Again, one more tip I gave yesterday which seemed to work for some people, that is, I said that you think that you have to chant and hear the whole mantra as a unit. So think that the current nama is the whole mantra on the beat, then it becomes easy for the jnana cheshta. It's a little easier job than high resolution each word. But I could have told this earlier itself, but I didn't want to tell this earlier because if you are focusing or if you train yourself for a higher goal, then the lower goal becomes very easy. I mean if you are able to run 500 meters race, then 100 meters will be very easy. So that is why we started with that. Since I heard that, you know, some devotees had difficulty, I thought of letting you know about this which I was going to let you know later on because who is the savior, who is the one who is receiving the service, what are you, whom are you repeating? Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Hare is what you are repeating. That is the mantra surupa. So 16 in 108, 16 into 108 seva is for the Hare Krishna maha mantra who has incarnated in this particular form, the mantra. So then by chanting that, by knowing that there is a Hare Krishna string, there is a Hare Rama string, this is the total mantra. So when you are chanting Hare Krishna, now you are to ensure that you know the entire mantra, that you have done the mantra as it is happening, as it is moving towards starting with Harry and completing with Harry Harry. So that also has helped some devotees.

Welcome all of you for the 48th session of Soulful Japaan. Bhagavata vidhi is a process of Japa, hearing Bhagavatam, hearing Bhagavata Gita, is a process of Shravanam and Kirtanam. Then, Shravanam, Kirtanam, Vishnu, Smaranam, Archanam. My original consciousness is not this world consciousness. My original consciousness is Krishna consciousness, which is natural to me. So the Bhagavata process is the process of chanting and hearing and especially through the process of like we are doing Japa. Parallelism. Normally, we do Bhagavata process, the Pancharatrika process. Pancharatrika process is for worshipping the deity in the temple and even at home, worshipping the deities. Offences to Nama is called Nama Prada. If you are inattentive to the deities presence and not respectfully serving them, then it would amount to disrespect and that's an offence. When you hear, when you attentively hear and chant that sound, though your tongue cannot touch me, transcendental, because I am transcendental, but your attention is transcendental. That touch develops into, you know, further quality touch in terms of faith and devotion and all those things. But connection itself is established. In other words, I will tell you very simple. Like Prabhupada says, simply chant and hear. How I am not thinking of anything. I do not have any knowledge and I am not thinking of Krishna conscious philosophy, nothing. And it is said that just by hearing, chanting and hearing, all perfection is achieved. It is true because through, if you think, just like if I take food through my mouth, I do not know what to eat. I do not have to do anything else. The food will go inside. It will convert into energy. It will convert into body and my body will grow. And I will be alert. Suppose for many days I am fasting, all my senses will go weak. I will be tired. All karma indriya, jnana indriya, all will be weak. Moment I give food to the stomach, food goes inside and it distributes energy all over the body. All my senses are enlivened. And the food I cannot put it through nose. I have to put it through my mouth. There are many holes. I have to put it through particular designated way. Then only it goes into me. Similarly, I have only one mouth now in the spiritual mouth in this material world. What is that? Attention. I can put anything into my soul only through my attention. And nothing material you can put through. It will not go through. But if there is something spiritual, if I put through the attention, it will go through. Where it will go through? Go through into my soul.

What is the next component? That Vajratima Avatara on millions of tongues with the same feature as, the sound vibrated by those tongues to give a very crude example. Suppose you have mango juice, you put it in a Bursary, water bottle, you will see that takes a shape of that. Has it a shape? Is it very broad? You pour that water into a balloon and it takes the shape of the water, the juice takes the shape of a balloon. You pour it in another, let us say, a bowl. The juice takes the shape of the bowl. But juice is the same or not? Yes. The same way, Shuddha Nama is same as Saprakta Nama. The Archa Rupa, the transcendental Archa Avatara is having the same features as the external Vigraha. But just like I said, the juice is same. It is the original form of the Lord himself. He should not differentiate. You are seeing Krishna means you are seeing Krishna. You are seeing Radharani. Juice is the same. Form is different. That is the difference. Somebody asked difference question between Avatari and Avatara. Both are transcendental and Shakti Avaisha. Avatari and Avatara, what is the difference? The difference is they are same. They are just another expansion. Avatari is another expansion in a different form. So, the Avatari Nama of the material world whose nature is Aprakrita or spiritual, whose features are resembling the sound vibrated by the trunk is called the Aprakrita Nama Avatara.

They are spiritual, transcendental to material nature. Therefore one cannot see with these material senses. #### Kriya Cheshta, Kariya Cheshta, Tasking
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7BZba3A4xM0?t=588
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Kriya means individual unit work. Work. Kariya means a task."
**Summary:** This segment differentiates between Kriya (individual action) and Kariya (task) and explains how both are offerings to the Lord during japa.

Now, you see Kriya Cheshta. That's where we are in Mindful Japa. Jnana Cheshta. Okay. And then Kariya Cheshta. What is Kariya Cheshta? Kariya Cheshta is tasking. Kriya means individual unit work. Work. Kariya means a task. Okay. Kariya. Kariya means many things put together. Kriya. Kriya means individual doing. Offering a full Aarati to the Lord is a Kariya. Offering Agarbatti alone is a Kriya. Offering flower alone is a Kriya. Offering handkerchief alone is a Kriya. So, all these Kriyas put together become the Kariya. So, Kariya Cheshta. Similarly, offering on each bead Hare Krishna Mahamantra, Shigyana Cheshta and Kriya Cheshta, on each of them is like they are like Kriyas. Whole Mala is a Kariya. Sixteen Mala is a Kariya. So, tasking is a Kariya. So, we are also not only in your mind you are not only offering to the Lord individual chanting, you are also telling that you are also making an offering that I will do sixteen rounds. That means you are offering a Kariya also. Kriya plus Kariya. Building up that Kriya one by one, one by one, one by one into sixteen into hundred and eight. This is my offering. So, individual unit Kriya is also Kriya Cheshta. Kriya Cheshta is also offering. Kariya Cheshta is also offering. That is why for Kariya Cheshta you need a goal, a fixed number of rounds. That is why Prabhupada gives us minimum sixteen rounds so that there is a minimum thing so that our Kariya, our Kariya, daily Kariya Cheshta is to complete sixteen rounds and all.

Sometimes 40, 50 instruments are there. But ultimately they all produce one music. Isn't it? That is bhakti. All these background items join together because they are all very natural for us. We don't have to do anything. Only we have to feel that. Bring them. We have to feel devotion towards Krishna, then all these things will automatically join together and become a very nice offering to the Lord. And Lord, this is called sadbhavana. Sadbhavana japa. Transcendental bhavana. Transcendental feeling. And Krishna is said to be bhava grahi janardana. One who actually grahi means one who consumes bhavas. A devotee's bhava he consumes.

All Hare Krishna always bad thoughts. Bad thoughts come while chanting, Prabhu. Please guide. For some time it will come, you ignore them and go on chanting. Why bad thoughts come, I will explain to you. Suppose, let us say a haunted house is there. And if you go there, you will see a haunted house. Forget haunted house. Let us say your own house, lot of thieves have occupied all the rooms. And then you are sitting in the veranda. You are unable to go inside. You are sitting in the veranda. They have all occupied. Somehow or the other, you bring the police. And the police, they will go inside. They will drive everybody out. Now while they are driving out, one by one has to go out of the door, out of the veranda. One by one has to go. So as they are going, who are they? They are the thieves, but you will become aware of them. The same way when you chant Hare Krishna Maha Mantra for first few days, for first few months or even a year, you will see all these dirty things are running away. So you should ignore them. They are being driven away by the Shakti Avashanama. All sinful tendencies, sinful acts, all the desires are going away. When they go away, now you don't tell, come, come, come, come, come, hold, let me hold it. Then what do you use? One side, you know, Harinam is going inside and cleansing and throwing out everything. But somehow it is crossing your eyes. Then you are telling. No, no, no, don't go away, please, please, I'll give you a space, please be inside. So Anarthanevrtti, Anarthas are all going away. So we should not. So instruction to you is, doesn't matter, don't worry, it is part of the process. Bad thoughts, probably more bad thoughts than before will start coming. It is an effect of the Holy Name. Now you should ignore that, disassociate with that, they are thieves, let it go. It has to pass by, it will pass by.

So be very happy. Be very fortunate. You told the 46th module to listen or focus on the full mantra as one name. I am able to focus on each word of the mantra, which one is better, hearing the full string as one or hearing each word? Please hear each word because each word joins up towards the full string. So anyway, when you move from one beat to another beat, you will become conscious of the full string. After my 16 rounds, many times I listened to Mayapur Shanai and some other instruments' tunes which convey a particular mood to me about Lord Rama's pastimes, like his marriage with Sita written in a tune. It keeps playing. So is that also spiritual sound or not? It all depends on the source you are hearing. If it conveys to you, makes you Krishna conscious, then it is a spiritualized sound. But mantra is mantra. It is just like a mantra. Mantra is like a mantra, but mantra is a mantra. After the 16 rounds, many times I listened to Mayapur Shanai and some other instruments' tunes which convey a particular mood to me about Lord Rama's mantra. A particular name of the Lord is also different from general singing, devotional song. Who sings is also important. You know, a professional singer is different. A devotee singing is different. The Shakti in that shabda, the spiritual Shakti in that shabda is different. Therefore we should always hear, just don't go by the meaning. Who is singing is also important because along with that singing, that bhavana is there. That bhavana, not material bhavana. There are good singers who sing with bhavana, but they will be materially attached. There will be all kinds of bad habits. They don't worship Krishna. They don't know Krishna is the supreme personality. You've got it. But when they sing, they generate that feeling and sing. That is not, you need atma bhavana, not mano bhavana. You need atma bhavana. Okay. Krishna is one who consumes atma bhavana. Mano bhavana is a shadow of atma bhavana. When atma bhavana is there, mano bhavana is 100%. But if mano bhavana is there, there is no guarantee there is atma bhavana. One can fake mano bhavana. You can fake feelings, but you cannot fake heart feelings.

So, so far we have discussed, we have understood Vaishnava philosophy, Bhagavatam philosophy is that there is this material world, which is this, you know, which undergoes through, undergoes the cycles of creation, maintenance and elation. So it undergoes these three cycles eternally. Therefore, in this material world. Okay. Everything is permanent. Everything is, even though the energy is real, energy of the Lord, but nothing is permanent. At the same time, Bhagavatam says that beyond this material universe, there is Vaikunthaloka, which is eternal, not subject to creation, maintenance and elation. They eternally exist. And in those Vaikuntha planets, there are unlimited knowledge. There are infinite knowledge. There are infinite nelathyayinathas, nilayas and nirayanas, the predominating deities, and then the topmost planet among them, Goloka Vrindavana, where Krishna is residing eternally. He has His abode there and that Krishna is the Brahma Samhita says, Eeshwara, Parama Krishna, saccid-ananda-vigraha, anadir-adirgovinda, sarvakarana-kaarna. That Krishna is a cause of all causes. From him all has emanated. As Krishna himself declares when he came down and spoke Bhagavad Gita that everything is coming from me everything is working because of me so all these things is what we understand from Bhagavatam and from the Vaishnavacharyas. Therefore there is a material world and there is a spiritual

**Summary:** The speaker explains the historical context of Buddha's appearance, focusing on the misuse of Vedic injunctions regarding animal sacrifice.

At the time of Buddha, Buddha himself incarnated, we know he is one of the incarnated for a particular purpose, he incarnated the purpose was at the time of Buddha the Vedas were quite prevalent in the society but what was happening was mass misuse of the vedic injunction that one can sacrifice an animal and then sacrifice an animal in a particular way and consume meat, there is a particular injunction for certain class of people. And that injunction is such that you have to do so many rituals and everything and finally end of the day, before killing the animal, you also need to tell into the ears of the animal that this life I am killing you some other life you can kill me. These are the regulations with all these regulations, the animal is sacrificed as per the procedures. And as per procedures, if you do that, that animal who sacrificed will get a human life is a promotion for that soul. So these are exemptions, rare exemptions somewhere here and there it is given, but that became order of the day. And indiscriminately animal killing was going on.

There is no Shakti which you cannot describe. Because the moment you describe, then that is part of this material world. So this is called Nirguna Brahman. taken over all over the world. So there was a purpose and that purpose is served after that Lord Vishnu is all Krishna is in control of this existence. So then I picked up storybook, I mean, there was normal Bhagavatam sect was there and Prabhupāda, Prabhupāda's other Krishna book were three Krishna books, big books, like a children's short storybook it is, the original print of Krishna book. So I took that big heartbound Krishna book, simply went and sat in the same place where Prabhupāda, where I used to sit and read every evening. They opened on Prabhupāda's one page and then I saw this, Īśvaraḥ paramaḥ kṛṣṇaḥ saccidānanda-vigraḥ anādhi rādhir govinda sarva-kāraṇa-kāraṇa. Then I read two pages, one page, two page. And then I saw this, Īśvaraḥ paramaḥ kṛṣṇaḥ saccidānanda-vigraḥ anādhi rādhir govinda sarva-kāraṇa-kāraṇa. Then I saw this, Īśvaraḥ paramaḥ kṛṣṇaḥ saccidānanda-vigraḥ anādhi rādhir govinda sarva-kāraṇa. I was shocked. I said, even in my mind, I didn't think of a person with unlimited capacity, a person who has unlimited senses, a person who has got unlimited energies, unlimited ownership, unlimited bhagavān, all those things, all those things came in those two pages, immediately

Scientists did not know, though all, so many philosophers did not know, now here somebody is speaking that God is a person and he is describing what kind of a person he is. So most of the people in the material world, they cannot accept person as a God because first of all understand that there is another category of person called Vishnu tattva, who is an infinite person. My thinking, feeling, willing is limited, but God's thinking, feeling is unlimited. There is a category of satya, truth of that type and that is God, God tattva. So the very easily impersonalist will say that, will, if you go on here. No, no, it's all, how can God be, see Krishna, we came, how can God be a finite boundary? How can God be a finite boundary? They say, but they don't know that in that finite, so-called finite boundary, Krishna showed the entire universe. That means they do not, they have no understanding of a form, which is infinite, which can fit everything inside. That's it. So this is one of the reason there is an intellectuals get blocked in accepting God because they do not know this fact about infinite personality with infinite senses, infinite intelligence, infinite and he can still be a person.

He established Krishna because when Krishna is directly saying certain things, aham, aham, sarvasya prabho mata sarvam pravartate, manmana bhavam adhbhakto madhyaji mam namaskaru, tameve sarvam, I am aham sarvasya prabho mata sarvam pravartate, sarvasya ca aham hrithi sannyavishta, mata smrtti dhyanam akho hamshya, ishrava sarva bhutaani hrideshi arjuna tishtati. Completely Bhagavad Gita is completely a personal exposition. Is like a love letter from God to the humanity. That is why it is written in the Bhagavad Gita. It is written in the Bhagavad Gita. Welcome all of you for the 52nd session of Soulful Japa. The lights are on. Okay, that is off. Okay, so... Yes. So we have discussed yesterday what was the Mayavada, the danger of Mayavada understanding, if somebody is interested in really absolute bhakti to Krishna. The Lord is absolute. I am absolute. My individuality remains. God's individuality remains. As Madhvacharya, as Ramanujacharya, as Nimbarka Swami, as so many, Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu have established that individuality is eternal. Individuality does not annihilate itself.

#### Cosmology, Krishna's Expansions, Eternal Reality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/uw8ZxhLdTiA?t=118

**Content Type:** philosophy

So, at the same time, the Lord's individuality is absolute, infinite. My individuality is tiny. Like the Jivas are like a drop of ocean water. The Lord is like the ocean. Lord is like an unlimited ocean. Lord's personality is like an unlimited ocean. My personality is like a drop of water. A drop of water has saltiness, same quality as the ocean. The quantity is different. Similarly, I can think, feel, will, desire, do, enjoy in a limited extent. God can do all this unlimitedly. But we are eternal. So, therefore, there is no point in bhakti if you are thinking that Krishna is also an historical personality who was born like anybody else and then maybe some special work. But finally he died. He died and emerged into that impersonal Brahman. So, where is Krishna now? Now you are chanting Hare Krishna to say that Aprakritanama comes on your tongue. All has no meaning. So, that is why we have to accept this caution. We have faith in Krishna. Even now, we know Krishna is in Goloka Vrindavan. He eternally lives. We know that through his expansions, this material world, Mahavishnu creates this material world, maintains, annulates. Unlimited Brahman does come out of his body. These are not stories or imagination. They come out of his body and in each Brahmanda, Garbhodakshaya Vishnu. He expands further as Garbhodakshaya Vishnu. From his navel, the lotus sprouts and Lord Brahma is the creator of this particular universe like that. Many, many Brahmandas are there. This Brahmanda is a very small, like a mustard seed, in a bag of mustard seeds.

What will be the feeling in our heart, the ananda in our heart. That is why I called soulful japa as anandamaya japa. Mindful japa as tapomaya japa, the practice. Let me again, you know, instead of being very technical about it, practical example I have given earlier also. Take any example. For instance, you are hearing this session. When you are hearing this session, you are hearing what I am speaking. Your foreground mind is hearing and processing what I am speaking. But your background mind is having a purpose that is not in your foreground mind, purpose that is driving your foreground mind. Why I am sitting for this Japa session? I want to learn about Japa. Then your background mind also has knowing. Who is Madhu Pandit? Who is speaking? Why I should hear from him? All these things you have got background, you have got conviction, you have got, you know, perception of who the speaker is, everything you are aware. So, anything we do in our life, there is always a context. And that context gives meaning to the foreground activity. So, why am I doing Japa? Even now soulful Japa, why we are all practicing? Because we have got the benefit in mind. Where is that benefit in mind? What is the purpose of this Japa? All those things are there in our mind. I want love of Krishna. If I chant Krishna's name, I get purified. I want love of Krishna. All these intentions are there behind. Isn't it? So, now the question is very simple. #### Feelings, Thoughts, Soulful Japa
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/uw8ZxhLdTiA?t=1173
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Here is a clue I am giving."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the concept that feelings are rooted in thoughts, emphasizing that genuine feelings are essential for pleasing Krishna.

Now, how to generate these feelings? Here is a clue I am giving. We all do that. We all do that. But still, you see, feelings are not plain feelings. Behind every feeling, there is a thought. So, in one sense, you can say feelings are deep feelings or thought feelings. Just like if I am angry with someone, there is an angry thought behind it. If I am lusty, there is a lusty thought behind it. If I am envious, there is a envy thought behind it. If I am proud, there is a proud thought behind it. So, every feeling has a thought. There is a thought feeling. If I have feeling of helplessness, there is a thought behind that. So, feelings are never alone. Of course, in a drama, you can do fake feelings. In a drama, you do not need to have the real thoughts. You can have a fake feeling. Okay, next scene, you have to cry. All right. Because anyway, they cut, cut, cut, cut and then afterwards they join together and make the movie. Next shot, you have to cry. They are used to it. Immediately, they will cry. They have fake feelings. They are not thought feelings. So, it is possible to give feelings without thought also with another purpose. So, there is a thought feeling and fake feeling. So, now let us say, we are talking about background having feelings of. Because ultimately, Krishna, what does he want? He wants feelings. Krishna is described as Bhava, Grahi, Janardana.

#### Devotion, Relationship, Nama Prabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/uw8ZxhLdTiA?t=1283
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is described as Bhava, Grahi, Janardana."
**Summary:** This segment uses the analogy of a son offering sweets to his father to explain that Krishna seeks our devotion and love, emphasizing that japa is a relationship with the Nama Prabhu.

Bhava, he takes our Bhava. Everything belongs to him. Actually, we cannot give him anything. Giving something is simply an expression. Just like the son asks his father, tomorrow is my birthday. I want to give you sweets. I want to give you sweets. I want to give you this box of sweets. And then he will tell the father, no, give me money. No, give me money. I want to buy the sweets for you. So, he uses the father's money. He goes, buys the sweets. And next day, birthday, he gives to his father. Father, what does he want? It is not the sweet. The father wants sweets. The father was very happy with this act of offering, his devotion, his love. So, exactly in the same way, everything belongs to Krishna. My body belongs to Krishna. How long I am owner, entire material energy is his energy. My body belongs to him. My tongue belongs to him. My brain belongs to him. So, my Atma Chesta, I am only giving my Atma Chesta. But with the Atma Chesta, without the body functioning, nothing can happen. So, that is why I said, mechanical japa is not much of interest to him. It is already his, what you are giving. At least behind that, if you are giving Atma Chesta, I am giving, if you say. Just like you take Ganges water and offer it back to Ganges. So, it is the devotion that Ganges accepts. What is the big deal? You are taking my water and giving to me. No, no, no. There is a devotion there. You are wanting to give me a feeling of offering. So, in the same way, you please, entire japa, Nama Prabhu is seeking our devotion, seeking our love, seeking our surrender. Seeking our dependence upon him. That means he is seeking a relationship. I told you, japa is a relationship with the Nama Prabhu. The relationship with the incarnation of the Lord.

#### Mindful Japa, Devotion, Nama Prabhu's Mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/uw8ZxhLdTiA?t=1428
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even though I am doing mindful japa, all the rules are there, do concurrently and all these things, that is to deal with the mind part of it."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that even mindful japa should be done with devotion, recognizing that success in japa is due to Nama Prabhu's mercy.

So, this is the key thing. So, always remember this. Even though I am doing mindful japa, all the rules are there, do concurrently and all these things, that is to deal with the mind part of it. But you should always know that even doing that mindful japa, there is a devotion behind it. Because I want to go into soulful japa, therefore I am doing mindful japa. I have to train my mind. But otherwise, training mind and all is dry. It is not really the pleasure of japa. Right. But it is a tapas. Not only dry, it is a tapas. But you are taking that tapas because behind it, you have got priti. Okay. The tapas of the mind and the body is required. Now. So, that is one aspect that we should always remember. What Krishna is seeking from our japa. Mindful japa is just the beginning. Mindful japa is indicative of our desire to connect and be in touch with our avatar. Mindful japa is just an expression that I welcome you my Lord. It is an expression that yes, I receive you my Lord. Hearing means I am receiving you. Chanting means I am welcoming you my Lord. And hearing is I am receiving you. As I said, I use the word sound embrace or hearing embrace or attention embrace. All mean the same thing that I am touching the Lord with my attention. So, that intention is behind. So, Krishna accepts that. Oh, I should make his mind full. I should make his mindful japa successful because his intention is he wants to associate with me. He wants to hear me. He wants to train his mind so that mind does not become a disturbance to hearing me when I go, when I descend on his tongue. So, I should make it successful. I will make him successful. Nama Prabhu will decide.

#### mindful japa, chanting and hearing, Srila Prabhupada's instructions
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/8bQrMgTyxww?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Mindful japa is the 1st step in mind discipline in japa it is not the final several times I said that this is just the beginning and I call this as tapo maya japa because there is little tapas in it to watch the mind and engage our doing effort and knowing effort Kriya chesta and Jnana chesta to fix the mind again on the Nama Prabhu"
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the concept of mindful japa as the first step in mind discipline, emphasizing the importance of concurrent chanting and hearing as instructed by Srila Prabhupada.

Jai Shila Prabhupada, Hare Krishna Nama Prabhu Ki Jai and welcome all of you to the 53rd session of soulful japa. We have almost completed 4 weeks of practice of mindful japa. Mindful japa is the 1st step in mind discipline in japa, it is not the final. Several times I said that this is just the beginning and I call this as tapo maya japa because there is little tapas in it to watch the mind and engage our doing effort and knowing effort, Kriya chesta and Jnana chesta to fix the mind again on the Nama Prabhu. So this was what we had, we have been practicing, and for this Srila Prabhupada's golden quote of chant and hear exactly the same name, and elaborately we discussed that concurrent chanting and hearing the same name was the golden commands of Srila Prabhupada for mindful japa. Chant and hear that very chanted name, while you chant you must hear, when you chant you must hear, when you chant you must hear, when you chant you must hear, as soon as you chant you should hear. These are the kind of commands that Prabhupada has given us and we all experienced tremendous change in our japa due to the concurrent chanting come here and it was planned that 30 days if we go on doing it then the old mind slice will mold, hold mind slice of know spaced out mindless chanting will become deactivated and your new mind slice will come in place whenever you chant you will chant and hear that very sound that is the purpose of the linking direction that from the background mind Prabhupada's command or the print out that we kept for practice of these quotes was actually helping our my foreground mind to link the Kriya Chesta and Jnana Chesta on the currently manifesting

So this was what we have been doing and result of it as I said and as you all experienced is that there is a Divya Atma Chesta Ruchi, there is a energy, doing energy and the knowing energy and because to do something, to know something from our heart, we give this Chesta, Kriya Chesta and Jnana Chesta and Jnana Chesta and Kriya Chesta coming from the heart, when we do a Divya or a transcendental activity of serving God himself, then there is a Divya or a divine taste of doing. And knowing, that divine taste of doing and knowing is a mercy of the Nama Prabhu, it's a divine, divine means it's not from you, it's divine, so that adds on to our this thing and that ensures that we want to hear one more, one more, one more, one more, one more, we want to hear. So even for mindful Japa to become successful, it's only by the mercy of the Nama Prabhu, you can become successful. Because by concurrent chanting and hearing, you are pleasing the Nama Prabhu, therefore it is possible. Otherwise any activity, you have to, for instance, in the mind, mind wants to go into, where our mind goes, wherever there is Rasa, is it not, both, that means that the Jnana Chesta, Kriya Chesta, Jnana Chesta, doing and knowing will always go wherever there is taste. Okay. And anywhere there, there is no taste, you need a discipline, self-control to keep it there, attention. By willpower they have to come back and hold it there by willpower. Now your willpower must be so much that it should resist the pulling of that Rasa. This is if you want to concentrate on something. So in the same way, our mind is continuously having, thinking Rasa, this Rasa, this Rasa, Kala, during Japa time, all these things are coming in our mind. And without willpower, we are able to hold the attention. Why? Because of this Divya Chesta Ruchi that is coming from the Nama Prabhu. And you have all experienced that after chanting so many years. #### tasking impulse, foreground mind, background mind

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Now there is something you have to do after chanting in each beat.
