But what is in between? Is there something I have to do?"
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the concept of the tasking impulse, which involves a feeling between each bead, shifting the focus from the foreground to the background mind.

Now how do we train this? Doing japa. Concurrent mindful japa you have been doing. I did not tell you much about going from one beat to another beat, I did not tell. All this so far mindful japa, our focus was on chanting and hearing, chanting and hearing. That means it was focusing only on one beat. What you should do on one beat? What are you expected to do on one beat? And then because you have trained yourself japa, you know what is japa. You went on. Without me telling, I went on. You went on. But somebody who doesn't know anything about japa, I would have taught. Now there is something you have to do after chanting in each beat: Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. You should go to the next beat and again chant Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Then you should go to the next beat. Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. But what is in between? Is there something I have to do? If the person asks, is there something else I have to do when I go from one beat to another beat? Yes, that is called the tasking impulse. The tasking impulse. Why I call it impulse? Because it is a feeling. Your foreground doesn't have to do it. Your foreground mind doesn't have to do. Foreground mind is only concurrently chanting and hearing. The supreme personality cares for your attention. Why? Because attention, giving attention is connected to devotion. Giving attention is connected to interest. You have interest in Krishna. Where you have interest, you will give. Where you have intention, you will give. Where your desire is, you will give attention. Is it not? Therefore attention, Krishna cares for the attention. Devotion is nothing but keeping the attention. Devotedly keeping the attention is called devotion. Devotion is not devotion. Unless you give attention, where is the question of giving love? There is no question of giving love. Attention, give attention means you have to give love. You have to give love means you have to give attention.

a big test. So falsely thinking. So enjoying mood. I shall extract. You know, I shall pleasure for my tongue from the chanting. No, no. This is a science of Krishna conscious. Then again some disturbance came. Again I stopped. No. Japa has to be a daily seva. Just like you feed your stomach every day. You have to feed your soul also every day. Be as a target. In that, keeping that as a target, being guided by that, he will take each step towards being. So he will take one step and he will know his step. Doing and knowing. One more step, that means he is aware of each step and he guides himself to the target. So he is very attentive. Because B is there, now I have to go towards B. Attentively he will go. Then he will ask him to come back. Come back. He will come back. Then about 5-10 times you do that, he loses attention. He will automatically walk up and down. He will automatically walk up and down. #### encouragement, pleasure, father-child relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rCsWeGCqQ6E?t=370
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Suppose the father of the child is there, in front of the child, standing near B."
**Summary:** Continuing the child-walking analogy, focusing on the father's encouragement and the child's pleasure in pleasing the father.

Suppose the father of the child is there, in front of the child, standing near B. He is telling him, okay, come over. Take one step. You talked about a small baby. Just learn to walk. The child also gets thrilled when it walks without falling. So let's say the child already has learned to walk up and down. But the father is here. And the child takes one step. The father is here. The father will give a smile. Very good. Come, one more. The child takes one more step. He gives another smile. And the child sees that it is pleasing the father. Then takes one more step. The father gives one more smile. Come, one more. Come, come, come. Yes, that's it. Come. Come. The child is now constantly trying to please the father to go from A to B successfully. Now he has already learned. But still, my father is appreciating me when I take one step. Oh, very good, very good, very good. One more step. Very good. I am happy. I am happy. Come. Ten steps. Come. So, imagine the mind of the child and the mind of the father. This is very important. The mind of the child and the mind of the father. Even though it has learned mechanically how to walk up and down. Because the father is standing and encouraging each step. The child takes pleasure in each step in giving pleasure to the father. Is it not? It can very easily happen. It is a real example. So, now the question of automatic does not come.

#### japa, pleasing Krishna, love
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rCsWeGCqQ6E?t=509
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "I said the desire in japa is to please the Nama Prabhu."
**Summary:** Relating the child-father analogy to japa, emphasizing the desire to please Nama Prabhu and the concept of love as giving pleasure.

Take our japa now. I said the desire in japa is to please the Nama Prabhu. And Nama Prabhu is present. So, Guru and Krishna has directed us to chant on the japa mala. Japa is a service. Seviya is the Nama Prabhu who is manifesting on the tongue. And each time I concurrently chant and hear, he is pleased. The Nama Prabhu is pleased. And if you can feel that he is pleased. Because the scriptures say so. He is pleased. It will encourage you to do one more. Give one more point of pleasure. Then again do one more point of pleasure. One more point of pleasure. Just like the father is in the scene. In the same way the child is knowing. Child who has got a chanter. Who has got Shraddha. That Krishna is coming on my tongue. And he is pleased with my chanting and hearing. He will feel that bead by bead. He is getting pleasure of giving pleasure. That is called Preeti. My own pleasure is called lust. Selfish pleasure. Preeti means love. Love means I get pleasure out of giving pleasure. Pleasure from giving pleasure. So therefore you feel a sense of accomplishment of giving pleasure. Which is also your pleasure. It is not that you give pleasure and you sit. If you love someone. When you give pleasure. That gives you pleasure. So it is pleasurable to desire to please the Nama. Not that you are trying to please the Nama. It is interaction means both way. Preeti means both way.

#### quality of japa, mindfulness, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rCsWeGCqQ6E?t=640
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Nama Prabhu. How is he pleased?"
**Summary:** Discussing how Nama Prabhu is pleased by different qualities of japa, from mindless to soulful, and the importance of devotion.

When I chant now, the Nama Prabhu, how is he pleased? Nama Prabhu is pleased to different degrees according to the quality of our Japa. We have learnt by now: Mindless Japa, Mindful Japa. He is more pleased. But he is pleased even with Mindless Japa. If there is no, only because there is no training. Mindless Japa also is pleased as long as you are following the Guru's order to finish 16 rounds. Like Prabhupada says somewhere, quality or no quality, first quantity. What does it mean? Obedience to Guru. I cannot do quality, but I will do my 16 rounds. So there is a big aspect of devotion. And there is a big aspect of feeling. That itself is a basic quality of even Mindless chant. So each beat, we have to please the Lord, beat after beat, build up, and we have to please the Lord, one mala up to 16 rounds. But his pleasure of Mindful Japa is more than Mindless. We should know. We are the chanters. If you want to please, you want to please him maximum. So Mindful Japa pleases him more than Mindless Japa. To Mindful Japa, when you add delight, relish, hope, and enthusiasm, and the tasking pleasure, it becomes Spiritful Japa. He is even more pleased. He is even more pleased. Then when you do Soulful Japa, he is even more pleased. So even though we are, when we are trying to please somebody, we want to please him most. So that is why we are doing all these things. We are all chanting 16 rounds for so many years. Let us say we want to please him more because we know that he is pleased by attentive chanting. We know he is pleased by offering Preethi.

#### completeness, incomplete knowledge, human life, illusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Zo1Noe_JECg?t=674
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "All forms of incompleteness, are experienced due to incomplete knowledge of the complete whole."
**Summary:** The speaker reads a quote from Isha Upanishad, explaining that incompleteness arises from incomplete knowledge of the complete whole, and that human life offers a chance to realize completeness.

This is from Isha Upanishad, purport by Prabhupada. I think purport or preface. All forms of incompleteness, are experienced due to incomplete knowledge of the complete whole. The human form of life is a complete life in the cycle of birth and death. If in this human life of full consciousness, the living entity does not realize his completeness in relation to the manifestation of consciousness of the living being, and it is obtained after evolving through 84 lakh species of complete whole, he loses a chance to realize his completeness and is again put into the evolutionary cycle by the law of material nature. Because we do not know that there is a complete arrangement in nature for our maintenance, we make our efforts to utilize the resources of nature to create a so-called complete life of sense enjoyment. Because the living entity cannot enjoy the life of senses without being doubted, with the complete whole. The misleading life of sense enjoyment is illusion. The hand of a body is a complete unit only as long as it is attached to the complete body. The hand is severed from the body. It may appear like a hand, but it actually has none of the potencies of the hand. Similarly, the living beings are part and parcel of the complete whole. And if they are severed from the complete whole, the illusory representation of completeness cannot fully satisfy him.

world. As a spiritualist, any spiritualist should understand that the deepest instinct of the soul, soul is me. Not the body. #### attention, serving attention, lording attention, mukti, selfish desire
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Zo1Noe_JECg?t=2358
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Initially, we simply said attention. Attention anybody can give. That way Kamsa also gave to Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker differentiates between favorable and lording attention, using Kamsa as an example, and clarifies that wanting completeness is a constitutional need, not a selfish desire.

Initially, we simply said attention. Attention anybody can give. That way Kamsa also gave to Krishna. Then later on we developed favorable attention, serving attention, not lording attention. Kamsa had continuous attention on Krishna, but he had a lording attention. He wanted to kill him. So he was, but because he was continuously attentive of Krishna, he also got mukti. Just see. That is the power of attention. Imagine we want to serve Krishna, we will get gifts much more than Kamsa. We will go back to Godhead. We get priti, we get love of Godhead. So, and then it looks like, you know, okay, I am chanting for my selfish desire. What is my selfish desire? Yeah, I want to feel completeness. For that I have to love God. So I am pleasing him. It looks like that. But please understand that these are our constitutional need, our need to be part of God, our need to feel the pleasure of loving God. It is our need. A constitutional need is not a selfish desire.

#### soulful japa, benefit to the world, relationship with Krishna, material vs spiritual
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Zo1Noe_JECg?t=3943
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You chant for your bhakti. You chant to save yourself. And you spread the holy name to save the world, to tell the world, to benefit the world."
**Summary:** The speaker addresses the question of how soulful japa benefits the world, explaining that it is a glory of the holy name, but one should chant for bhakti and not for material purposes.

As you mentioned, chanting should not be done for any purpose of this world, but once we discuss that soulful japa will benefit the whole world. How do we understand this? You don't chant for that. See the thing is, for instance, Shastra says, you know, Sarva Rogan Asha happens by Achyutha's name, chanting Achyutha's name. It is a glory of the holy name, but you don't have that kind of relationship with the Nama that I use him for that. Doesn't mean he doesn't. He doesn't cure diseases by chanting. If anybody who chants, he will cure disease. Shastra says means pakka. Keshava's name you take, your disease should get cured. But we don't have that kind of relationship. It is a glory of the holy name. Similarly, more and more we chant, automatically the world will get benefit. You chant for your bhakti. You chant to save yourself, and you spread the holy name to save the world, to tell the world, to benefit the world. Even otherwise, simply by sitting and chanting soulful japa, because you're bringing down the shakti of the Lord more by soulful japa, it spreads through your body, through your mind, through your activities. Apart from that, through the atmosphere. It benefits the whole world. So please understand. It is not about finally the holy name will do everything. Holy name we are depending on. Even if I get food every day, it is because of the holy name only. He is my maintainer, but I don't ask. I don't. I have a loving relationship with him. I cannot make him do material work, just like yesterday we discussed, because that is my relationship with Krishna. People who are not doing that kind of a japa, they can ask. Okay. I have got a harsh stroke. Yesterday somebody asked, a paralytic stroke. Can you pray? I said, we can pray. I don't want to ask my nama Prabu for such things. You ask that. I would say that give Krishna consciousness to this person who is in that body. Please bless him. That I will do, because he's incarnated for that purpose. By the way, he wants the material things also, he will bless. So as far as krishnanama is concerned, not that for him nothing material and spiritual, he blesses somebody with wealth, that is also spiritual only for him. There is nothing, but I should not ask. This is the point. Don't separate Krishna from material life. I don't want to connect him to like order carrier. How can I order the Lord for something? I cannot. I am supposed to serve him and he will take care of all these things. So this is a higher principle.

Hare Kṛṣṇa, Hare Kṛṣṇa, Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa, Hare Hare, Hare Rāma, Hare Rāma, Rāma, Rāma, Hare Hare. Hare Kṛṣṇa, Hare Kṛṣṇa, Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa, Hare Hare, Hare Rāma, Hare Rāma, Rāma, Rāma, Hare Hare. Hare Kṛṣṇa, Hare Kṛṣṇa, Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa, Hare Hare, Hare Rāma, Hare Rāma, Rāma, Rāma, Hare Hare. Just simply Chandrakrishna has tried to hear this sound. The sound of Chandrakrishna is very powerful.

Our highest Rasa is also our original Rasa. In the material world, we run after this Rasa, that Rasa, so many Rasas. Actually, they are all not connected to us. But Krishna, Krishna Rasa or Bhakti Rasa is our original Rasa. And Rasa means Sat Rasa, Chit Rasa, Ananda Rasa. Sat Rasa means Rasa of its experience. Sat means it is the experience of permanency, experience of freedom, experience of power, experience. Sat means experience of unrestricted power. At your beck and call. That is the Sat experience.

#### Sat Chit Ananda, Krishna consciousness, eternity, freedom, power
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1ZErQDx4KV0?t=1115
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna consciousness is a perfect Sat Chit Ananda experience. Because it's not based on this body which is temporary."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that Krishna consciousness is a perfect Sat Chit Ananda experience because it is based on eternal connections and relationships, providing freedom from material energy and the power to serve Krishna.

Now Krishna consciousness is a perfect Sat Chit Ananda experience. Why is it perfect Sat Chit Ananda experience? Because it's not based on this body which is temporary. We are talking Bhakti is all about eternal connection with Krishna, eternal relationship with Krishna, our eternal self related to the supreme eternal self. So, there is experience of eternity there. Everything, everything is grounded on eternity, not on any temporary transient thing in Krishna consciousness. Krishna Nama is eternal. Jeeva is eternal. Seva to the Nama is also eternal. And freedom. Sat Krishna conscious freedom means freedom from the material energy. Freedom from forgetfulness of Krishna. Because what do you want? Freedom is freedom from bondage. Bondage is caused by what? My nature is freedom, but I am not experiencing freedom because I am bonded in material world. We all want to be free, but we are bonded. In one way, you must be thanking, oh, now this COVID-19, so much freedom I got. I could sit at home and do so many things. Nobody likes to be bonded in work. When you love some work, it's a different matter. Nobody likes to be bonded. Power. When you are in Bhakti, when you are connected to Krishna, the unlimited source of power, you have all the power that you want for his service. His service is executed by his power.

#### Sat, Chit, knowledge, intelligence, karma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1ZErQDx4KV0?t=1224
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the material world, people cannot experience eternity because they do not know what is eternal. Because they think their body is permanent."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa contrasts the experience of Sat, Chit, and Ananda in the material world with that in Krishna consciousness, explaining that material experiences are temporary and limited by karma, while spiritual experiences are eternal and based on pure knowledge and intelligence.

So you are no shortage. In the material world, people cannot experience eternity because they do not know what is eternal, because they think their body is permanent. Their nature is to be eternal. So they never think that they will die. There is no freedom in this. There is no Sat freedom experience in this material world. There is no Sat power experience in this material world because if it is there, it is only as per karma. It's not free. You know, it's not freely. Whatever you desire, you don't get. But in the spiritual world, whatever I desire, I get for the spiritual, for the Lord's service. Chitta is the experience of knowledge and intelligence. In the material world, so much, you know, knowledge is gathered through the ascending process, through the senses. They are all imperfect knowledge. There is no purity. It's not pure knowledge because tomorrow they change again. But this descending process of knowledge, knowledge that is coming from Krishna through the parampara, Krishna through the shastras, that is something that is the experience that does not change. It is perfect knowledge. And intelligence. Intelligence is the application of that knowledge. Why do you need intelligence? You need intelligence to act. How to act towards achieving something. That is intelligence. And when you are connected in devotional service to the Lord, then your intelligence is connected to supreme intelligence. He gives us the intelligence to engage in his service.

#### Ananda, love, Sat Chit Ananda, completeness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1ZErQDx4KV0?t=1420
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only our sattva, chitta and ananda links up with Krishna's satchit ananda. We can get complete experience or rasa."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa discusses Ananda as the seeking of love, and explains that only by linking our Sat, Chit, and Ananda with Krishna's can we achieve complete experience or rasa.

Ananda. In the material world, we are always seeking love. We want to love and be loved, engaging loving activities including sense pleasures. No doubt in this material world all this sattva, chitta and ananda we are after running after. But it doesn't satisfy us. But it gives you a just like a gilded gold gives you an idea what is gold. A gilded gold is fake. Fake gold. But it gives you an idea what is gold. So, in the similar way we are not strangers to satchit ananda. But the satt is fake satt. Chitta is fake chitta. And pleasure ananda is fake pleasure in this material world. Why is it fake? Okay. Experience is an experience. Something you call fake because you expect it to be something but it is not that. So, some experience is there. That's why living in it is running around in this material world. But what do I deeply want? I want eternal sattva. I need pure chitta. I need pure love, perfect love. And that only God can give. And only satchit ananda. Only our sattva, chitta and ananda links up with Krishna's satchit ananda.

#### Sat Chit Ananda, japa, love, freedom
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1ZErQDx4KV0?t=1505
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that Krishna consciousness gives us our original sense of completeness, and that japa is for experiencing Sat Chit Ananda, including the love of Krishna and the freedom to serve Him.

We can get complete experience or rasa. So, that is Krishna consciousness is giving us our original complete sense of completeness which we discussed yesterday about the purpose of japa. When our satchit ananda links up with the Lord, then we get the experience of satchit ananda. So, entire japa is for experiencing satchit ananda. Ananda means experiencing love of Krishna, experiencing activities, serving the Lord, experiencing the glories, unlimited glories, the chitta, glories of the Supreme Lord, experiencing the freedom, the power to serve the Lord. We are not impersonalists. Vaishnava philosophy doesn't say that. Vaishnava philosophy says that this material world, all the variegatedness that are there in this material world is Krishna's energy. If I am using it properly, I can experience satchit ananda here. I can experience sattva rasa, real sattva rasa. By handling money in this material world, because nothing belongs, nothing is material. If it is used for Krishna, then it is a sattva experience. When I get freedom from the bodily concept of life, when I get freedom from my sense urges, I experience sattva. I experience freedom.

#### freedom, eternity, Sat Chit Ananda, material world
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1ZErQDx4KV0?t=1600
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna consciousness is perfect satchitananda experience. Please understand that. This whole japa, he is for getting our original experience."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa continues to explain that Krishna consciousness provides the experience of eternity and freedom, and that the root cause of material existence is forgetfulness of Krishna and the desire to seek Sat Chit Ananda independently.

What is freedom? I experience liberation from bondage in the material body, freedom from bodily concept of life. So freedom rasa, I experience. It is absolute, even in this material world. I don't die. I have no birth, I have no death. Soul does not have birth and death, eternity, experience of eternity. You cannot get experience of eternity unless you know who is supreme eternal. So Krishna consciousness is perfect satchitananda experience. Please understand that. This whole japa, he is for getting our original experience. Foremost of that being love of Krishna. Our heart is actually, our heart is craving for love of Krishna, but it is forgotten and it is misdirected in so many ways. Next. So what is the root cause of our material existence? What is the root cause of losing our satchitananda? Our root cause is forgetfulness of Krishna and wanting to seek satchitananda on my own. Is it the whole material world? Is not everybody is doing that? This world has nothing but this: power, power pleasure, freedom pleasure, power pleasure, knowledge pleasure, intelligence pleasure, love pleasure, sense pleasure. You tell me anything which is not covered under this. All happiness and pleasures are covered under this three items: Satrasa, chitrasa, anandrasa.

#### Sat Chit Ananda, material world, japa, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1ZErQDx4KV0?t=1718
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Something that we all are seeking very deep within is given in its perfect or in its completeness that is Krishna consciousness."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that everyone in the material world is trying to satisfy their Satrasa, Chitrasa, and Anandrasa, but Krishna consciousness offers these in their perfect and complete form, and that japa is connected to the path of recovering our Sat Chit Ananda consciousness.

So the material world everybody is, you know, for some, let's say now you are going and you are working hard to earn money and you are sitting and working. It is to earn money. You are trying to satisfy your satrasa. You are trying to gain some knowledge, read some book. You are trying to satisfy your chitrasa. You come back home and then you have feel relief with people loving people around you. You feel anandrasa. Wife cooks a nice tasty meal for you. Love combined with pleasure. Anandrasa for the senses. Something that we all are seeking very deep within is given in its perfect or in its completeness that is Krishna consciousness. So what happens next and forget why we are discussing this is because you see ultimately if you know the cause how we lost our satchitananda, satchitananda experience and how you will see that japa is connected to the reverse path of recovering our satchitananda consciousness or forgetfulness of Krishna and wanting to seek satchitananda on my own. The whole world is doing that but they are never satisfied.

#### material energy, forgetfulness, identity, body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1ZErQDx4KV0?t=1818
**Verse References:** BG 7.5
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When I say that I am not satchitananda I am this body material body. Next what happens once I identify with I am the body."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that seeking independent Sat Chit Ananda leads to association with material energy, causing forgetfulness of our true self and identification with the material body.

So when that, when that seeking is there for independent satchitananda we come in association with the material energy. Krishna says in the Bhagavad Gita association material energy is what causes us forgetfulness of our true self as satchitananda self as satchitananda servant of satchitananda lord. We identify with the material body as a soul and experience being the identity I am this body. Here the trap begins. First it was only an attitude of seeking independent satchitananda. You come in association with the material energy because your spirit your satchitananda matter is no satchit no sat no chit no ananda. It is achit asat achit nirananda vastu matter. I come in association with that. When I come in association with that naturally asat is opposite of sat achit is opposite of chit. Satchitananda is opposite of ananda. When I say that I am not satchitananda I am this body material body. Next what happens once I identify with I am the body. When you identify with I am the body. Next. We lose our satchitananda experience completely. Why should you lose? Body is achit and nirananda. If I think I am the body the body doesn't exist eternally.

#### eternity, material energy, three gunas, lording attitude
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1ZErQDx4KV0?t=1926
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Loving serving consciousness of the living entity transforms into lording experience. That means independently I want to experience these things."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that identifying with the body leads to a loss of the feeling of eternal experience, and that material energy traps consciousness in the three gunas, leading to a lording attitude.

Therefore I have to feel I am not, you know, I feel a lacuna in my feeling of eternal experience. With little intelligence I can realize that see there is not eternal experience. I took birth in this body I will leave someday. And what is the alternate we get? We get original attitude wanting independence satchitananda. Once material energy captures us our consciousness is trapped in the three gunas material three gunas. Then the characteristic of three guna is what? To independently seek satchitananda. That is called lording attitude. Lording means independently. When actually when you are connected to Krishna satchitananda you don't have to work for satchitananda. Everything is lovingly you know shared. Krishna sattva Krishna's chitta Krishna's ananda. Our satchitananda is linked together. That is serving attitude. It is a serving consciousness. Loving serving consciousness of the living entity transforms into lording experience. That means independently I want to experience these things.

#### Sat Chit Ananda, nature of the soul, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1ZErQDx4KV0?t=2027
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna consciousness is giving people something that they are all chasing after."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that in the material world, we chase after perfect Sat Chit Ananda but never achieve it, while Krishna consciousness provides what everyone is seeking.

But in the material world in reality what happens? Deep in our heart I cannot change what I am. My nature I cannot change. My nature is to look for sattva and chitta and ananda, of the quality that I want. But I don't get it. I can only chase for the perfect satchitananda but never get it in the material world. So it is a fact. Everybody is chasing for power. Everybody is chasing for freedom. Yes or no? This is very important to understand. Nature of the soul. This is what I am. I need freedom. I need power. I want freedom. I want my free will. And I don't want to die. I want permanency. Just like light has got, fire has got the quality of light and heat. This is dharma. Dharma means quality of a substance. Similarly dharma atma is what? Sattva. You have a property. You don't want anybody to grab your property. You want the property to be yours. You want loving people around you. You don't want them to really grow old and die. But they will grow old and die. You also will die. Your identity as a lover and the loved people's identity both vanish. The souls will be somewhere else and souls don't remember this at all. Only when they were relating there was an erasa. But that's not what we want. We want eternal relationship. We never get in this material world. So, we simply chase this thing in the material world. But we don't get it. So, actually Krishna consciousness is giving something that everybody is seeking. Isn't it amazing? Krishna consciousness is giving people something that they are all chasing after.

#### Nama japa, Sat Chit Ananda, freedom, power, knowledge, love
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1ZErQDx4KV0?t=2172
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Japa is to re-experience our pure freedom and spiritual power of our free will."
**Summary:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa explains that Nama japa allows us to reverse the journey back to Sat Chit Ananda, re-experiencing pure freedom, spiritual power, knowledge, and love.

And that is this Nama japa. By chanting this Hare Krishna Maha Mantra. Now the key point I am going to mention after introducing this Satchitananda. When we do our japa, okay. So, we can reverse the journey back to Satchitananda existential experience by associating with the ocean of Satchitananda, the Nama Prabhu. So, japa is to re-experience our pure freedom and spiritual power of our free will. Who does not want? I mean for millions of lives this is what I have been chasing after. But you know I get something else. And here there is pure freedom and spiritual power. Japa is to re-experience our pure freedom and spiritual power of our free will. So, japa is to re-experience our pure chitta consisting of knowledge of the self, free from I am this body, but an eternal being of Satchitananda and loving servant of Krishna and Krishna's unlimited glories, knowledge of Krishna's unlimited glories. Re-experiencing pure love experience of belonging to Krishna. See we all want to belong to somebody. See when you come home, you feel okay I belong. I belong to my children. My children belong to me. Wife belongs to me. A sense of belonging is something very fundamental to us.

## HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

### Soulful Japa: Session 62
**Speaker:** HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

#### Japa, Mindful Chanting, Hearing
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gzSx-IEeIV4?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "When you chant, you should also hear, while you chant, you hear, as soon as you chant, you hear."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of hearing the holy name while chanting, recommending a practice using Srila Prabhupada's audio tape to cultivate mindful japa.

Jai Srila Prabhupada, Hare Krishna Nama Prabhuti Jai, welcome all of you for the 62nd session of soulful japa. then you regain your lost Krishna consciousness. How did you lose your Satchitananda? Why? your Sakshidananda status. In chapter 13, I have spoken in London in lecture, August 7, 1973. #### Material Nature, Lording Attitude, Satchitananda
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gzSx-IEeIV4?t=2029
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To enjoy bliss of Krishna consciousness is your existential right."
**Summary:** The speaker describes how being covered by the three modes of material nature leads to forgetfulness of our divine relationship with Krishna, emphasizing that enjoying Krishna consciousness is our existential right and that a lording attitude is the cause of losing Satchitananda consciousness.

Covered by the experience of three. modes of material nature, the joyful Satchitananda Jeeva becomes morose in this world by being forgetful, forgetful of his divine, eternal loving relationship or pure eternal ananda with Krishna. To enjoy bliss of Krishna consciousness is your existential right. Being covered by the three modes of material nature, you falsely feel that we are, you are the material body and belong to the material world. And Krishna is a stranger to you as if you are coming to know of him for the first time in your life. This is the depth of your forgetfulness of your greatest friend. You are like fish out of the water in the material world. Your joyful spirit is entitled to be in the Satchitananda world with Satchitananda self-conscious forms, self-conscious senses. With the consciousness of uninterruptedly loving and serving Krishna, the perfect lovable supreme person who cares for a relationship with every bit of his existence, including you. Your lauding attitude is a cause of the loss of Satchitananda consciousness. The original spirit of Satchitananda is joyful attitude of loving service, but the material consciousness is attitude of lauding or controlling. When you, the particle of spirit, the soul, does not give up lauding or controlling attitude and surrender to Krishna who is your eternal shelter, you cannot recover your joyful Krishna consciousness. Matter can be lauded over by Jeeva. You have been experiencing lauding rasas only by identifying yourself as a material body. Sometimes as toy males and sometimes as toy females of different species made of material flesh. Only after being trapped in these artificial bodies, you have been playing the perverted lauding games as if you are the material, your material bodies. As long as you continue to live the big lie of life that you are this temporary material body, you can never experience the real life. The real spiritual dimension of Satchitananda of yourself. Living in the body with joyful Krishna consciousness is not the problem. But living in the body by thinking that you are that body is the problem. You have come into this material world to play the game of fake Satchitananda rasas. The material creation is set up for those Jeevas to freely do what they want. What they cannot do in the spiritual world of pure love of Krishna. If you have to go to the moon, you cannot go there with this earthly body alone. You need a special suit designed to be worn over your earthly body. In the same way to fulfill your independent enjoying attitude. By forgetting Krishna. You need to have a suit of a material body mind system over your Satchitananda spirit.

That is why you are in the material body in this world, while you taste the Rasa of lauding or controlling in this world through identification with the material body. The deeper truth of your situation is that you are lauded over by Maya's dictates. You have become a servant of Maya or illusion. In the material world you have surrendered your soul power or faculties like free will, identifying faculty, being, desiring, thinking, feeling, willing, acting, for the enjoyment of Krishna less fake Sattrasa, which is freedom and power; fake Chitrasa, knowledge and intelligence; fake knowledge and intelligence; fake Anandrasa, which is fake love, loving activities and pleasure, sense pleasure. So, and what is the result? It is resulting in the opposite experience of Satchitananda. The attempt to be attempt in this material body was to experience Satchitananda. The end of the day every living entity trapped in this body is having the opposite experience of Satchitananda, which is his intrinsic joyful nature. You don't see anybody you know having anybody who can say that I will not die in this body. If he has to say that then he has to become spiritual. I am not this body. Now, Game Krishna is a name of, sorry, Game Karma, just like so many games are there, video games are there, like that. Game Karma is a name of the game of fake Satchitananda Rasas. The Supreme Mother is the internal energy of the Lord, who is the Supreme Father of all Jeevas. She expands as external material energy and provides material bodies. She protects the spiritual atmosphere of the spiritual world from the lording attitude of any Jeeva. By this arrangement Krishna respects by creating a material world separately. Krishna respects your free will to an absolute degree, to allow you to enjoy Krishna less so called happiness and pleasure, and permit to freely act and enjoy or suffer as per the laws of Karma in this toy world, trying to enjoy lording Rasa as much as we want according to law of Karma. Here any Jeeva can become very powerful like Hiranyakashipu or Ravana by his tapasya as per the laws of Karma, material world.

#### Game Karma, Illusion, Bodily Identity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gzSx-IEeIV4?t=2442
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Each life from birth to death is one game of Game Karma."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a movie to describe our position in the material world, explaining that each life is a temporary game of fake Satchitananda Rasas, and that the illusion is so perfect that it's difficult to distinguish from reality except through objective knowledge.

We are thus playing the game called Game Karma. Each life from birth to death is one game of Game Karma of fake Satchitananda Rasa, just like you know. Each life from birth to death is one game of Game Karma. These imitation Rasas in the game called Game Karma are so perfectly orchestrated to feel like the spiritual Rasas of the spiritual world that it is difficult to say it is illusion, except by objective knowledge of things as they are, including reality of death many many times. You have experienced birth in different bodies and death of those bodies, along with the different enjoyer identities, who all vanish into thin air, lock, stock, barrel, making life itself meaningless at that point. What identity you thought of as your own being and lived your whole life for that being, along with the mapping of the world itself vanishes from your consciousness, but the world outside does not vanish. Your each life just remains a temporary memory in some other temporary minds and become temporary history, to be soon forgotten in the years or few generations. Such a world of identities of material bodies and its Rasas, mapped out of real material energy, exists only in the minds, whether it is present, past or future. Each of those material lives was a dream you weaved out of the real material energy, like you weave out a movie world by giving a meaning in our minds, the colourful light patches on the movie screen. What really exists is a movie director, projector, screen, light patches, and you the movie watcher, and your temporary experience of the movie. Everything is real except the content of the story of the movie, just like movie is short film, but in the real world, and theatre is real etc. This is exactly your position in the material world. Ultimately this material world is not eternal existence. The meaning content of the life that is experienced is all illusion, but the mechanism and the ingredients that create the illusion is real energy of the Lord. This world of matter is real, but we read the matter world based on the bodily concept of soul and life. Though you experience happiness and distress, pain and pleasures of the hero in the movies, and other characters you identify with, as your pain and pleasure, they are all merely identified rasa experiences of the toy like material body. It is just like watching a movie. We identify with the hero, along with all his pains and pleasures. You identified with concorded pains and pleasures of this bodily egos. They are not really your own pain and pleasure, but appearing to be so.

---
#### Karma, Neutrality of God, Transcendental Knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gzSx-IEeIV4?t=2672
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Law of karma are the rules of this game."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the law of karma is the rule of the game, dispensed through time, and that God is a neutral spectator, facilitating the game, and that this illusion can only be broken by transcendental knowledge.

Law of karma are the rules of this game, Karma. Law of karma. Through kala or time is a dispenser to the jivas different types of perverted rasas in the form of momentary power rasa, knowledge rasa, love rasa and sensual rasa, post at the bodily plane and at social plane of this world, in complete forgetfulness of Krishna. The entire thing is facilitated by Lord's all pervading expansion in the material world as Paramatma, standing himself completely as a neutral person observing and sanctioning the results of the activities of the jiva. The rules of this game karma is called the laws of karma. The game purely runs according to those laws administered by his agent kala through gunas and karmas of each person. So everything experienced in this world by you is a play of guna kala karma, responding to your lording actions arising from the lording attitude. It is to be remembered that though it is a life of fake rasas, you as the experiencer of these rasas is a real eternal being. Law of karma which determine the quality and quantity of imitation or fake sakchit ananda rasa experiences of the jiva is a manifestation of God's equality and justice. Law of karma is a manifestation of Paramatma's equality and justice, and your temporary experience of the movie. Everything is real except the content of the story of the movie, just like movie is shot in the real world, and theatre is real etc. This is exactly your position in the material world. Ultimately this material world is not eternal existence. The meaning content of the life that is experienced of material rasas. You want good dreams? You have to sow good seeds in the form of good deeds in this very same dream. That is called Punya. It is never the choice of God to give punishing rasas of pain for some bodies or rewarding rasas of pleasure for certain other bodies, but remember that we are not this body but the soul. If you successfully disidentified from the body then either pain or pleasure experience is reduced. Only spiritualist can do that. Krishna's creation of illusion is so perfect that the Jeeva cannot break the illusion till he becomes conscious that the body is toy like fake body, like any other machine, and concludes that he cannot be just machine. He discovers who he really is beyond body from the revelations of Guru, Sadhu and Shastra. This illusion can be busted only by flow of awakening, transcendental knowledge in our consciousness.

---
#### Paramatma, Disconnectedness, Desires
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gzSx-IEeIV4?t=2881
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Krishna is a neutral spectator."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the role of Paramatma as a neutral facilitator of the game karma, residing in every material body, and explains that all Jeevas are disconnected from the source of Satchitananda, leading to the creation of false material identities and desires.

Lord Krishna is a neutral spectator, facilitator of game karma for fake Satchitananda Rasa. In the material world the Satchitananda Lord is residing in every material body, you as super soul or Paramatma, along with you in this invisible expansion to facilitate your miserably failed unconstitutional journey of perverted Rasa to lord over toy like fake forms, names, qualities and activities displayed by the material energy of the Lord. He as a friend respects our free will to exploit matter and permits or sanctions our lording to be successful or to fail as we desire and deserve in accordance with the laws of karma. Without Paramatma we cannot experience even the fake Satchitananda Rasas, though he is neutral to all. Without his ordained or sanctioned system of gunakala karma, not a blade of grass can move, just like no vegetation in the forest can exist without the common factor rain. Every material experience of Jeevas is because of the mere presence of the all pervading Lord in this body, just as the rain is a common cause for all seeds. It is not the specific cause for one seed to become a mango tree or another to become a banyan tree or a peepul tree or any other tree. Paramatma is not the cause of happiness and sufferings of Jeeva. The characteristics of the seed is what causes it to be the kind of tree, but by taking advantage of the rain. The rain is not responsible. Similarly in this world of karma, you become what you are by your own choice of material actions, followed by the reactions, but only by the sanction of the Lord in the heart. You are disconnected from the source of Satchitananda Rasa. In the material world all Jeevas are disconnected from the source of all Satchitananda Krishna. All sensual, emotional, intellectual and egoistic desires of the living entity, or all expressions of Jeeva, to fulfill the deepest urge for feeling completeness of Satchitananda nature, to fill this spiritual incompleteness due to disconnect from the ocean of Satchitananda Krishna, you went on creating false material identities, hopes and desires for freedom, power, knowledge, love and pleasure through different forms of gross and material arrangement, subtle material arrangement. All desires under the sun fall under these categories. However it is never fulfilled. A little careful observation will reveal that you.

---
#### Samsara, Mistaken Identity, Eternal Reality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gzSx-IEeIV4?t=3070
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You are swimming in an ocean of Asat and Achit called Samsara."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that we are swimming in an ocean of illusion (Samsara), experiencing transient pleasures and identifying with bodily egos, leading to a mistaken identity and forgetfulness of our true self, while the real world is the Satchitananda world of eternal bodies.

This illusion can be busted only by the flow of awakening transcendental knowledge in our consciousness. Lord Krishna is a neutral spectator, facilitator of the game of karma, for fake Satchitananda Rasa. In the material world, the Satchitananda Lord is residing around madly, trying to seek completeness through different kinds of gross and subtle bodies, sense and social gratification in this material world, called as material, so-called material happiness and pleasure. You are deeply experiencing what a fish would experience when kept out of the water. This unnatural state of existence is caused because you, being Satchitananda nature, are swimming in an ocean of Asat and Achit called Samsara. Your madness subsides when you begin your journey to reconnect with your own forgotten supreme Satchitananda person, Krishna. You are experiencing Rasas which are not your own Rasas. Life offers only transient lording pleasure of so-called freedom and power, knowledge and intelligence, love and loving pleasures, the transient bodily ego which your real ego identifies with, as if it is your Rasa. The unholy alliance of your real ego with the bodily ego of this world is a root of forgetfulness of the true self. Living entities in this material world do not have their own Rasa or life. They are living a life of mistaken identity, a life of details of desires which are characteristic of specific kinds of bodily egos which impersonate the soul. The real world is the Satchitananda world of Satchitananda bodies. Your real spiritual eternal body is of the quality of Satchitananda and not of matter, and the real spiritual world is a world of only Satchitananda objects, each of them having unique identities and consciousness. The different identities you experience in the material world are just role-play identities as your temporary body in the karma game. Material energy, under the supervision of the Supersoul in you and everyone, creates varieties of material bodies for different living entities, for varieties of independent Krishna-less fake pleasures. So the material world is full of eternal beings being haunted by this 'I am this material body,' playing the game of karma. The real eternal Satchitananda identity of yours is to be uncovered, disabling you to fulfill the need for being back in the Satchitananda consciousness. All the while your eternal friend, the Paramatma, has been accompanying you in your heart and is waiting to assist you to wake up to the eternal reality of eternal rasas of pure devotional service to Krishna.

---
#### Devotees, Incarnations, Guru
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gzSx-IEeIV4?t=3273
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Your eternal identity is that you are lovers of Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that pure devotees and incarnations are beacons of light in the material world, and that Krishna guides us to a Satguru to awaken our original Satchitananda consciousness, revealing our eternal identity as lovers of Krishna.

Your eternal identity is that you are lovers of Krishna and are eternally loved ones of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Krishna. Great comforting truth. Lords, pure devotees, and incarnations are beacons of light in the midst of this great grand trap of this material life. There is a great comforting truth: pure devotees of Krishna who are fully realized are awakened to the absolute situation of everything. They never participate in the game of karma of this material world. They are free from the belief that there is ananda in anything if it is disconnected from Krishna, the Supreme Satchitananda person. They are called mukta purushas walking in the midst of illusioned jivas. That is how I was brought to you to guide you to Krishna because you are looking for Satchitananda rasa, or perfect existence, perfect knowledge, and perfect love. An even greater comforting truth is Krishna, this Satchitananda Lord, incarnates into this world in different forms like Namavatara, Archavatara, Leelavatara, etc. The basic function of all these incarnations is to mercifully give Satchitananda association to the jivas who are all looking to satisfy the deep instinct of Satchitananda after being fed up of this game of karma for Krishna-less fake rasa which never satisfies the soul. Their association awakens the jiva's taste of their original loving serving consciousness. Their association frees you from the lording atmosphere of the three modes of material nature. You have been fortunate enough to be gifted by the shelter of the Nama Prabhu. Krishna brings you to a Satguru. It is revealed by the scriptures that Krishna as Paramatma, who is observing each jiva's journey in this material world for millions of lives, intervenes in the life of one who is in search of Satchitananda rasa. The search for fulfillment of Satchitananda rasa is nothing but the search for Krishna. He lovingly has guided you to me, and you have surrendered to my guidance and instructions to come out of this illusion and awaken your original Satchitananda consciousness. From then on, Paramatma became your first Guru, known as Chaitya Guru. The combined effort of Paramatma and the eternal Subguru, like me, you are undertaking this journey towards Satchitananda Krishna consciousness. Slowly you are beginning to replace your material consciousness. At every aspect of life, there is a loving reciprocation of the Lord. Internally within you is the intelligence to reach him, and even externally he is arranging things to guide and facilitate you to get liberated from the illusion.

---
#### Spiritual Energies, Association, Nama Avatara
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gzSx-IEeIV4?t=3472
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore the real need of yours in the material world is nothing but awakening of your original eternal consciousness."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the real need is to awaken our original consciousness through the transmission of spiritual energies, which happens by association with the Lord, especially through His Nama Avatara (holy name).

And realize your Satchitananda nature of eternity, knowledge, and bliss as the eternal loving servant of Krishna. Therefore, the real need of yours in the material world is nothing but the awakening of your original eternal consciousness, and also to wake up others whom you love too. Each living entity can potentially become free or liberated from the material energy by awakening his own Satchitananda nature of consciousness by association with the Supreme person and his Satchitananda energies. Your awakening will be characterized by humility and Krishna conscious happiness, and uninterrupted remembrance of Krishna in constant appreciation of him along with his unconditional love and compassion. Under my guidance as your eternal spiritual master, the Lord as Paramatma in the heart, and I as your spiritual master, are guiding you in your journey to rejoin Krishna in the spiritual world. The main process of awakening is by the transmission of spiritual Satchitananda spiritual energies from the Lord to your soul, which can dissolve the contaminated consciousness that covers your soul. The transmission of spiritual energy to awaken your soul happens by the principle of association with the Lord, who is an ocean of Satchitananda eternal existence, knowledge, and bliss, just like a piece of iron is kept immersed in the fire. Gradually it gets heated and eventually it becomes red hot and acts like the fire. In the same manner, when the soul who associates with the Supreme Personality of Godhead under the guidance of Guru, Sadhu, and Shastra, can awaken his original Krishna consciousness. Krishna's different incarnations are different merciful manifestations in this material world, and in this age Krishna has decided that he will very liberally give out his association through his Nama Avatara to one and all without any pre-qualification of any sort, simply for worshipping him in the form of chanting and hearing his names.

---
#### Japa, Spiritual Energy, Bhakti Bhavanas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gzSx-IEeIV4?t=3607
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Japa is a mystical process."
**Summary:** The speaker describes japa as a mystical process that invokes spiritual energy, dissolving false identities and bestowing spiritual pleasure, and emphasizes the importance of building up Bhakti Bhavanas (devotional feelings) in the background mind.

You are already blessed to get the shelter of the Nama Prabhu. Japa is a mystical process. It invokes the unlimited fire of Satchitananda before himself. As devotees, as neophytes, you do not presently have spiritual eyes to see and experience Sat and Chit, but by the mercy of the Nama Prabhu, as a sadhaka, you will slowly start experiencing this brilliant spiritual energy through the association with the Hare Krishna Nama Prabhu. It starts with the flow of the sense of eternity, Sat, and pure spiritual knowledge or Chit Prasadam that dissolves our false identities and egos that you are this body. You will start seeing this body as a machine on which you are riding in this world of fake rasas. You will be bestowed with the experience of Satrasa or spiritual pleasure of your eternal existence beyond everything that you now think you are, including the social identities and egos of this world. Unless you become charged by the touch of the supreme entity energy of God, supreme eternal energies of God, and receive that energy, you cannot realize or experience the substance of your true self as pure eternal existence. This knowledge will remain only in the domain of information or knowledge or Jnana until it is converted to Vijnana or realized knowledge with the actual flow of pure Chit or Vijnana energy from the Lord to charge your tiny Sat. Serving the Hare Krishna Nama Prabhu through the soulful japa will gradually transfer real Chit energy to your heart, and Nama Prabhu will enlighten you to realize what you really are. All this is the effect of just the beginning drops of the flow of mercy of Hare Krishna Nama that you have started receiving and will continue to receive through rendering service, step by step, starting with mindful japa. By serious training of the foreground mind, you advance your japa to spiritful and soulful japa by reading my books to build up the absolute thoughts of Krishna consciousness and contemplate on them and turn them into Krishna conscious feelings towards Nama Prabhu. This will make your background mind rich with Bhakti Bhavanas, enabling your background mind to offer these Bhavanas to the most merciful Nama Prabhu during soulful japa every day.

---
#### Satchitananda, Mindful Japa, Love of Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gzSx-IEeIV4?t=3775
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The sense of Satchitananda completeness is possible only in association of the Hare Krishna Nama Prabhu."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes that Satchitananda completeness is possible only in association with the Hare Krishna Nama Prabhu through mindful, spiritful, and soulful japa, leading to eternal attachment to Krishna and freedom from material miseries.

The sense of Satchitananda completeness is possible only in association with the Hare Krishna Nama Prabhu. When you connect yourself with Krishna, who is a reservoir of Satchitananda, through mindful and spiritful japa, you start to experience spiritual completeness and begin to quench all your spiritual instincts for Satchitananda. And when that matures into soulful japa, then perfection of life is achieved, and I will take you to the spiritual world and make you dance with Krishna. I have explained this in my book, The Matchless Gift. Those who thus see Krishna are not anxious, but they know where they are going at death. One who has taken the gift of Krishna consciousness knows that he will not have to return to this material world to take another body, but that he will go to Krishna. It is not possible to go to Krishna unless one attains a body like Krishna's, Satchitananda Vigraha body, a body full of eternity, knowledge, and bliss. One cannot enter into the fire and not perish unless he himself becomes fire. And similarly, one cannot enter the spiritual realm in a body that is not spiritual. In a spiritual body, one can dance with Krishna and Rasa dance like the gopis and cowherd boys. This is not an ordinary dance, but the dance of eternity in association with the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Only those who have become purified in their love for Krishna can participate in it. One therefore should not take this process of Krishna consciousness as something cheap, but as a matchless gift bestowed upon suffering humanity by the Lord himself. Simply by engaging in this process, all the anxieties and fears of one's life, which in actuality revolve about the fear of death or allied. Unquote. Thus, the benefit to your soul in associating with Hare Krishna Nama Prabhu through mindful to spiritual to soulful japa is a progressive experience of spiritual existential completeness of your sacchidananda consciousness of love of Krishna, resulting in eternal attachment to Krishna and his service, and freedom from material miseries. Be blessed. Thus ends the story of life.

#### attention, connection, japa

**Key Quote:** "So wherever chesta goes, attention also will go."
**Summary:** The lecture explains how thinking during japa disconnects us from the holy name by diverting chesta and attention.

If you do thinking, then your chesta for doing and knowing the sound will go for doing and knowing the thoughts. So wherever chesta goes, attention also will go. So now you see the danger. Once it goes away like that, then we are disconnected. Chesta goes away, attention goes away. Attention goes away, we are disconnected from the holy name, incarnation, the sound incarnation of the Lord. We are disconnected from Shakti Avesha Rama on our tongue because our connection, attention connection is through attention. Our connection is through attention. So we lose our attention connection if we indulge in thinking. And what is the result of that? When Krishna is saying no, then how is it bona fide, how is it good for us to, how is it good for us to think about preaching aspects and preaching problem solving, preaching hankering, preaching lamentation, preaching plan making during japa? Definitely not. You lose your hearing, lose your hearing. Creative ideation, same thing, new, new ideas, accepting ideas, rejecting ideas, no, this is not good, that is not good, this is good, that is good. Oh, this is very good, very good. Then again you analyze, no, no, no, it is not so good, that was better, this was better. Creative ideation, that is also a kind of a thinking. Then you are worrying, worrying is about, worrying about some consequences in the future. Lamentation is about negative thinking about the past, worrying is about negative thinking about the future. What will happen? Whatever may happen in two hours, what is going to happen in my two hours of my japa? I will give my full time to the Lord. Afterward this Lord will be with me to handle with this worry. I will not worry during japa. When Lord is with me, at least that time I should not worry, no.

#### daydreaming, attention, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AbzlUj9gBg4?t=2351
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A dirty mind will not leave us. When you want to go to Krishna and be with him for two hours, this mind will not leave us."
**Summary:** The speaker describes daydreaming as another form of thinking that distracts from japa, comparing it to flipping through channels on television and emphasizing the mind's tendency to pull attention away from Krishna.

So don't worry about, these are all natural. Our mind is engaged in all these things. All these kind of things. Then next kind of thing I have written is daydreaming. What is daydreaming? Daydreaming is exactly like you have a television, you have your remote, go on flipping the channels. That will keep you very busy, go on flipping the channels, aimlessly trying to find a aim. What should I do? What should I do? What should I do? What should I do? What should I do? So in the same way day dreaming, simply dreaming of so many desirable things. How will that be? How will this be? How can that be? Like that, daydreaming. So nice it will be if my son gets a job here, it will be so nice. After that, you know, you get married, it will be so nice. Dad, dad, dad. Hundreds of things you can think of. All these things. Yes. Yes. Yes. Yes. Yes. Yes. think of. We always do this daydreaming. A dirty mind will not leave us. When you want to go to Krishna and be with him for two hours, this mind will not leave us. They will bring problems to you. They will bring plan making to you. They will bring hankering. They will bring lamentation. They will do creative ideas. They will do daydreaming. They will bring new worries. Just do. No, no. Give me attention. Give me attention. Come away from Krishna.

#### rasayim, Krishna, hearing
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AbzlUj9gBg4?t=2448
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This supreme object of all rasayim is before you and you are going rasayim something else."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the term 'rasayim' to describe dreaming about enjoyment of future situations or recalling past pleasures, urging listeners to focus on the direct rasayim of Krishna in the nama instead.

Come away from Krishna. Then there is rasayim. This is a word I have coined. Rasayim. That means you are dreaming about enjoyment of future situation and rasa. Or even past also. You are recalling. Oh, how nice. How nice it was. Pilgrimage event. For so nice. Like that. This is rasayim. God himself, this supreme object of all rasayim is before you and you are going rasayim something else. Why don't you do direct rasayim of Krishna? Krishna rasayim is before you in the nama. Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama, Hare Hare. You are leaving that and then you are going for other rasas. Or you are thinking this will help me. That will help me. This will protect me. When Krishna is before you, this is the highest protection. He is highest protector. Nobody can protect you like Krishna. Nobody can give you rasa like Krishna. So, like this, different kinds of thinking activity is considered like a thinking noise. Thinking is a noise. Suppose I and you want to talk in this room. So many people are talking. Big noise. You get irritated. No, allow us to talk. No, please maintain silence. You tell. All thinking is like a noise for japa. Understand this concept. During japa, thinking is a noise. What is the result of noise? Whom you want to hear, you cannot hear. Whom do you want to hear? Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna

**Summary:** The speaker explains that thinking is a noise that prevents hearing the holy name clearly, emphasizing the technology behind the diamond coat to fix the mind on the nama.

Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. You want to hear him. That is why Prabhupada said, when, what is the diamond coat? See the technology behind diamond coat. Diamond coat is a sound. Diamond coat is a sound. Diamond coat is a sound. Diamond coat says that when the mind goes away, don't worry about the gone mind. Immediately again fix your mind on the nama. Your goal is not to bring back the mind. Give fresh cheshta for the next mantra that is coming. Forget about gone mantra, gone mind, gone. I did not attend. I did not eat that. It fell outside. Just like a baby, no? Two year old baby, if you ask to eat, it will eat and they will find all rice all over everywhere. It won't know how to put everything in the mouth. #### mind, intelligence, noise
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AbzlUj9gBg4?t=2647
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Mind slice means mind is comparing this feeling is good or that feeling is good."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the different kinds of chatting that occur between the mind and intelligence, emphasizing that this continuous chat is a noise that disturbs the hearing of the holy name.

So, this, this, okay. So, different kinds of chatting by the mind, intelligence slice to intelligence slice, mind slice to mind slice. Mind slice means mind is comparing this feeling is good or that feeling is good. Intelligence means this solution is intelligible or that solution. Between the two intelligence slice discussion. Finally, they come to a conclusion and mind also come to a conclusion and the mind and intelligence talks together. And finally, you know, ultimately whatever the intelligence is able to convince the mind only. Mind is the final doer. Intelligence has to convince. Just like if you have a desire for sense gratification. It comes. Then the mind naturally feels good. Now the intelligence says, no, no, no, no, no, no. Wait, wait, wait. This is feel good now, but later on, it is not feel good. You have to pay through your nose for it. Okay. Then the intelligence tell them, tell the mind. That look, remember the feel bad that follows this feel good. Now the mind, what does it do? Between these two it will make a choice, ah no no no no shreyas and preyas. Yes, I will not indulge in this sense gratification because it is going to be problematic for my spiritual life long term. I will not do it, so I better to maintain my feel good long term than my short term feel good. But mind ultimately decides everything by feeling. Intelligence decides everything by intelligence. Okay, so this continuous chat is going on, stop all that child.

**Summary:** The speaker explains that chanting free from noise allows one to hear the holy name clearly, leading to a feeling of the presence of the Lord.

That you have learnt now, tasking also. So with the power of tasking and with the power of the first round of tapasya, round, you have succeeded. So that is the threshold austerity. Another thing I will tell you, what happens, when you chant free from the noise, you will really hear the holy name clearly, remember Krishna's instruction, Prabhupada's instruction, hear clearly. When you hear clearly, let's say I say pen, as soon as I say pen, sound, in my mind the concept of pen is also there. Not only sound I am hearing. The concept of pen is there. That is due to training, learning. But in the case of nama, the concept of the pen is a pen, like that. The concept of Krishna, the sound is concept of Krishna, sound is Krishna, sound is identity of Krishna, immediately you feel the presence of the Lord, presence of the Lord is made felt as his identity. Just like you see, just to give you an example, if me and you are in the room and a third person is sitting in one corner, through the window he is looking through and you are aware that he is watching you. You become aware of his identity, he is watching you, he is watching you, he is watching you, though you are talking, is it not? That means you are remembering that, exactly in the same way, feeling the presence of nama will happen. Not only from the shabda, shabda is equal to nama's identity, the vikthi. Then that becomes nama smaran, that is an advance, when you are able to keep your mind free from all thinking exclusively for your Lord, mala after mala after mala, when you are able to do, this nama shabda will reveal its identity as a feeling of the presence.

**Summary:** The speaker answers a question about the bodily concept of the soul, explaining that it leads to a false identity and a life centered around the bodily ego.

What is the meaning of bodily concept of soul? See, when I think that the body is the soul, that's what we are all thinking, body is a soul, that means body is a spirit, or I am thinking this body is my spiritual body. When I think of my body, I don't think of the bone, flesh, bone, all those things. I don't see it as a machine. So bodily concept of soul is same as bodily concept of life started the bodily concept of soul, that means this body is the soul and everything connected to this body is my life. So that is called bodily concept of life. From bodily concept of soul comes bodily concept of life. First I mistake who I am and then I create a life based on that mistaken identity. Therefore I said that everybody in the material world is not living his own life, soul life, is not living the life of his own identity, is living the life of a false identity, some body who is not him eternally because my bodily ego is not my permanent ego. So I am serving my bodily ego and my social relationships are all centred around bodily ego. So therefore it is all a bubble, soap bubble, it burst at the

#### hearing, chanting, Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AbzlUj9gBg4?t=3781
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If hearing Lord's name is purifying my heart, is it not better to hear Mahamantra directly in Prabhupada's voice instead of my own?"
**Summary:** The speaker answers a question about whether it's better to hear the Mahamantra in Prabhupada's voice, explaining the science behind chanting and the difficulty of sustaining attention when only hearing.

time of death if Lord's name if unless you are sent away here from me you don't have a life mind your soul is dead there is a serious question I come to the keyboards played the felicagary of the body I'm here and there is ending time right there is that man you're If hearing Lord's name is purifying my heart, is it not better to hear Mahamantra directly in Prabhupada's voice instead of my own? It's a very good question. That is why all this knowledge that I have given you, what all you offer to the Lord at the time of japa? Doing and knowing. Hearing is only knowing. Doing plus knowing, it is difficult for you to lock attention. You are knowing, you try, you hear Prabhupada's chanting, how many days you will hear? Much more easily the mind will go away. Maybe just like that farmer, when it is new, 2-3 days you may feel, yes. But you try, few days, it will go away. Therefore, to lock the attention you need, because if you want to only hear, then your Kriya Chesta is hard. It is free. It will start thinking. So, therefore, the advice, therefore the instruction is, you hear Bhagavatam class from Prabhupada. That time you don't have to chant. You hear means read his books, let it be one way, no problem. But for two hours you practice doing and knowing the transcendental sound, which is the Mahamantra. And that is why Prabhupada has instructed us to do so like that. Otherwise Prabhupada would have told. There is a science behind it. There is a science behind not telling you to chant his name, only hear his name. None of you have to do japa, just hear your name. It is not practically possible. But hearing is very powerful. Hearing Prabhupada's chanting is very powerful. But you cannot sustain it. Because a pure devotee's chanting is very powerful. And if you cannot sustain it, you make more offences.
