#### Prabhupada, chanting, tasking
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AbzlUj9gBg4?t=3901
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You have to take tasking pleasure mala after mala on the Sakshi Mala also. Train your mind like that."
**Summary:** The speaker addresses a question about forgetting to update the Satyamala counter, emphasizing the importance of tasking pleasure on the Sakshi Mala.

Just like people simply put Prabhupada's tape all over the house so that ghosts don't come. Somebody told me that. Why you are playing Prabhupada's tape? Night also some bad soprana and all will get in this house. Ghosts are there. You are not hearing. You are sleeping nicely and Prabhupada is chanting. You are not doing anything. Prabhupada is your sevak, is it? To kill the ghost? He is a ghost buster is it? No offence to the holy name. Offence to Prabhupada. Does it help if we have Prabhupada's chanting running in the background? Yes it helps. But you should think that you are chanting in association with Prabhupada chanting, not that in the background you hear his chanting. If you want to follow his speed, you can follow his speed, but you do your chanting. Best is to have your own speed. Okay. Otherwise, your active attention will go away in doing and knowing. Many times I forget to update my Satyamala counter and when I complete my second round, I remember the same. What should I do in this case? Increase the counter by two or no? First of all, don't miss like that. I told you. You have to take tasking pleasure not only in Japamala, bead after bead. You have to take tasking pleasure mala after mala on the Sakshi Mala also. Train your mind like that. When you finish your round, try to look like this. One has gone up like this. Two has gone up. Three has gone up. And then you feel good when this much has gone up. Up means finished. Early morning you bring it up. Up means finished. Early morning you bring it up. Up means finished. You bring them all down. Then as soon as you finish one round, bring one up. Bring one up. Feel good. As it goes up, feel good. Feel good. What is this feel good? Transcendental feel good of having accumulated 108 beads into my internal account. I am able to please the Lord 108 times. I do not know what will happen even if, you know, next moment in my life. I do not know what to happen. It doesn't matter. I am happy one more.

#### Govardhan Parikram, attention, connection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AbzlUj9gBg4?t=4206
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "When you are chanting, you are going in Govardhan, you are walking, you think the holy name is with you, you are walking with the holy name."
**Summary:** The speaker addresses a question about chanting during Govardhan Parikram, emphasizing the feeling of connection with the Nama and that outside disturbances are not disturbances.

Do you recommend devotees to chant 64 rounds while doing Govardhan Parikram as they are not walking on an even surface and many shops, traffic, etc.? Is it good practice or bad for attention connection? No. When you are chanting, you are going in Govardhan, you are walking, you think the holy name is with you, you are walking with the holy name. So what? Suppose let us say you are walking with me in Govardhan, right? And we are talking. While talking and while we are going, does the shops at all disturb? Thousands of people are doing Parikram. Does it disturb? No. You are still giving attention to me. So in the same way, this is not technical. It is a feeling of connection with Nama. So you can be attentive. If you have tasking pleasure, you will be you want if you give delight that the Lord is on your tongue, and he is walking with you. All these outside disturbances are not disturbances. They are not disturbances because we are you are hearing please understand, so many things may fall on my eyes when I am hearing I don't see even what others are doing. Has it not happened many times? Somebody has you are observing somebody, somebody has crossed in front of you. You say, hey, I never saw you. You went where? I didn't see you. Just like when you are reading a book and then you are eating. Then at the end of it, somebody asks you, what is the sabji you ate? No, I don't know. In the same way, you are walking in Gordhan, Parikram, you are chanting, continuously you are chanting. The Lord is walking with you. Nama Prabhu is walking with you. Why you have to lose your attention just because some shops are there? Let the shops sit there on the ground, no? Why are you taking them in your head? In your head, you take Hare Krishna Mahamantra.

#### body, soul, dance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AbzlUj9gBg4?t=4313
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "You have to move your soul."
**Summary:** The speaker responds to an 82-year-old devotee who doesn't move their body while chanting, advising them to move their soul and dance in their heart.

I am 82 years old, passed two years chanting 16 rounds at a stretch, but I see that you all move your body while chanting. But I am not getting that taste. Please, don't have to move. Move the body. You have to move your soul. So don't worry about it. Okay? Probably, you know, it's too much energy consuming for an 82 year old person to move the body. Suppose you had begun chanting at 50 years old, or 45 years old, that time you would have also moved your body. Because when the soul is feeling bliss, the soul moves. Because the body also moves. Okay? Can 82 year old dance like a young man? Cannot. So some difference is there. The heart can dance. 82 year old can also dance. The heart of 82 year old can also dance. Please dance in your heart. Next. Why God made it so much difficult to understand him?

#### God, understanding, Bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AbzlUj9gBg4?t=4372
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "God is very easy to understand. We have made it difficult."
**Summary:** The speaker answers a question about why God is difficult to understand, explaining that we have made it difficult and emphasizing the importance of Bhakti and understanding the heart of God.

Probably, have you seen Krishna? Why God has made it so much difficult to understand him? Because we have become very difficult. God is very easy to understand. We have made it difficult. How is it difficult for us to understand? Like a small baby, do you have to make him understand who is father? Who is mother? It is natural to him who is father and who is mother. That simple is to understand God. But we have made it difficult. So we have to find out how I made it difficult. Okay? Now, so therefore we have to be very, you have to understand that all this is common sense. Krishna consciousness knowledge is also common sense. So common sense is not difficult to understand. The love in this world is never satisfied. Everybody is incomplete person, limited person. God is unlimited person. Difficult to understand? No. Prabhupada says, man is a sample God. You understand, you can think, feel and will. God can think, feel and will. You have love. You can love and give love and take love. God can take, but unlimited. Capacity is unlimited. So what is the difficulty in understanding God? It's simple. Okay? You don't have to understand, you know, from Vishnu, Tattva, from Krishna, then Balaram came, then from Balaram, Chaturmyuha came, Shankar, Shana, Pratyam, Nadyu, Aniruddha, and from them, then from one of the expansions, Narayana, then Mahavishnu came from Mahavishnu, then expanded into Garbhodakshavishnu, then Garbhodakshavishnu expanded into Shirodakshavishnu, Shirodakshavishnu, and Shirodakshavishnu is extended into everybody's heart. You don't have to know all these things. You have to, you need Bhakti. If you know it's good, you see to live in Bangalore, I don't have to know the whole geography of Karnataka. But if you want to know it's good, it's a Rasa to know. Similarly, it's a Bhakti Rasa to know about the glories of the Lord and his unlimited expansions. But that is not, you have to understand the heart, the heart of God. That is enough for Bhakti.

Okay. So, have you seen Krishna? Yes, I have seen Krishna the way Prabhupada wants us to see Krishna. But the same Krishna, same Krishna changes his look and comes as Radha Krishna Chandra, Radha Vrindavan Chandra, Radha Vrindavan Chandra, Radha Vrindavan Chandra. Same. So are you not seeing or not? You are seeing number one. Number two, Prabhupada says, you can hear Krishna. Why you want to see Krishna? Why you are emphasizing seeing Krishna? If, why you want to see Krishna? Oh, he's so beautiful. Therefore I want to see Krishna. Shastra has described me so beautifully I want to see Krishna. Prabhupada says, why don't we hear Krishna? When you hear Krishna, oh, so beautiful. So tasty his Nama is. And then the shastras say, what a beautiful Nama is. The shastras say when you get that taste, you will see Krishna's form also because there is no difference between Nama and Rupa and Guna and Leela. Thank you very much. #### japa, tasking, mindfulness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/dEThXx99G2c?t=538
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Chanting you must hear."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces practical lessons on japa, focusing on concurrent chanting and hearing, along with tasking to accumulate association with the Lord and prevent the mind from wandering.

So, today we will go to some very practical lesson on japa. We have all practiced concurrent chanting. Come here, which means chanting and hearing the very same chant. Chanting and exactly hearing the same sound. When we chant, we should hear. While we chant, we should hear. As soon as we chant, we should hear. We should chant and hear. We should hear every word. These are all the golden commands of Srila Prabhupada. And we trained our mind. During the training time, the mind kept going towards thinking. And Srila Prabhupada has given us the diamond commands to deal with this situation. He said simply, don't worry. Don't try to withdraw the mind. You simply again give your chesta, chant loudly and hear. Every time the mind goes away, don't bother about the mind. You simply, once you understand that, simply chant and hear loudly. Chant loudly. In this way, over a period of now five, six weeks, we have nearly six weeks, we are practicing concurrent chanting, come hearing. Then to support this, we added tasking. And that tasking is between the beads, chanting, come hearing is on the beads. So chanting, come hearing, then tasking, again chanting, come hearing, then tasking. In this way, tasking means to build up. The accumulate, accumulate, accumulate the association of the Lord without allowing the mind to drain away by going somewhere else. So that tasking with tasking pleasure, chanting come hearing with hrstam and aghlada or chanting with delight, hearing with relish and enthusiastically tasking with hope for the kripa of the Nama Prabhu in the form of flow of Satchitananda, mercy, bead after bead uninterruptedly if you can complete the rounds that is what we are trying to train ourselves and more or less we are trained.

Let us say it goes X temperature, X plus 1 degree. Next what happens? X plus 2 degrees, it builds up, X plus 3 degrees, X plus 4 degrees, then red hot. The same way it builds up. It builds up.
